Orderly Home Page

Everything you need to build on Orderly
=======================================

Platform architecture, API references, and SDK guides for builders at every stage — from first integration to production operations.

Understand the system

Learn what Orderly is, how the system is structured, and what the integration path looks like.

What is Orderly?
----------------

Understand the product model, shared liquidity layer, and who Orderly is built for.

Building on Omnichain
---------------------

See how Orderly’s system layers connect and where your integration plugs in.

Integration Checklist
---------------------

Follow the step-by-step path from tech stack selection through development to go-live.

Live Builders
-------------

Browse the builders already live on Orderly and the platforms they’ve shipped.

Implement core flows

Jump directly into the integration work most teams need first: auth, accounts, funding, real-time data, and SDK selection.

API Authentication
------------------

Generate signatures correctly, manage Orderly keys, and send authenticated requests.

Accounts
--------

Register user accounts, map wallets to builders, and understand account lifecycle steps.

Deposit and Withdrawal
----------------------

Implement deposits and withdrawals across supported chains with the right contract calls and API requests.

Websocket API
-------------

Stream real-time market data, order updates, and account events over persistent connections.

SDK Overview
------------

Choose between React components, hooks, core utilities, or lower-level programmatic access.

Expand your market offering

Add builder-controlled market expansion once your core trading integration is already in place.

Permissionless Listing
----------------------

Learn how qualified builders can bring additional perpetual markets live under an isolated-risk model, and what users need to understand before trading them.

Explore vault products

Understand how strategy-based yield products work in the Orderly ecosystem.

Strategy Vault
--------------

Learn how OmniVault works and where strategy-based yield products fit into the broader Orderly ecosystem.

Operate and troubleshoot

Use these pages when something is broken, behavior looks off, or you need production support context fast.

Troubleshooting
---------------

Start with the common diagnostic path for account, transaction, and trading issues.

Error Codes
-----------

Look up API and platform error codes to quickly identify what went wrong.

Partner Support
---------------

Access developer support, marketing resources, grants, and partnership programs for your integration.

Example DEX
-----------

Inspect a concrete integration example when you need implementation patterns, not just docs.

Getting started

What is Orderly?

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

On this page

*   [Who Orderly Is For](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#who-orderly-is-for)
    
*   [Why Teams Build On Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#why-teams-build-on-orderly)
    
*   [What Orderly Provides](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#what-orderly-provides)
    
*   [How To Build On Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#how-to-build-on-orderly)
    
*   [Where To Go Next](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#where-to-go-next)
    

![Orderly Preview](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/getting-started/preview-image.jpeg?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=0fa968a6dba3a60034ab38ebb68cb1cd) Orderly is a headless omnichain trading infrastructure. Instead of building matching engines or settlement stacks, developers use Orderly’s APIs and SDKs to launch branded trading experiences on top of shared liquidity. The core model: You own the frontend and user relationship; Orderly provides the trading backend.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#who-orderly-is-for)

Who Orderly Is For
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **DEX builders and trading apps:** Launch a branded perps experience on top of shared liquidity instead of operating a standalone venue.
*   **Wallets, custodians, and partner platforms:** Add trading functionality to an existing product without building the backend trading stack yourself.
*   **Trading teams and API integrators:** Connect programmatically to trading, account, and market-data interfaces while using the same infrastructure as partner frontends.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#why-teams-build-on-orderly)

Why Teams Build On Orderly
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Shared liquidity from day one:** Builders connect to a common orderbook instead of trying to bootstrap isolated liquidity market by market.
*   **CEX-like performance with on-chain settlement:** Orderly combines low-latency trading infrastructure with documented deposit, withdrawal, and settlement rails.
*   **Flexible integration paths:** Teams can build with APIs, SDKs, UI components, or Orderly One depending on how much control they need.
*   **Builder-owned product experience:** Builders control the user-facing product, fee strategy, and go-to-market approach.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#what-orderly-provides)

What Orderly Provides
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly’s primary product is omnichain trading infrastructure for perpetual futures. In practical terms, that includes:

*   shared liquidity and orderbook access
*   account, order, and market data APIs
*   smart contract rails for deposit, withdrawal, and settlement
*   builder-facing controls for fees, referrals, and operational workflows

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#how-to-build-on-orderly)

How To Build On Orderly
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Different teams need different levels of control. Builders can choose between:

*   **Direct API access:** Build your own backend and frontend against Orderly’s REST and WebSocket interfaces.
*   **SDKs and connectors:** Use the [React SDK](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview)
    , [Hooks SDK](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/overview)
    , or [Python connector](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-connector-python)
     to accelerate implementation.
*   **Orderly One:** Launch with a lower-code path when you want a faster route to market. See [Builder Guidelines](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines)
     for the code of conduct and branding standards.

Orderly also has adjacent product areas, such as [Strategy Vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
, but those sit alongside the core builder integration path rather than replacing it.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly#where-to-go-next)

Where To Go Next
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use this page to understand the product model. Then pick the next page based on what you need:

*   **Need the system view?** Go to [Building on Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
     for the architecture and integration surface area.
*   **Ready to set up your builder identity?** Go to [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
    .
*   **Ready to start implementation?** Follow the [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
    .
*   **Need support during setup?** Use [Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)
     and [Integration FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs)
    .

[Product Roadmap](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap)

Overview & FAQ

Overview

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Security](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#security)
    
*   [OmniVault Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#omnivault-smart-contract-addresses)
    

Orderly OmniVault is Orderly’s flagship strategy vault. It is an omnichain and permissionless strategy vault carefully designed to leverage the participation of protocol stakeholders to amplify liquidity and enhance on-chain trading experience across tradable markets within the Orderly ecosystem, creating new yield-earning opportunities for our stakeholders. As part of the initiative, Orderly is collaborating with industry-leading market makers (our trusted partners) as **Strategy Providers (SP)**, starting with Kronos Research.[1](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#ref1)
  We leverage their extensive expertise to execute efficient market-making strategies through the utilization & management of the OmniVault assets. Users can conveniently deposit USDC into the Orderly OmniVault from any of the supported blockchain networks and become protocol liquidity providers, granting them a proportional share of OmniVault’s PnL. This enables them to earn attractive passive yields generated by professional market-marking strategies.[2](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#ref2)
  Orderly OmniVault opens up profit opportunities previously inaccessible to retail users.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#security)

Security
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly has completed two independent audits on our strategy vault smart contracts to ensure the highest security level of asset security for users. Audit reports are publicly available on our [GitHub](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/Audits)
. Users should carefully assess their risk tolerance and the vault’s risk profile before depositing. It’s important to note that every strategy vault carries its own risks.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview#omnivault-smart-contract-addresses)

OmniVault Smart Contract Addresses
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

0x70fe7d65ac7c1a1732f64d2e6fc0e33622d0c991

1.  [Arbitrum](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x70fe7d65ac7c1a1732f64d2e6fc0e33622d0c991)

| Ledger (verifying contract for EIP-712 withdraw msg) | 0x6F7a338F2aA472838dEFD3283eB360d4Dff5D203 | 0x1826B75e2ef249173FC735149AE4B8e9ea10abff |
| LedgerProxyAdmin | 0xA2eA0a58b083c492AdC91A687FAc8B53AdB7c0Fd | 0x24ea35cCD9d8c7aA5D35BDdc140A5f97D89289F8 |
| OperatorManager | 0x7CC5B6433eb33164c88F6512f56C566CFC3420BF | 0x1A46be28AB241F5A64F82ddFc384911520E3d557 |
| VaultManager | 0x14a6342A8C1Ef9856898F510FcCE377e46668F33 | 0x873c120b42C80D528389d85cEA9d4dC0197974aD |
| LedgerCrossChainManager | 0xa0a07a78c7d31E6f8698F48Fc9219f9a3030f38C | 0xb592011550a7E2a85c69EdF1788F460075b958fc |
| LedgerCrossChainRelay | 0x173B47eDBeCa665125edc24C509bfE545CDA60a9 | 0xa1a9C94bb24B09739Af80E6EfaB7ca0F576182bc |

A list of chainIDs can be found on [ChainList](https://chainlist.org/)
.

Latest contract ABI files can be found on our [GitHub](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/contract-evm-abi)
. All of our contracts are verified, so you can also see the ABI on their respective explorers.

Websocket API

Websocket API

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

On this page

*   [Market Data Base Endpoints:](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction#market-data-base-endpoints)
    
*   [Private User Data Stream Base Endpoints:](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction#private-user-data-stream-base-endpoints)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction#market-data-base-endpoints)

Market Data Base Endpoints:
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mainnet: `wss://ws-evm.orderly.org/ws/stream/{account_id}` Testnet: `wss://testnet-ws-evm.orderly.org/ws/stream/{account_id}` `{account_id}` is your account id. Users can subscribe/unsubscribe to topics such as: `orderbook`, `trade`, `ticker`, `kline_1m`. See individual topic pages for the full list of available topics.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction#private-user-data-stream-base-endpoints)

Private User Data Stream Base Endpoints:
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mainnet: `wss://ws-private-evm.orderly.org/v2/ws/private/stream/{account_id}` Testnet: `wss://testnet-ws-private-evm.orderly.org/v2/ws/private/stream/{account_id}` `{account_id}` is your account id Users need to be authenticated before subscribing to any topic. They would be disconnected if authentication fails. For more information, refer to the [Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/authentication)
 section. Users can subscribe/unsubscribe to topics such as: `account`, `balance`, `position`, `executionreport`. See individual topic pages for the full list of available topics.

[Get kline history](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-kline-history)
[PING/PONG](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/ping-pong)

# Building on Orderly - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Builder quickstart

Building on Orderly

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [What builders get](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#what-builders-get)
    
*   [High-level architecture](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#high-level-architecture)
    
*   [Start here](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#start-here)
    

Orderly gives builders the trading infrastructure, liquidity access, and integration tooling needed to launch their own derivatives application without building the full stack from scratch. Builders can create a perp DEX on top of Orderly’s shared-liquidity orderbook, while users interact from supported chains without switching to a separate trading chain in the product experience.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#what-builders-get)

What builders get
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Shared liquidity:** Access the same liquidity layer used across the Orderly ecosystem.
*   **Orderbook infrastructure:** Build on top of Orderly’s matching and order management services instead of operating your own engine.
*   **Omnichain user flows:** Support registration, deposits, withdrawals, and trading from supported chains.
*   **Flexible integration paths:** Choose between SDKs, UI components, Python tooling, or direct API access.
*   **Self-custody model:** Users keep control of their wallets while interacting with your application.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#high-level-architecture)

High-level architecture
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly’s omnichain system is organized into three layers: ![Orderly Overview Light](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/build-on-omnichain/light.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=dea85e008db60e170602adc5cc72aac1) ![Orderly Overview Dark](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/build-on-omnichain/dark.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=fc9a63649524a18ca950e7655ec427ad)

1.  **Asset Layer:** Lives on each supported chain and handles user-facing actions such as account registration, deposits, and withdrawals.
2.  **Settlement Layer:** Lives on a single chain and acts as the transaction ledger behind the scenes.
3.  **Engine Layer:** Runs the orderbook and related trading services used for order management and execution.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain#start-here)

Start here
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use these pages as your builder onboarding path:

*   **Plan the integration:** Start with the [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
     for the overall build and launch sequence.
*   **Understand the system:** Review the [Smart Contract Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/overview)
     and [Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/addresses)
    .
*   **Implement core user flows:** Follow [Accounts](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/accounts)
    , [Wallet Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication)
    , [Withdrawal & Deposit](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/withdrawal-deposit)
    , and [Order Management](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/order-management)
    .
*   **Cover trading operations:** Add [Settle PnL](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl)
    , [Custom Fees](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)
    , and real-time integrations from the [WebSocket API docs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction)
    .
*   **Integrate against the APIs:** Use the [API introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction)
     and [API authentication guide](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication)
    .
*   **Handle onboarding questions:** Use [Integration FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs)
     and [Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)
     for builder-facing support guidance.

Start with [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
. If you are using Orderly One, continue from [https://dex.orderly.network/](https://dex.orderly.network/)
. For implementation questions, join our [Discord](https://discord.gg/OrderlyNetwork)
.

[Smart Contract Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/overview)

⌘I

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview#evm)

EVM
----------------------------------------------------------

React Components SDK
--------------------

The components library gives you high level components, that can be used as building blocks to develop your DEX.

React Hooks SDK
---------------

The hooks library provides a series of React hooks for React developers to ensure a seamless integration experience.

Core SDK
--------

The core library is the foundational SDK used by hooks SDK with some helper functions for Orderly Key management, signatures, etc.

Perp SDK
--------

A library of calculation formulas related to Orderly futures trading. This library is designed to be purely functional, with no context dependencies.

[Release Notes](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/release-notes)

**Expanding to Mantle**The multichain expansion is progressing, and we are excited to announce our launch on Mantle, along with the introduction of support for USDC.e on the Mantle network.

**Updates**

*   New WebSocket topic for maintenance status
*   Adding optional “order\_tag” param to /v1/public/campaign/user API

*   Add sort parameter to GET referee endpoint
*   Performance improvements

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain#april-16-2024-minor-update)

April 16, 2024 - Minor Update
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**New bracket order support**We’re pleased to introduce bracket orders to Orderly, a feature designed to enhance your trading strategy by automatically setting stop loss and take profit orders alongside your primary trade. This functionality allows you to better manage risk and lock in profits without constant market monitoring.

**Traders can now see realized PnL**All order APIs now return realized PnL per position for orders closing or reducing open positions.

**Updated endpoints**Two new websocket topics `{broker_id}${symbol}@ticker` and `{broker_id}$tickers` to query each builder’s volume per market.Two new endpoints for verifying and checking referral codes.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain#march-12-2024-minor-update)

March 12, 2024 - Minor Update
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Builders are now chain specific**Given Orderly’s growth of supported chains, we understand some projects choose to only focus on certain chains, thus builders can now choose which chain they want to operate on.Supported builders for each chain can be retrieved through `GET /v1/public/chain_info`

**Automating rebates for trades with order tags**We’ve heard your feedback! The rebates for trades with specific order\_tag can now be automated by using our refferal system. Create a refferal code and use that refferal code in order\_tag when submitting orders.

**Introducing max slippage for market orders**We are excited to announce a new feature to our trading platform: the Max Slippage Setting for Market Orders. This functionality allows traders to set a maximum slippage for their market orders, giving unprecedented control over order execution relative to the market’s mid-price.For buy market orders, this means your order will be fully or partially filled at price levels greater than the order book’s mid-price adjusted by your specified slippage. Conversely, sell market orders will be executed at price levels less than the mid-price, also adjusted by your chosen slippage. This ensures that your orders are executed within your comfort zone of market price movement, enhancing predictability and control over your trading outcomes.For example, if the mid-price for MATIC is $1 and you place a buy market order with a 0.02% slippage, only portions of the order that meet the slippage criteria will be executed, with any remaining quantity being canceled. This precision ensures that you achieve the best possible price, according to your specified tolerance for slippage.

**Updated endpoints**Orders with an order\_tag can now be retrieved via `GET /v1/orders` & `GET /v1/algo/orders` APIs with the new order\_tag parameter.Enhancements to `GET /v1/referral/info` API, adding the following data in response:

*   **For referrers:**
    *   1d\_referrer\_rebate
    *   7d\_referrer\_rebate
    *   30d\_referrer\_rebate
    *   total\_referrer\_rebate
*   **For referees:**
    *   1d\_referee\_rebate
    *   7d\_referee\_rebate
    *   30d\_referee\_rebate
    *   total\_referee\_rebate

Adding volume and fees of all referrees for the refferal code in response for `GET /v1/referral/rebate_summary`Adding maker and taker volume breakdown in `GET /v1/volume/broker/daily` APIBuilders can now retrieve total fees collected under the criteria (given broker\_id, aggregate logic, order\_tag filter, and etc.) + builder rebate via`GET /v1/volume/broker/daily` APINew query parameters in `GET /v1/referral/admin_info` API:

*   New query parameter user\_address, to filter all codes of the matching user\_address only (should only match 1 account since the broker\_id has to match the builder admin one)
*   New query parameter account\_id, to filter all codes of the matching account\_id only
*   **NOTE:** Only user\_address or account\_id can be provided

Ability to change the referral code’s rebate split via `POST /v1/referral/edit_split`Additional response fields in `GET /v1/referral/admin_info` API:

*   total\_referrer\_rebate = Lifetime total referrer rebate on this code
*   total\_referee\_rebate = Lifetime total referee rebate on this code

Additional responses in `GET /v1/public/campaign/ranking` and `GET /v1/public/campaign/user` APIs:

*   total\_deposit\_amount
*   total\_withdrawal\_amount
*   start\_account\_value
*   end\_account\_value (which is current\_account\_value)

Increased update frequency of `GET /v1/volume/broker/daily` API to hourly from dailyAdded address in the response for `GET /v1/volume/broker/daily`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain#february-5-2024-major-update)

February 5, 2024 - Major Update
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Introducing the new referral system**We’ve heard your feedback and delivered! The long-awaited Orderly Referral System is finally here, marking a significant milestone in enhancing our platform. Tailored to enrich our community, this system introduces an exciting way for our existing users to connect with new users, strengthening our network through shared rewards.At the core of this initiative is a straightforward yet rewarding referral mechanism. Users can easily generate a personalized referral code and share it with potential new members.All endpoints for the referral system can be found under the [“Referral Program”](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code)
 section.To learn more about how the Orderly Referral System works, refer to [this article](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program)
.

**Introducing the new fee structure**We’re thrilled to unveil the latest enhancement to our services: the Custom Trading Fee System. This innovative feature introduces a suite of flexible fee-setting options, allowing builders to tailor fee structures to their unique user bases more effectively than ever before. By departing from the limitations of Orderly’s standard fees, builders now have the freedom to set fees that align perfectly with their strategies and customer profiles.We believe that this new feature will significantly enhance the developer experience on our platform, offering both improved user satisfaction and a competitive edge in the dynamic trading landscape.All fee rate settig endpoints are under the [“Fee Setting”](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate)
 section.The fee tiers are now deprecated from `/v1/public/fee_futures/program`, `/v1/broker/user_info` and `account` websocket.New volume-based fee rate setting tool can be found on our [GitHub](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-broker-fee-admin)
.To learn more about how the new fee structure works, refer to [this article](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)
.

**Adding New Order Types: Take Profit/Stop Loss**Traders can now use more order types: stop loss/take profit and positional stop loss/take.Features include:**1\. Three Trigger Price Options:** Initially available with the Mark Price, with future expansions to include Last Price and Index Price.**2\. Auto-Cancellation of TP/SL Orders:** Take Profit (TP) and Stop Loss (SL) orders will be automatically canceled if the position is closed, ensuring more precise trade management.**3\. Two Types of TP/SL Management:****\- Full/Entire Position TP/SL:** Apply TP/SL to the entire position with a limit of one maximum TP/SL per position.**\- Partial Position TP/SL:** Offers the flexibility to define the amount of the position to be closed, with a maximum of 10 partial TP and 10 partial SL orders on each side.This update empowers traders to implement more dynamic and sophisticated trading strategies with enhanced risk management features. As always, we are committed to improving and expanding the capabilities of the Orderly to meet the evolving needs of our users.All “algo order” endpoints are ammended to add the new order types.

**Introducing Smart Contract trading support**We are excited to announce the launch of the “Delegate Signer” feature in the Orderly, a new feature enabling Smart Contracts to trade.It allows Smart Contracts to assign an Externally Owned Account (EOA) to sign trades on their behalf using the EIP-712 signature. This solves the issue of Smart Contracts’ inability to sign for trades, broadening trading capabilities on the Orderly platform.New EIP712 signature types for Delegate Signer functions can be found [here](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication)
.New Delegate Signer function on-chain. Docs can be found [here](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication)
.New Delegate Signer APIs under [“Delegate Signer”](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer)
 section.Full write-up of how Delegate Signer functionality works can be found in [this article](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/delegate-signer)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain#january-4-2024-major-update)

January 4, 2024 - Major Update
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Introducing the new order types**Added create algo order(GET /v1/algo/order), edit algo order(PUT /v1/algo/order), cancel algo order(DELETE /v1/algo/order) and get algo order APIs(GET /v1/algo/order).Only STOP orders are supported for now.

**Changes to deposit withdrawal flow**Deposits and Withdrawals now require a fee to cover cross-chain message cost to/from Orderly Ledger contract.Added withdraw\_fee to each tokens in get token info(GET /v1/public/token) APIAdded allowCrossChainWithdrawal parameter to create withdraw request(POST /v1/withdraw\_request) APIAdded PENDING\_REBALANCE status for withdrawal request(POST /v1/withdraw\_request) in REST and WSS

**Introducing new endpoints to retrive user data**Added get account details (GET /v1/public/account) APIAdded get user statistics (GET /v1/client/statistics/daily) APIAdded get user fee tier(GET /v1/broker/user\_info) API

**Introducing new liquidation model**Added liquidation\_tier parameter for each symbolAdded symbols array and high/low risk perps in claim liquidated positions(POST /v1/liquidation) API

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain#october-13-2023-major-update)

October 13, 2023 - Major Update

August 10, 2023 - Minor Update
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Estimated timeline](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#estimated-timeline)
    
*   [FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#faqs)
    
*   [Do I need builder onboarding if I am only trading through an existing integration?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#do-i-need-builder-onboarding-if-i-am-only-trading-through-an-existing-integration)
    
*   [Where do I manage my builder settings after setup?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#where-do-i-manage-my-builder-settings-after-setup)
    
*   [Where do I find the current builder fee tiers?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#where-do-i-find-the-current-builder-fee-tiers)
    
*   [How do I know when my builder profile is ready?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#how-do-i-know-when-my-builder-profile-is-ready)
    
*   [What should I do after onboarding is complete?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#what-should-i-do-after-onboarding-is-complete)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#builder-onboarding)

Builder Onboarding
==========================================================================================================================

Use this page when your team is planning to launch its own trading frontend on Orderly and onboard its own users. This page covers builder setup and launch readiness. Once your builder identity is in place, move to the technical docs to implement the product itself. If you are an API trader using an existing builder, you do not need the full builder onboarding flow. Start from the [Integration FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs)
 instead.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#what-this-page-covers)

What this page covers
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This guide helps you:

*   choose the right launch path
*   set up your builder profile
*   understand how builder fees and tiers work
*   register your admin account
*   hand off cleanly into implementation and launch preparation

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-1-choose-your-launch-path)

Step 1: Choose your launch path
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two primary ways to launch on Orderly. Pick the path that matches your product scope and engineering needs.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#option-a-orderly-one)

Option A: Orderly One

Orderly One is the faster path to market if you want a lower-code route and a branded trading experience with less custom frontend work.

*   **Best for:** teams that want to launch quickly with a more packaged setup
*   **Next step:** continue from the [Orderly One portal](https://dex.orderly.network/dex)
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#option-b-custom-sdk/api-integration)

Option B: Custom SDK/API integration

Custom integration is the better fit when you want full control over product design, frontend behavior, and integration logic.

*   **Best for:** wallets, existing exchanges, and teams building a more customized trading journey
*   **Next step:** continue through the builder setup steps on this page, then move into the [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-2-contact-orderly-and-align-onboarding)

Step 2: Contact Orderly and align onboarding
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Start by reaching out through an official Orderly channel so the team can align with you on onboarding, launch scope, and any commercial or operational requirements.

*   [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/orderlynetwork)
    
*   [Telegram](https://t.me/Orderly_Discussions)
    
*   [Orderly website](https://orderly.network/)
    

This step is where you confirm the launch path, builder profile setup, and the practical sequence for getting to go-live.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-3-prepare-your-builder-profile)

Step 3: Prepare your builder profile
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To initialize your setup, you should be ready to provide the core information Orderly needs to create your builder profile:

*   **Builder ID**
*   **Builder name**
*   **Initial fee configuration**
*   **Launch path** (Orderly One or custom integration)

Once this information is confirmed, Orderly can provision your builder profile, assign the appropriate permissions, and help you move toward admin registration.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-4-review-the-fee-model-and-builder-tiering)

Step 4: Review the fee model and builder tiering
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before launch, make sure your team understands how builder monetization works:

*   Builders define the user-facing fee charged on their own frontend.
*   Orderly charges a separate base fee underneath.
*   The gap between those two numbers is the builder’s margin.

Base fees depend on your tier in the [Builder Staking Programme](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees#builder-staking-programme)
, so this page does not repeat the fee tables. Use the canonical [Trading fees](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees)
 page when you need the current tiers, requirements, and fee rates.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-5-register-your-admin-account)

Step 5: Register your admin account
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your builder profile is ready, register the admin account that will manage builder-level settings and fee configuration.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#for-orderly-one-builders)

For Orderly One builders

1.  Complete the setup or graduation flow in the [Orderly One portal](https://dex.orderly.network/dex)
    .
2.  Secure any Orderly keys issued during setup.
3.  Complete the wallet-linking steps required by the onboarding process.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#for-custom-sdk/api-builders)

For custom SDK/API builders

1.  Visit the [builder registration tool](https://github-dev.orderly.network/broker-registration/)
    .
2.  Connect your wallet and complete the registration flow.
3.  Secure any Orderly keys issued during setup.
4.  Complete the wallet-linking steps required to finish admin setup.

After the linkage is complete, you can access the [Orderly admin portal](https://admin.orderly.network/)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#step-6-confirm-readiness-and-move-into-implementation)

Step 6: Confirm readiness and move into implementation
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At this point, your team should have:

*   a builder profile
*   an admin account
*   a clear fee and tiering model
*   alignment with Orderly on launch readiness

From there, continue with the technical build and launch path:

*   [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
     for the recommended implementation sequence
*   [Building on Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
     for the architecture and integration surface area
*   [Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)
     for support channels and launch coordination

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#estimated-timeline)

Estimated timeline
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Stage | Estimated time |
| --- | --- |
| Choose launch path and provide builder information | Immediate |
| Builder profile creation | 1-2 business days |
| Admin registration and final linkage | 2-3 business days |

**Total onboarding time:** ~3-5 business days

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#faqs)

FAQs
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#do-i-need-builder-onboarding-if-i-am-only-trading-through-an-existing-integration)

Do I need builder onboarding if I am only trading through an existing integration?

No. If you are using an existing builder rather than launching your own frontend, start from the [Integration FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs)
.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#where-do-i-manage-my-builder-settings-after-setup)

Where do I manage my builder settings after setup?

Use the [Orderly admin portal](https://admin.orderly.network/)
 after your admin account has been linked and activated.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#where-do-i-find-the-current-builder-fee-tiers)

Where do I find the current builder fee tiers?

Use the [Trading fees](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees)
 page for the current builder fee model and the [Builder Staking Programme](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees#builder-staking-programme)
 tiers.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#how-do-i-know-when-my-builder-profile-is-ready)

How do I know when my builder profile is ready?

Orderly will notify your team as the builder profile and admin setup are completed during onboarding.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding#what-should-i-do-after-onboarding-is-complete)

What should I do after onboarding is complete?

Move directly into the [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
 so the builder setup work turns into an actual implementation plan.

[Product Roadmap](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap)
[Overview of $ORDER](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order)

![Orderly 2026 H1 roadmap](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/getting-started/roadmap-2026-h1.jpg?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9eee5069b3907bb4446c812eb0af6c2d)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap#2026-h1-focus)

2026 H1 focus
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The 2026 H1 roadmap targets four tracks — growth, trading capabilities, platform and developer tooling, and vault upgrades — each designed to increase volume, attract builders, and deepen liquidity across the Orderly ecosystem.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap#growth)

Growth

*   **Point Module for Orderly One:** A unified points system that connects campaigns, quests, and on-chain activity into a single incentive layer. This gives builders a turnkey engagement framework and drives measurable user retention, reducing the cost and effort of sustaining growth programs across the ecosystem.
*   **Multi-Level Referral:** Extends the referral model to multiple tiers, enabling communities and builders to create deeper acquisition funnels. This turns each active user into a distribution channel, producing compounding organic growth instead of one-off marketing spend.
*   **Orderly Monthly Competition:** A recurring monthly competition cadence that keeps active traders engaged and attracts new participants on a predictable schedule. Regular events drive consistent volume spikes and give builders a reliable calendar to plan promotions around.
*   **Expanded RWA Market:** Broadens Orderly’s tradable asset universe with real-world-asset-linked perpetuals. Unique instruments differentiate Orderly from competitors, open up a new trader demographic, and give builders exclusive markets unavailable on other venues.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap#trading-capabilities)

Trading capabilities

*   **Isolated Margin:** Lets traders margin each position independently, capping downside to a single trade instead of the entire account. This attracts professional and high-frequency traders who require granular risk control — directly increasing per-user volume and overall platform utilization.
*   **Permissionless Listing:** Removes manual gatekeeping from the market listing process, enabling anyone to propose and spin up new trading pairs. This accelerates market breadth at near-zero operational cost and positions Orderly as the fastest venue to list emerging tokens.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap#platform-&-developer)

Platform & developer

*   **System Performance Upgrade:** Reduces latency and increases throughput across the matching engine and settlement layer. Better execution quality under load is the single biggest factor for retaining high-volume integrators and attracting institutional flow.
*   **Core SDK Enhancements:** Cleaner APIs, richer primitives, and streamlined integration paths that cut builder onboarding time. Less custom glue code means faster go-to-market for new front-ends, directly growing the number of active builders shipping on Orderly.
*   **Mobile SDK:** Purpose-built SDK for iOS and Android covering wallet connection, account management, and order flow. This unlocks the mobile DeFi segment — a largely underserved market — for every builder in the Orderly ecosystem.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/roadmap#vault-upgrades)

Vault upgrades

*   **Permissionless Vault:** Opens vault creation to any strategist without manual approval, enabling a wider range of yield strategies to deploy on Orderly. More vaults drive more TVL and deeper liquidity, creating a self-reinforcing flywheel between vault depositors and the trading layer.
*   **Non-USDC Vault:** Supports vault deposits in assets beyond USDC — such as ETH, BTC, or LSTs — removing a key friction point for holders who prefer not to convert. This expands the addressable depositor base and increases total capital flowing into Orderly’s liquidity layer.

[What is Orderly?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)

$ORDER tokenomics

Overview of $ORDER

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Ecosystem participants](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order#ecosystem-participants)
    
*   [Token utility](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order#token-utility)
    

$ORDER is the native token of Orderly Network. It aligns incentives across the ecosystem — rewarding participants who provide liquidity, trade actively, build products, and govern the protocol.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order#ecosystem-participants)

Ecosystem participants
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Role | What they do |
| --- | --- |
| **Market makers** | Provide liquidity by placing buy and sell orders in the orderbook. They earn trading profits and liquidity provision rewards paid in $ORDER. |
| **Traders** | Buy and sell assets on Orderly, contributing to volume and price discovery. |
| **$ORDER holders** | Stake tokens to earn a share of protocol revenue and participate in governance decisions. |
| **Builders** | Develop front-end DEXs and other applications on Orderly’s infrastructure. They may receive builder grants based on trading volume, active users, and other metrics. |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order#token-utility)

Token utility
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Governance** — Staked $ORDER grants voting rights over protocol decisions as governance is formalized.
2.  **Earn VALOR** — VALOR measures your staking position (amount and duration) and entitles you to a share of the protocol treasury.
3.  **Trading reward boost** — Staking $ORDER increases your share of trading rewards.
4.  **Market making reward boost** — Staking $ORDER increases your share of market making rewards.
5.  **Future utilities** — Additional uses will be introduced with upcoming Orderly products and third-party integrations.

[Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
[Distribution and Emission Schedule](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule)

Markets & trading

Trade on Orderly

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

On this page

*   [Why trade on Orderly?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#why-trade-on-orderly)
    
*   [Two ways to trade](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#two-ways-to-trade)
    
*   [What can you trade?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#what-can-you-trade)
    
*   [Learn more](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#learn-more)
    

Orderly Network is a decentralized trading infrastructure that powers perpetual futures trading across multiple blockchains. All liquidity is shared in a single orderbook, so no matter which front-end you use or which chain you connect from, you access the same deep liquidity pool.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#why-trade-on-orderly)

Why trade on Orderly?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Shared liquidity** | One unified orderbook across all integrated DEXs — no fragmented liquidity. |
| **Omnichain access** | Deposit and trade from 15+ supported chains including Arbitrum, Base, Ethereum, Solana, and more. |
| **CEX-grade performance** | Central limit order book (CLOB) with off-chain matching and on-chain settlement. |
| **Multi-collateral** | Trade using USDC, USDT, ETH, SOL, BNB, WBTC, and other supported collateral types. |
| **Self-custody** | Your funds remain on-chain — no centralized custody risk. |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#two-ways-to-trade)

Two ways to trade
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Trade on a DEX
--------------

Choose from numerous front-ends built on Orderly. Each DEX offers its own UI and features while sharing the same liquidity.

Trade via API
-------------

For programmatic traders — connect directly to Orderly’s API for automated strategies and advanced execution.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#what-can-you-trade)

What can you trade?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly supports **100+ perpetual futures markets** including major crypto assets (BTC, ETH, SOL) and real-world assets (S&P 500, NASDAQ 100, Gold, equities). All contracts are denominated and settled in USDC.

View all supported markets
--------------------------

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly#learn-more)

Learn more
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderbook Design
----------------

How Orderly’s CLOB combines CEX-level speed with on-chain settlement.

Accounts
--------

How accounts work across different front-ends.

Order Types
-----------

Market, limit, stop-loss, and other supported order types.

Trading Fees
------------

Fee structure for traders and the Builder Staking programme.

Supported Chains
----------------

All EVM and non-EVM chains you can trade from.

Multi-Collateral
----------------

Collateral types, weights, LTV, and auto-conversion rules.

[$ORDER Related Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses)
[Orderly Orderbook Design](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design)

Navigation

$ORDER tokenomics

esORDER

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Redemption (vesting)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#redemption-vesting)
    
*   [Burn mechanics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#burn-mechanics)
    
*   [Why escrowed tokens?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#why-escrowed-tokens)
    

esORDER is the escrowed form of $ORDER. Starting from epoch 7, trading rewards and market making rewards are paid in esORDER instead of liquid $ORDER. When you receive esORDER, you can either:

*   **Stake it** — esORDER staking provides the same benefits as staking $ORDER (VALOR accrual, reward boosts).
*   **Vest it** — Convert esORDER back to liquid $ORDER over a vesting period.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#redemption-vesting)

Redemption (vesting)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Converting esORDER to $ORDER requires a vesting period. The conversion ratio increases linearly with the vesting duration:

| Option | Duration | Conversion rate |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Minimum vesting | 15 days | 50% |
| Maximum vesting | 90 days | 100% |

The remaining 50% unlocks linearly between day 15 and day 90. You can claim once at any point during this window.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#burn-mechanics)

Burn mechanics
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you redeem esORDER before the full 90-day vesting period, the unredeemed portion is permanently burned. **Example:** Bob receives 100 esORDER and redeems after the minimum 15-day vesting. He receives 50 $ORDER — the other 50 $ORDER is burned and removed from circulation.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/esorder#why-escrowed-tokens)

Why escrowed tokens?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Reduces sell pressure** — Rewards vest over time rather than being immediately liquid, unlike typical farming tokens.
*   **Compounding loop** — esORDER can be staked to earn additional rewards, incentivizing users to keep tokens staked rather than vesting immediately.

[Staking Information](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/order-staking/staking-information)
[$ORDER Related Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses)

⌘I

---

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#token-allocation)

Token allocation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Category | %   | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Community | 55% | Long-term incentives: Retroactive Airdrop (13.3%), Ecosystem Incentives (25%), Builder Rewards (8.35%), Future Product Launches (8.35%) |
| Strategic Investors | 15% | Early backers and strategic investors |
| Team & Advisors | 20% | Founding team, contributors, future hires, and advisors |
| Foundation | 10% | Ecosystem grants, $ORDER liquidity on CEX and DEX |

![ORDER Token Allocation](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/order-tokenomics/token-allocation-light.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9b52be3a850f726ca121c17abaad8f4e) ![ORDER Token Allocation](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/order-tokenomics/token-allocation-dark.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=832355212f53147f46a5f6326151bc61)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#emission-schedule)

Emission schedule
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Emission Schedule Light](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/order-tokenomics/emission-light.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=1f42975ffc57b166cc0ca7ecdc7be9f8) ![Emission Schedule Dark](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/order-tokenomics/emission-dark.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9674561bb2c23b2e56d1a51bc114893e)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#community-55%)

Community (55%)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#epochs)

Epochs

Trading rewards and market making rewards unlock based on epochs. One epoch = 14 days. These rewards are intended to unlock over 200 epochs (~7 years), subject to future governance changes.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#retroactive-airdrop-%E2%80%94-13-3%-133000000-$order)

Retroactive Airdrop — 13.3% (133,000,000 $ORDER)

Unlocked at TGE as retroactive rewards to early supporters.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#ecosystem-incentives-%E2%80%94-25%-250000000-$order)

Ecosystem Incentives — 25% (250,000,000 $ORDER)

Distributed based on measurable contributions: trading volume, user engagement, and ecosystem growth impact.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#builder-rewards-%E2%80%94-8-35%-83500000-$order)

Builder Rewards — 8.35% (83,500,000 $ORDER)

Grants for builders based on trading volume, daily active users, new users, and other criteria. Some grants were committed to early builders before TGE; the program remains open to new builders.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#future-product-launches-%E2%80%94-8-35%-83500000-$order)

Future Product Launches — 8.35% (83,500,000 $ORDER)

Locked until usage is formally established and communicated.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#strategic-investors-15%-%E2%80%94-150000000-$order)

Strategic Investors (15% — 150,000,000 $ORDER)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Locked for the first 6 months after TGE, followed by 3.5-year linear vesting. No cliff or sudden unlock when linear vesting starts.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#team-&-advisors-20%-%E2%80%94-200000000-$order)

Team & Advisors (20% — 200,000,000 $ORDER)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1-year cliff (25% unlocks), followed by 3-year linear vesting. No team tokens unlock before the 1-year cliff.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/distribution-and-emission-schedule#foundation-10%-%E2%80%94-100000000-$order)

Foundation (10% — 100,000,000 $ORDER)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Designated for $ORDER liquidity on CEX and DEX, ecosystem grants, partnerships, and related initiatives.

*   38,500,000 $ORDER available post-TGE for liquidity.
*   12,575,000 $ORDER earmarked for pre-arranged marketing partnerships over 2 years.
*   Remaining tokens held in Foundation reserve with full transparency on usage.
*   The Foundation’s goal is to ultimately transfer full control to the DAO.

[Overview of $ORDER](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/overview-of-order)
[Staking](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/order-staking/staking)

OFT
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

$ORDER Omnichain Fungible Token (OFT) contracts for cross-chain transfers.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#$order-oft-adapter)

$ORDER OFT Adapter

Converts native $ORDER (ERC-20) on Ethereum into the OFT version at a 1:1 ratio, enabling transfers to and from other supported chains. **Network:** Ethereum | **Address:** [`0x17435cC940e03aA52c349738c72C7Aa44FfA6525`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x17435cC940e03aA52c349738c72C7Aa44FfA6525)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#$order-oft)

$ORDER OFT

Cross-chain $ORDER token that maintains a 1:1 ratio with the native ERC-20 on Ethereum. **EVM chains** — Address: `0x4E200fE2f3eFb977d5fd9c430A41531FB04d97B8`

Applications & Cross-Chain Messaging
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Contracts that manage $ORDER distribution, vesting, and cross-chain messaging.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#airdrop-mdl1)

Airdrop MDL1

Handles the claiming process for the TGE $ORDER airdrop. **Network:** Ethereum | **Address:** [`0x94FdC38C4A8e34248F8Dd19CB3336E0295227023`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x94FdC38C4A8e34248F8Dd19CB3336E0295227023)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#team/investor-$order-vesting)

Team/Investor $ORDER Vesting

Manages $ORDER vesting for the founding team, contributors, and strategic investors. **Network:** Ethereum | **Address:** [`0x6d00268a47d48474f999c18210c6877491ae6fb3`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x6d00268a47d48474f999c18210c6877491ae6fb3)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#omnichain-ledger)

Omnichain Ledger

Manages staking, vesting, and reward operations — including claiming rewards, staking/unstaking, $ORDER withdrawals, esORDER vesting, and VALOR redemption. **Network:** Orderly | **Address:** [`0x7819704B69a38fD63Cc768134b8410dc08B987D0`](https://explorer.orderly.network/address/0x7819704B69a38fD63Cc768134b8410dc08B987D0)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#proxy-contract)

Proxy Contract

Intermediary contract that lets users interact with the Omnichain Ledger from different EVM chains without directly interfacing with cross-chain messaging protocols. **Address:** `0xC8A8Ce0Ab010E499ca57477AC031358febCbbF17`

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/tokenomics/addresses#occ-manager)

OCC Manager

Affiliate program

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#overview)

Overview
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The referral system is a crucial component of the Orderly ecosystem, designed to incentivize user growth and engagement through a structured rewards program. A builder can choose to share part of its builder fees to incentivize a distributor, affiliate, or referrer for bringing in new traders. The ecosystem consists of three key participants:

1.  **The Builder (DEX):** The DEX built on Orderly. Builders determine the fee structures and maximum rebate limits.
2.  **The Referrer (Affiliate):** A user (e.g., a KOL, influencer, or community leader) who invites others to trade. They can modify the fee split between themselves and the trader, but not the maximum rebate limit.
3.  **The Referee (Trader):** The new user who signs up using a referral code.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#how-it-works)

How It Works
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#1-the-binding-process)

1\. The Binding Process

The binding is established when a new user (Referee) connects to a Builder and enters a referral code. Their account is permanently bound to the Referrer. A bound referral code cannot be removed or modified after binding. ![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA/images/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program/bind-ref-code.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA&q=85&s=282ae62757916c2873a092c86884d75d)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#2-the-reward-split)

2\. The Reward Split

When a Referee executes a trade, the trading fee is split automatically. Orderly Network retains a base protocol fee, and the remaining **Builder Fee** is distributed among the Builder, Referrer, and Referee based on the configured rebate settings.

*   **Referrer Rebate:** Commission earned by the affiliate for inviting the user.
*   **Referee Rebate:** A discount returned to the trader, incentivizing them to use the referral code.
*   **Builder Revenue:** The remaining portion of the fee retained by the platform.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#example-calculation)

Example Calculation
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example demonstrates how fees flow between parties. **Scenario:**

*   **Orderly Base Fee:** 3 bps Taker fee and 0 bps Maker fee.
*   **Builder Fee Structure:** 6 bps (0.06%) Taker Fee and 3 (0.03%) bps Maker Fee.
*   **Builder Net Profit for each trade (regardless of maker or taker):** 3 bps.

**Referral Configuration:** The Builder generates a code for a KOL with an **80% Max Rebate**, split **50:50** between the Referrer and Referee.

| Party | Share of Net Profit | Calculation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Total Net Profit** | **100%** | **3 bps** |
| **Referee (Trader)** | 40% | Receives 40% of the 3 bps profit as a discount. |
| **Referrer (Affiliate)** | 40% | Receives 40% of the 3 bps profit as commission. |
| **Builder** | 20% | Retains the remaining 20% of the 3 bps profit. |

**In Dollar Terms:** If a user generates **$10 USDC** in _Net Builder Fees_:

1.  **$4.00** is returned to the Trader (Referee).
2.  **$4.00** is credited to the Affiliate (Referrer).
3.  **$2.00** is retained by the Builder.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#configuration-for-builders)

Configuration for Builders
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Builders manage referral codes and rebate rates via the Orderly Admin Console. **Admin console:** [https://admin.orderly.network/referral](https://admin.orderly.network/referral)
 Click ""+ create code” button. ![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA/images/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program/admin-portal.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA&q=85&s=c1c7cba226dfbd4bcc3291f046c94878)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#setup-fields)

Setup Fields

A popup window will appear for Builders to create a referral code for an address. ![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA/images/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program/create-ref-code.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA&q=85&s=7935165849068b2d3226883a3cf0c620)

1.  **User Address:** The wallet address of the Affiliate (Referrer) who will own this code.
2.  **Referral Code:** The unique string traders will enter (e.g., `REFFCODE1`).
3.  **Max Rebate:** The total percentage of the Builder’s Net Profit allocated for rebates (e.g., 80%).
4.  **Split Slider:** Determines how the rebate is divided:
    *   **Referrer Rebate:** The percentage of the rebate the Affiliate keeps.
    *   **Referee Rebate:** The percentage of the rebate returned to the Trader.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#distribution)

Distribution
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rewards are calculated automatically by the Orderly protocol.

*   **Frequency:** Rewards are typically distributed **daily**.
*   **Currency:** Payouts are settled in **USDC**.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program#auto-referral-system)

Auto referral system
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA/images/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program/auto-referral.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=xQGsQ2u6o_VWY8PA&q=85&s=61bbe995cf73d69714738bbb2896de47) Similar to the affiliate system, the auto-referral system grants traders a referral code once they reach a specific trading volume. This means that instead of manual entry, traders are automatically assigned a code based on their trading activity.

[Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Builder's Quick Guide)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide)
[Builder Guidelines](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines)

# Builder Guidelines - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Builder programs

Builder Guidelines

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
