On this page

*   [Why These Guidelines Exist](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#why-these-guidelines-exist)
    
*   [What Orderly One Is (and Isn’t)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#what-orderly-one-is-and-isn%E2%80%99t)
    
*   [Independent by Design](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#independent-by-design)
    
*   [Names and Brands That Build Trust](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#names-and-brands-that-build-trust)
    
*   [Talk Straight, Build Credibility](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#talk-straight-build-credibility)
    
*   [Build for the Long Game](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#build-for-the-long-game)
    
*   [Be Compliant](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#be-compliant)
    
*   [How We Shine a Spotlight](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#how-we-shine-a-spotlight)
    
*   [When We May Step In](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#when-we-may-step-in)
    
*   [Examples: Do’s and Don’ts](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#examples-do%E2%80%99s-and-don%E2%80%99ts)
    
*   [Moving Forward Together](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#moving-forward-together)
    

Orderly One is built to empower developers to launch independent DEXes with speed and creativity. Every DEX built on our infrastructure reflects not only your project but also the wider ecosystem. These Builder Guidelines sit alongside the Orderly One Terms of Use and are meant as a practical code of conduct for builders, outlining how projects should present themselves and how Orderly may respond if the standards are not met. These Builder Guidelines may be updated from time to time, and the latest version shall apply once updated.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#why-these-guidelines-exist)

**Why These Guidelines Exist**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Builder Guidelines exist to:

1.  Protect the reputation and integrity of the Orderly brand.
2.  Safeguard the ecosystem from harmful, misleading, or unlawful behaviour.
3.  Give builders clarity about expectations from Day 1.

Orderly One celebrates creativity and independence, but with independence comes responsibility. Projects that are offensive, defamatory, misleading, unlawful, or reputationally damaging may not be promoted through Orderly channels and, in serious cases, may lose access to Orderly One services altogether. By following these Guidelines, you help ensure the ecosystem remains trusted, respected, and welcoming to both users and builders.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#what-orderly-one-is-and-isn%E2%80%99t)

**What Orderly One Is (and Isn’t)**
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly One is a no-code SDK that lets developers launch decentralized exchanges (DEXes) quickly and efficiently. By integrating the SDK, you gain access to Orderly’s backend liquidity, order-matching engine, and cross-chain connectivity. Orderly One is a toolkit, not a managed service. You control your front-end, branding, and user experience. DEXes created with Orderly One are independently developed and operated by builders, not by Orderly Network.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#independent-by-design)

**Independent by Design**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Do not present your DEX as an “official Orderly product.”
2.  You may say your project is “built with Orderly One” or “powered by Orderly liquidity.”
3.  Make it clear that users interact with your DEX at their own risk, and that Orderly is not responsible for your operations, marketing, or outcomes.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#names-and-brands-that-build-trust)

**Names and Brands That Build Trust**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Avoid offensive, defamatory, discriminatory, or otherwise inappropriate names or imagery.
2.  Avoid names that are misleading, confusing, or deliberately controversial.
3.  Do not copy or impersonate Orderly, other projects, companies, or individuals. It is important to respect the intellectual property rights of others.
4.  Choose branding that reflects originality, professionalism, and respect for the community.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#talk-straight-build-credibility)

**Talk Straight, Build Credibility**
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Be transparent about your product, fees, risks, and governance.
2.  Do not make exaggerated or misleading claims such as “guaranteed returns” or “risk-free profits.”
3.  Keep your messaging consistent across your website, documentation, and community channels.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#build-for-the-long-game)

**Build for the Long Game**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Treat your users with fairness and respect.
2.  Avoid pump-and-dump schemes, scams, or misleading gimmicks.
3.  Do not adopt imagery, memes, or tactics that could be defamatory, abusive, or damaging to the ecosystem’s reputation.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#be-compliant)

**Be Compliant**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  You are fully and solely responsible for ensuring your DEX complies with all applicable laws and regulations.
2.  Do carefully consider the users you allow on your platform. Accepting users from certain countries may attract licensing requirements, while accepting users from sanctioned countries may result in your DEX enabling sanctioned activity.
3.  While it is tempting to name your DEX after a famous person, brand or event, this may breach intellectual property laws.
4.  While we have mentioned a few examples of legal considerations you should factor in for your DEX, this is by no means exhaustive, and these issues vary from DEX to DEX. You should seek legal advice if you are unsure of the legal considerations for your DEX. Orderly is not responsible for your DEX nor any such legal risks.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#how-we-shine-a-spotlight)

**How We Shine a Spotlight**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Orderly may choose to showcase projects on its channels, but this is always at our discretion.
2.  Projects that show professionalism, transparency, and alignment with ecosystem values are more likely to be featured.
3.  Projects that create reputational, legal, or regulatory risk will not be promoted.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#when-we-may-step-in)

**When We May Step In**
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Access to Orderly One SDK, liquidity, and connectivity is provided at Orderly’s discretion.
2.  Access may be suspended or withdrawn if a project engages in offensive, defamatory, misleading, or high-risk behaviour.
3.  Access may also be suspended if a project creates reputational, legal, or regulatory risk.
4.  These decisions are made to protect the integrity of the ecosystem and community.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#examples-do%E2%80%99s-and-don%E2%80%99ts)

**Examples: Do’s and Don’ts**
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

✓ Launch a DEX with a fun, clear, and professional name.  
✓ Communicate transparently about fees, risks, and governance.  
✓ Use branding that is original and consistent.  
✓ Build a community presence that is constructive and engaged. ✗ Launch with an offensive, misleading, or copied name.  
✗ Over-promise returns or imply official Orderly endorsement.  
✗ Use branding that imitates others or confuses users.  
✗ Engage in pump-and-dump style marketing that damages trust.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/builder-guidelines#moving-forward-together)

**Moving Forward Together**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly One gives builders the tools to innovate, experiment, and scale. These Guidelines exist to protect both Orderly and the ecosystem by ensuring that projects built on our infrastructure maintain integrity while leaving room for creativity. By building responsibly, and with personality, you strengthen your project and contribute to a community based on trust, transparency, and innovation.

[Affiliate & Referral Program (Legacy)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program)
[Supplemental Terms for DEXes](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/supplemental-terms-for-dexes)

# Orderly Orderbook Design - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Markets & trading

Orderly Orderbook Design

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Orderbook design](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#orderbook-design)
    
*   [Omnichain approach](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#omnichain-approach)
    
*   [MEV protection](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#mev-protection)
    

Orderly is an omnichain decentralized orderbook protocol. It provides the matching engine, liquidity, and infrastructure for builders (DEXes) to offer perpetual futures trading without building it themselves.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#orderbook-design)

Orderbook design
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly uses a **central limit order book (CLOB)** — the same type of system used by major centralized exchanges. The key difference: Orderly settles everything on-chain, combining CEX-level performance with decentralized transparency.

*   **Transparency:** All orders are recorded on the blockchain and publicly verifiable.
*   **Security:** On-chain settlement means data is immutable and tamper-proof.
*   **Speed:** A high-performance off-chain matching engine provides low-latency execution.
*   **Self-custody:** Users retain full control of their funds at all times.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#omnichain-approach)

Omnichain approach
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unlike most DEXes that run separate order books per chain, Orderly brings all orders from all supported chains into a **single shared order book**. This unifies liquidity across chains. The system has three layers: **Asset Layer (Asset Vaults)** — on each supported chain

*   Where your funds reside. You interact with this layer when registering, depositing, and withdrawing.

**Settlement Layer (Orderly L2)** — on a single chain

*   A transaction ledger that stores all transaction and user data on-chain. Users do not interact with it directly.

**Engine Layer (Orderbook)** — order-related services

*   Where trade execution happens. Includes the matching engine, risk engine, and other services. You interact with this layer when managing orders.

After orders are matched, they settle on the Orderly L2 (built with OP Stack), which periodically settles to Ethereum. Cross-layer communication (deposits, withdrawals, balance updates) is powered by LayerZero.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/orderbook-design#mev-protection)

MEV protection
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MEV (maximal extractable value) is when someone profits by front-running or reordering transactions before they are confirmed on-chain. Orderly mitigates this through:

*   **Fast matching:** The sequencer matches orders with minimal latency, leaving little window for front-running.
*   **Deep liquidity:** A deep order book makes price manipulation significantly harder.
*   **Off-chain matching:** Orders are matched off-chain in a neutral environment — there is no on-chain transaction to front-run.

[Trade on Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trade-on-orderly)
[Glossary](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/glossary)

⌘I

---

# Permissionless Listing - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Markets & trading

Permissionless Listing

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

On this page

*   [Standard Listing vs. Permissionless Listing](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#standard-listing-vs-permissionless-listing)
    
*   [How Permissionless Listing Works](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#how-permissionless-listing-works)
    
*   [Per-Builder risk isolation](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#per-builder-risk-isolation)
    
*   [Per-Builder symbols](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#per-builder-symbols)
    
*   [Controlled launch sequence](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#controlled-launch-sequence)
    
*   [Price sources](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#price-sources)
    
*   [Real-time monitoring](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#real-time-monitoring)
    
*   [Who Can List](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#who-can-list)
    
*   [How Community Listed Markets Differ for Traders](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#how-community-listed-markets-differ-for-traders)
    
*   [What this means in practice](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#what-this-means-in-practice)
    
*   [What’s Next](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#what%E2%80%99s-next)
    
*   [Related Pages](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#related-pages)
    

On most perpetual exchanges, listing a new market is a platform decision — the team evaluates the asset, sets up the infrastructure, and takes on the risk. This works for high-demand assets, but it also means the platform becomes the bottleneck: every new market competes for the same listing queue, the same shared Insurance Fund, and the same risk budget. Permissionless Listing removes that bottleneck. Qualified Builders can list new perpetual markets themselves, on their own timeline, without waiting for Orderly to evaluate and approve each one. In return, the Builder takes direct responsibility for the market they introduce — funding the Insurance Fund, supporting liquidity, and managing risk for that market independently.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#standard-listing-vs-permissionless-listing)

Standard Listing vs. Permissionless Listing
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to list markets on Orderly. While the standard model is built for platform-wide stability, Permissionless Listing is built for Builder-level autonomy.

| Feature | Standard Listing | Permissionless Listing |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Who manages it** | Orderly | Individual Builder |
| **Insurance Fund** | Shared across the platform | Dedicated per Builder |
| **Listing Process** | Evaluated & approved by Orderly | Instant & self-managed |
| **Risk Parameters** | Standardized & conservative | Customizable by Builder |

Standard listing is well-suited for established assets. But for long-tail assets or markets that a specific Builder’s user base demands, Permissionless Listing provides a faster, more flexible path.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#how-permissionless-listing-works)

How Permissionless Listing Works
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Permissionless Listing decouples the risk of new markets from the rest of the platform. The key design principle: **a Builder’s market should only be able to affect that Builder’s own risk exposure.**

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#per-builder-risk-isolation)

Per-Builder risk isolation

Each Builder that lists a market must set up and manage their own accounts:

*   **Insurance Fund (IF) account** — A dedicated sub-account that covers liquidation losses for all of the Builder’s listed markets. The minimum required balance is **$50,000 per active symbol** (e.g., 3 active markets = $150,000 minimum). If the IF drops too low, the system will automatically restrict trading or trigger delisting.
*   **Market Maker (MM) accounts** — Optional sub-accounts for market-making. Builders can bind one or more MM accounts to their listed symbols to provide liquidity.

The IF is completely separate from Orderly’s platform IF and from any other Builder’s IF. If a liquidation loss occurs, it draws only from that Builder’s IF. If the IF is fully depleted, auto-deleveraging (ADL) is scoped to that specific market only. No other markets or users on the platform are affected. This is why all permissionlessly listed markets are **Isolated Margin only**. If these markets allowed Cross Margin, a sudden crash on a long-tail token could cascade into a user’s BTC or ETH positions. Isolated Margin ensures that each position’s risk is self-contained — the worst case for a trader is losing the margin allocated to that specific position.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#per-builder-symbols)

Per-Builder symbols

Multiple Builders can list the same underlying asset independently. If Orderly already has a standard `PERP_BTC_USDC` market and Builder A lists their own BTC perpetual, these are completely separate markets — separate order books, separate liquidity, separate Insurance Funds, separate risk parameters. Builder B can also list BTC. None of these interfere with each other. On the trading page, each market is displayed with the symbol and the Builder’s name (e.g., `BTC woofi`) to clearly show who is operating it.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#controlled-launch-sequence)

Controlled launch sequence

New markets don’t go straight to full trading. They follow a phased launch:

1.  **POST\_ONLY** — The market is live but only accepts limit orders. This gives Market Makers time to build liquidity on both sides of the order book before real trading begins.
2.  **ACTIVE** — Once ±2% order book depth reaches $100,000 on both bid and ask sides and holds for 10 minutes, the market automatically opens for full trading including market orders.

If conditions deteriorate after launch — insufficient depth, Insurance Fund running low, funding rate anomalies — the system can automatically force the market into **REDUCE\_ONLY** mode (only position-closing allowed) or initiate delisting entirely. These circuit breakers apply per-market, not platform-wide.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#price-sources)

Price sources

Index prices are aggregated from a combination of CEX price feeds and on-chain oracles. Builders select which sources to use when configuring a market; at least one valid source is required, and two or more are recommended to reduce single-point-of-failure risk. Currently supported oracle sources are **Pyth** and **Stork**. If all configured sources become unavailable, the market is automatically placed into reduce-only mode.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#real-time-monitoring)

Real-time monitoring

Builders receive **real-time Telegram notifications** throughout the market lifecycle — from launch status updates to risk alerts when IF balance, order book depth, or funding rate thresholds are breached.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#who-can-list)

Who Can List
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Permissionless Listing is available to Builders in the **Diamond Tier** of the [Builder Staking Program](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees#builder-staking-programme)
. Each Builder can have up to **5 active markets** by default (adjustable by Orderly). Builders configure price sources, trading parameters, and Insurance Fund setup through the Builder dashboard.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#how-community-listed-markets-differ-for-traders)

How Community Listed Markets Differ for Traders
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Permissionlessly listed markets appear on the frontend as **“Community Listed”**. They work like standard Orderly perpetuals in most ways — same matching engine, same order types, same settlement — but with several important differences:

|     | Standard Orderly markets | Community Listed markets |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Who operates it** | Orderly | Individual Builder |
| **Insurance Fund** | Shared platform IF, managed by Orderly | Dedicated per-Builder IF, funded by the Builder |
| **Margin mode** | Cross Margin and Isolated Margin | **Isolated Margin only** |
| **What happens if IF is depleted** | Platform backstop | ADL applied only to positions in that market |
| **Liquidity** | Platform-coordinated | Depends entirely on the Builder’s market-making |
| **Risk controls** | Platform-wide standards | Market-specific, can be restricted independently |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#what-this-means-in-practice)

What this means in practice

*   **Your other positions are safe.** A liquidation on a Community Listed position cannot touch your margin on other markets. Your BTC cross-margin position is unaffected even if a Community Listed token crashes.
*   **Liquidity varies.** Community Listed markets depend on the Builder’s own market-making setup, which means spreads and depth can vary significantly compared to standard markets.
*   **Markets can be restricted or delisted.** If a Community Listed market’s liquidity, funding rate, or Insurance Fund health deteriorates beyond thresholds, the system will automatically restrict or delist it. This protects traders from continuing to trade in unhealthy conditions.
*   **A risk disclosure is shown before your first trade.** The first time you trade a Community Listed market, a disclosure explains the differences so you can make an informed decision.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#what%E2%80%99s-next)

What’s Next
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Permissionless Listing is the first step toward a fully open market infrastructure. Planned future expansions include:

*   **Custom oracle support** — Allow Builders to integrate their own oracle sources beyond the current supported set.
*   **RWA market support** — Extend Permissionless Listing to real-world asset (RWA) perpetual markets.
*   **Permissionless Vault** — Enable community-driven vault strategies built around permissionlessly listed markets.
*   **Public MM Program** — Open market-making participation beyond Builder-managed MM accounts, allowing broader liquidity provision for Community Listed markets.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/permissionless-listing#related-pages)

Related Pages
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Builders](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders)

Start building on Orderly
===============================================================================================================================

Ready to launch your own trading platform? Join the ecosystem and leverage Orderly’s omnichain infrastructure to scale your business.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders#why-become-a-builder)

Why become a Builder?

*   **Immediate liquidity:** Tap into a shared orderbook without the need for bootstrapping.
*   **Revenue autonomy:** Define your own fee structure and keep **100% of the margin** above base fees.
*   **Builder Staking Program benefits:** Stake $ORDER or grow your volume to lower your base fees and unlock dedicated support.

**[Become a Builder today →](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
**

# FAQ - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/faqs#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Navigation

Security, legal & FAQ

FAQ

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

What is Orderly?

Orderly is a combination of an orderbook-based trading infrastructure and a robust liquidity layer offering perpetual futures orderbooks. Unlike traditional platforms, Orderly doesn’t have a front end; instead, it operates at the core of the ecosystem, providing essential services to projects built on top of it.Our DEX white-label solution is carefully crafted to save builders time and capital while granting access to our bootstrapped liquidity. Picture having the best features of CEXs while keeping settlements on-chain and maintaining full self-custody.With Orderly, anyone can create a trading application thanks to our seamless plug-and-play experience leveraging our liquidity and composability.Looking ahead, our grand vision is to create an omnichain protocol, connecting traders from both EVM and non-EVM chains within the same orderbook.

How do I get started?

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/faqs#for-traders)

For traders:
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Traders can start using any interface built on Orderly listed on [this](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders)
 page

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/faqs#for-developers-and-api-traders)

For Developers and API traders:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EVM Quick Start
---------------

What can I do on Orderly?

Developers can build a full suite of products:**Builders:** Create your own Perps DEX on EVM. Benefit from our professional trading infrastructure and deep liquidity.**Perps Aggregators:** Unlock the power of shared orderbook and liquidity. Access to all of Orderly’s Perps ecosystem liquidity directly by creating a front-end.**Wallets & Custodians:** Connect to Orderly and offer your users the best Swap rates for major assets. Build your own Swap widget powered by Orderly and open up our ecosystem to all your users.**Trading Desks & Sophisticated Traders:** Use our API and enjoy a CEX-level trading experience thanks to our low-latency orderbook.**Games/dApps:** Enhance your in-app experience and token utility with our in-game Swaps using the built-in widget. Keep your users engaged in your app and avoid redirecting them to third parties.**Trading Bots:** Connect to our orderbook and offer the industry’s best Perps rates. Enjoy SL/limit orders, gasless transactions, and fully customizable fees.**Hedging:** Safeguard your positions on other exchanges by using our orderbook for effective hedging strategies.  
Traders can explore an ecosystem of front-ends built on Orderly. A comprehensive list can be found [here](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders)
.

How are trading fees calculated/charged?

Fees are deducted from the converted token for spot trades (e.g., converting USDC to ETH incurs fees in ETH) and added to the cost position for perps (denominated in USDC).Each builder sets their own fee rates.

Can I list my token on Orderly?

Yes. If you want to list your token on Orderly, start here: [https://orderly.network/listing/](https://orderly.network/listing/)
.

What are the different order types?

**MARKET:** It matches until the full size is executed. If the size is too large (larger than the whole book) or the matching price exceeds the price limit (refer to `price_range`), then the remaining quantity will be canceled. **IOC (Immediate or Cancel):** It matches as much as possible at the order\_price. If not fully executed, then the remaining quantity will be canceled. **FOK (Fill or Kill):** If the order can be fully executed at the order\_price, then the order gets fully executed otherwise would be canceled without any execution. **POST ONLY:** If the order executes with any maker trades at the time of placement, then it will be canceled without any execution. **REDUCE ONLY:** Executes buy or sell orders which only reduce a current position

How do you ensure no fake orders are settled on-chain?

All orders coming into the Orderly off-chain matching engine are signed by users’ private trading keys and are settled on-chain with those signatures. Public keys are stored on the contract, and the signatures are verified to validate orders.The above model ensures that even if the operator is compromised, no fake orders are executed and all user funds are safe.

Are there code examples or tutorials for how to build on Orderly?

Yes! This documentation includes code examples throughout and you can find full tutorials/sdk with code examples here:[EVM SDK](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)

[Orderly Distributor Program](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program)

PING/PONG

[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)

{
        "event":"ping"
    }

Response

{
        "event":"pong",
        "ts":1614667590000
    }

The server will send a ping command to the client every 10 seconds. If the pong from client is not received within 10 seconds for 10 consecutive times, it will actively disconnect the client. The client can also send ping every 10s to keep alive.

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| event | string | Y   | ping/pong |

    {
        "event":"ping"
    }
    

Response

    {
        "event":"pong",
        "ts":1614667590000
    }
    

[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction)

Affiliate program

Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Builder's Quick Guide)

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [1\. Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#1-overview)
    
*   [2\. Prerequisites](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#2-prerequisites)
    
*   [3\. New vs. legacy affiliate programs](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#3-new-vs-legacy-affiliate-programs)
    
*   [4\. Coexistence of legacy affiliate program](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#4-coexistence-of-legacy-affiliate-program)
    
*   [5\. Referral commission structure](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-referral-commission-structure)
    
*   [5.1 Builder’s margin](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-1-builder%E2%80%99s-margin)
    
*   [5.2 Affiliate commission](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-2-affiliate-commission)
    
*   [Example: 3-Level Referral Network](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#example-3-level-referral-network)
    
*   [5.3 affiliate commission rate adjustments](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-3-affiliate-commission-rate-adjustments)
    
*   [5.4.1 Default affiliate commission rate](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-4-1-default-affiliate-commission-rate)
    
*   [5.4.2 Custom affiliate commission rate](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-4-2-custom-affiliate-commission-rate)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#1-overview)

1\. Overview
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly adds a brand new affiliate programme that allows DEXs and their affiliates to configure the percentage of referral commission share to foster the growth of their trading platform. The core model is a funneled and multilevel referral system:

*   Builders work with their direct affiliates to grow their trading platform, with attractive referral commissions for the direct affiliates to build their own multilevel referral networks.
*   Affiliates higher up the hierarchy have the flexibility to set higher commission sharing ratios. This allows affiliates to distribute a portion of their commissions to referees, based on their commission sharing ratio, thus creating a multilevel chain of referrals.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#2-prerequisites)

2\. Prerequisites
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Only DEXs with a **valid builder ID** can enable the affiliate program. This applies to all DEXs built with SDK, API, and Orderly One. For Orderly One DEXs specifically, they must first graduate to obtain a builder ID before they are eligible to enable the affiliate program.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#3-new-vs-legacy-affiliate-programs)

3\. New vs. legacy affiliate programs
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The key differences between the legacy and new affiliate programs are summarized below:

| Details | Legacy | New |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Referral network** | **Single-level**, only direct referrals | **Multilevel** (up to 15 levels), direct and indirect referrals |
| **Referral commission** | Affiliates earn commissions from **direct referees only** | Affiliates earn commissions from both **direct and indirect referees** down the referral chain, with additional 10% bonus from direct referees |
| **L1L\_1L1​ affiliate’s commission rate** | Up to 100%, decided by builder | Up to 90%, decided by builder |
| **Referral code creation** | Created primarily by the builder or qualified affiliates upon meeting the applicable trading volume requirements. | Qualified affiliates can create codes after meeting the required trading volume (where applicable). Affiliates may only create one unique referral code. |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#4-coexistence-of-legacy-affiliate-program)

4\. Coexistence of legacy affiliate program
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This new affiliate program is a multilevel referral system which supersedes the legacy affiliate program. Existing builders who have enabled the legacy affiliate program could choose to keep the legacy system or upgrade to the new one.

1.  **For new builders or existing builders who did not enable the legacy affiliate program:**
    *   The new system will take place when the builders enable the affiliate program.
    *   The legacy system is not available.
2.  **For existing builders who enabled the legacy affiliate program, they can either:**
    *   Keep the legacy affiliate program; or
    *   Upgrade to the new affiliate program which incorporates a multilevel referral structure. Please note that the upgrade is irreversible. All legacy referral codes remain valid and functional after the upgrade, but no legacy referral codes can be further created.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-referral-commission-structure)

5\. Referral commission structure
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Builders have the flexibility to determine the referral commission rate allocated to L1L\_1L1​ affiliates. These L1L\_1L1​ affiliates are the initial referrers who establish a multilevel network and do not report to any referrer above them.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-1-builder%E2%80%99s-margin)

5.1 Builder’s margin

Affiliates receive their referral commissions from the builder fees shared by builders. This commission is paid out for every trade executed on the DEX. (Fee)Builder\=(Trading fee)N−(Base fee)Builder\\text{(Fee)}\_\\text{Builder} = \\text{(Trading fee)}\_N - \\text{(Base fee)}\_\\text{Builder}(Fee)Builder​\=(Trading fee)N​−(Base fee)Builder​ The builder’s margin from each trade within the multilevel referral network is determined after allocating a referral commission rate X1X\_1X1​ to the L1L\_1L1​ affiliates. (Margin)Builder\=(Fee)Builder×(0.9−X1)\=\[(Trading fee)N−(Base fee)Builder\]×(0.9−X1)\\text{(Margin)}\_\\text{Builder} = \\text{(Fee)}\_\\text{Builder} \\times (0.9 - X\_1) = \\big\[\\text{(Trading fee)}\_N - \\text{(Base fee)}\_\\text{Builder}\\big\] \\times (0.9 - X\_1)(Margin)Builder​\=(Fee)Builder​×(0.9−X1​)\=\[(Trading fee)N​−(Base fee)Builder​\]×(0.9−X1​) where (Base fee)Builder\\text{(Base fee)}\_\\text{Builder}(Base fee)Builder​ depends on the builder fee tier.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-2-affiliate-commission)

5.2 Affiliate commission

L1L\_1L1​ affiliates may distribute a portion of their commission to their direct referees (L2L\_2L2​). This process can be extended further, as L2L\_2L2​ affiliates can further sub-divide their allocation to their direct referees (L3L\_3L3​), establishing a multilevel structure. This multilevel referral network can extend up to a total of 15 levels. For every trade executed by a user (LN)(L\_N)(LN​) on the DEX, the affiliates within the multilevel referral network can earn the following commission:

| Role | Affiliate commission rate | Affiliate commission |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1L\_1L1​ affiliate** | (X1−X2)(X\_1 - X\_2)(X1​−X2​) where X2∈\[0,X1\]X\_2 \\in \[0, X\_1\]X2​∈\[0,X1​\] | (X1−X2)×FeeBuilder(X\_1 - X\_2) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X1​−X2​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **L2L\_2L2​ affiliate** | (X2−X3)(X\_2 - X\_3)(X2​−X3​) where X3∈\[0,X2\]X\_3 \\in \[0, X\_2\]X3​∈\[0,X2​\] | (X2−X3)×FeeBuilder(X\_2 - X\_3) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X2​−X3​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **L3L\_3L3​ affiliate** | (X3−X4)(X\_3 - X\_4)(X3​−X4​) where X4∈\[0,X3\]X\_4 \\in \[0, X\_3\]X4​∈\[0,X3​\] | (X3−X4)×FeeBuilder(X\_3 - X\_4) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X3​−X4​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **…** | …   | …   |
| **LN−1L\_{N-1}LN−1​ user (direct referrer)** | XN−1+10%X\_{N-1} + 10\\%XN−1​+10% | \[XN−1+10%\]×FeeBuilder\[X\_{N-1} + 10\\%\] \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}\[XN−1​+10%\]×FeeBuilder​ — Direct referrer earns full rate plus 10% bonus |

> Note: Direct referrers earn a 10% bonus referral commission rate from trades executed by their direct referees.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#example-3-level-referral-network)

Example: 3-Level Referral Network

Consider a trade where Builder revenue for a given trade = $100, with the following commission rates:

*   X1\=40%X\_1 = 40\\%X1​\=40% (L1L\_1L1​ affiliate rate set by builder)
*   X2\=25%X\_2 = 25\\%X2​\=25% (L2L\_2L2​ rate set by L1L\_1L1​)
*   X3\=15%X\_3 = 15\\%X3​\=15% (L3L\_3L3​ rate set by L2L\_2L2​)

**Commission distribution for $100 Builder revenue:**

| Role | Calculation | Commission |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1L\_1L1​ affiliate | (40%−25%)×100(40\\% - 25\\%) \\times 100(40%−25%)×100 | **$15** |
| L2L\_2L2​ affiliate | (25%−15%)×100(25\\% - 15\\%) \\times 100(25%−15%)×100 | **$10** |
| L3L\_3L3​ affiliate (direct referrer) | (15%+10%)×100(15\\% + 10\\%) \\times 100(15%+10%)×100 | **$25** |
| **Total distributed** |     | **$50** |

In this example, when L4L\_4L4​ (the trader) executes a trade, L3L\_3L3​ earns their full 15%15\\%15% rate plus the 10%10\\%10% bonus, totaling 25.The25\. The 25.TheL\_4$ trader does not earn commission from their own trade.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-3-affiliate-commission-rate-adjustments)

5.3 L1L\_1L1​ affiliate commission rate adjustments

Admin console: [https://admin.orderly.network/referral](https://admin.orderly.network/referral)

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-4-1-default-affiliate-commission-rate)

5.4.1 Default affiliate commission rate

Builders can configure a default L1L\_1L1​ commission rate that serves as the default for users claiming referral codes as L1L\_1L1​ affiliates. The builders can customize the commission rate of these L1L\_1L1​ affiliates, subject to the following rules:

*   If no referral codes have been claimed, the builder can freely adjust the default commission rate up or down.
*   Once referral codes are claimed, the builder may only increase the default commission rate, applying changes to all direct referees on that rate. This restriction protects existing referrals and prevents unfavourable downstream changes.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide#5-4-2-custom-affiliate-commission-rate)

5.4.2 Custom affiliate commission rate

Builders may customize a higher affiliate commission rate to overwrite the default rate for any L1L\_1L1​ affiliates, but cannot decrease it.

[Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Affiliate's Quick Guide)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide)
[Affiliate & Referral Program (Legacy)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/affiliate-referral-program)

\[evm\] react components SDK

Overview

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

On this page

*   [Storybook](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#storybook)
    
*   [Features](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#features)
    
*   [Architecture](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#architecture)
    
*   [Compatibility](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#compatibility)
    

![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/sdks/react/sdk-cover.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=0588af0164cc9a1492bb001ed414a6fa) The Orderly Components SDK provides a set of components needed to build a DEX UI, allowing you to quickly build a DEX on top of Orderly. It is designed modularly and consists of various npm packages. Based on your needs you have to install the respective packages:

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `@orderly.network/ui` | Base UI component library. |
| `@orderly.network/ui-scaffold` | Page layout components (e.g., headers, footers, sidebars). |
| `@orderly.network/react-app` | Providers for any React app built with Orderly components. |
| `@orderly.network/trading` | Trading page components. |
| `@orderly.network/portfolio` | Personal portfolio dashboard components. |
| `@orderly.network/trading-rewards` |     |
| `@orderly.network/affiliate` |     |
| `@orderly.network/markets` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-connector` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-chain-selector` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-order-entry` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-orders` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-positions` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-tpsl` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-transfer` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-share` |     |
| `@orderly.network/ui-leverage` |     |
| `@orderly.network/wallet-connector` |     |
| `@orderly.network/default-evm-adapter` |     |
| `@orderly.network/default-solana-adapter` |     |
| `@orderly.network/web3-provider-ethers` |     |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#storybook)

Storybook
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can check out our [Storybook](https://storybook.orderly.network/)
, which has all components included.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#features)

Features
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Provides feature-complete React components, allowing you to build a DEX with little to no code
*   Full compatibility on different viewports
*   Modular design and layered, allowing you to customize according to your needs
*   i18n support
*   Customizable themes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#architecture)

Architecture
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/sdks/react/orderly-architecture.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=83ab25ba1f39ab4408918789d45b237a) `@orderly.network/react` has three layers, all of which can be used individually.

*   UI components: Provides basic UI components, such as buttons, input forms
*   Block components: Provides DEX related components, such as symbol chooser, orderbook, trade history components
*   Page: Provides fully functional pages, such as the trading page

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview#compatibility)

Compatibility
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Compatible with all modern browsers and IE11 or above

[Migrate to SDK v2](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/migrate)
[Getting started](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started)

Audits
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly has been audited by multiple security firms, including [Halborn](https://www.halborn.com/)
, [Zellic](https://www.zellic.io/)
, [Guardian Audits](https://guardianaudits.com/)
, and several independent consultants. All audit reports can be found on our [GitHub](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/Audits)
 Trusted and incubated by WOO Network

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/security#governance)

Governance
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Updates to the protocol are governed and approved by a multi-sig approver mechanism with signers who have the deepest interest in the success of Orderly

*   [How to Calculate Weight?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#how-to-calculate-weight)
    
*   [Weight & Discount Collateral Factor](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#weight-%26-discount-collateral-factor)

*   [Free Collateral](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#free-collateral)
    
*   [Total Account Value](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#total-account-value)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#collaterals)

Collaterals
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#max-deposits-global)

Max Deposits (Global)

These values represent the maximum amount of each collateral type (USDT or ETH) that can be deposited across all users on a global scale for the specified blockchain network (Arbitrum, Ethereum, or Base).

| Collateral | Chain | Max Collateral (global) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| USDT | Arbitrum | 2,000,000 |
| ETH | Arbitrum | 1,000 |
| USDT | Ethereum | 3,000,000 |
| ETH | Ethereum | 500 |
| ETH | Base | 500 |
| USDT | BSC | 4,000,000 |
| YUSD | Ethereum | 500,000 |
| YUSD | BSC | 500,000 |
| SOL | SOL | 40,000 |
| USDT | SOL | 1,500,000 |
| BNB | BSC | 10,000 |
| WBTC | Ethereum | 80  |
| USD1 | Ethereum | 1,000,000 |
| USD1 | BSC | 1,000,000 |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#max-deposits-user)

Max Deposits (User)

| Collateral | Max Collateral |
| --- | --- |
| USDT | 500,000 |
| ETH | 100 |
| SOL | 2,000 |
| BNB | 500 |
| YUSD | 50,000 |
| WBTC | 5   |
| USD1 | 500,000 |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#what-is-loan-to-value-ltv-)

What is Loan-to-value (LTV)?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Loan-to-Value (LTV) is a key risk metric that measures the ratio of your negative USDC value, which includes your negative balance (`USDC_balance`) and unrealized PnL (`upnl`), to your collateral’s value. On Orderly, LTV replaces traditional credit scores to determine your borrowing power in our asset-backed lending system. A lower LTV signifies a safer loan. By maintaining a healthy LTV, you can maximize the loan you can access while enhancing the safety of your position against market volatility. This empowers you to borrow with confidence. Your collateral is held securely by Orderly until the loan is repaid, providing a clear and powerful way for you to leverage your holdings. ![LTV](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy/images/multi-collateral/ltv.jpg?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy&q=85&s=b52d01e98dd0c261b959a0335dc8b324)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#how-to-calculate-ltv)

How to calculate LTV

    LTV = ( abs(min(USDC Balance, 0)) + abs(min(upnl, 0)) ) / [ sum( max(Collateral Quantity, 0) × Index Price × Weight ) + max(upnl, 0) ]
    

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| USDC Balance | The total absolute USDC balance that has been borrowed |
| min(upnl, 0) | Unrealized losses from cross-margin positions |
| Collateral Quantity | The quantity of a specific asset you hold as collateral |
| Index Price | The current market price of the collateral |
| Weight | The collateral weight (refer to Collateral Ratio section) |
| max(upnl, 0) | Unrealized profits from cross-margin positions |

The acceptable range of LTV ratio remains below 95%. Auto conversion of collateral will be triggered if the LTV ratio reaches 95% and above.

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#collateral-ratio)

Collateral Ratio
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#what-is-collateral-ratio)

What is Collateral Ratio?

The Collateral Ratio on Orderly is the value at which we recognize your asset deposited as collateral, expressed as a percentage. A higher ratio provides a greater safety buffer against market fluctuations and lowers the risk of liquidation. Orderly calculates this by assigning asset-specific “weights,” where more volatile assets contribute less to the ratio than stable assets. The Collateral Ratio serves as the primary indicator of an account’s financial health on the platform. ![Collateral Ratio](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy/images/multi-collateral/collateral.jpg?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy&q=85&s=608e3d61824f8a4c3463786ed22187ed)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#how-to-calculate-weight)

How to Calculate Weight?

    Weight = min (Base Weight, K / (1 + DCF * abs(Collateral Quantity * Index Price )^(4/5)))
    

where:

*   `DCF_i` = Discount Collateral Factor
*   `K` = 1.2

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Base Weight | The standard or default collateral weight for a specific asset |
| K   | A constant system parameter set at 1.2 unless otherwise stated |
| DCF | Discount collateral factor determining weight sensitivity to position size |
| Collateral Quantity | Quantity of the collateral deposited |
| Index Price | Current market price of the collateral |
| ^(⅘) | Smoothes impact of large position size |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#weight-&-discount-collateral-factor)

Weight & Discount Collateral Factor

Weight and DCF will be determined based on the table below:

| Collateral | base\_weight | DCF\_i |
| --- | --- | --- |
| USDT | 1   | 0.0000015 |
| ETH | 0.8 | 0.000007 |
| YUSD | 0.9 | 0.0000045 |
| SOL | 0.8 | 0.000007 |
| BNB | 0.8 | 0.000007 |
| WBTC | 0.8 | 0.000007 |
| USD1 | 0.98 | 0.0000045 |

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#collateral-value)

Collateral Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    Collateral Value = min(Collateral Quantity , Collateral Cap) * Weight * Index Price
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#total-collateral-value)

Total Collateral Value

    Total Collateral Value = sum(Collateral Value) + upnl
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#free-collateral)

Free Collateral

    Free Collateral = Total Collateral Value - Total Initial Margin With Orders
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#total-account-value)

Total Account Value

    Total Account Value = sum(Collateral Quantity * Index Price) + upnl
    

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#auto-conversion-haircuts)

Auto Conversion (Haircuts)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If your Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio reaches the 95% threshold or a negative USDC value equivalent to -11,000, the system will automatically convert a necessary portion of your collateral into USDC. This action immediately reduces your LTV to a safer level, helping to preserve your account. This is a protective measure to manage risk. As always, remember that the lower your LTV ratio, the more secure your position is from this type of automated deleveraging. The fee for auto conversion of each collateral will follow the table below:

| Collateral | Auto Conversion Fee (Haircuts) |
| --- | --- |
| USDT | 0.025% |
| ETH | 3.5% |
| YUSD | 0.035% |
| SOL | 3.5% |
| BNB | 3.5% |
| WBTC | 3.5% |
| USD1 | 0.035% |

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#manual-conversion)

Manual Conversion
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We recommend monitoring your LTV ratio to ensure it stays below the 95% and/or -11,000 USDC threshold. We permit manual conversion of collateral to assist with this maintenance via on-chain swaps. Each collateral’s minimum and maximum swap amount can be found in the table below: ![Convert Rate](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy/images/multi-collateral/convert.jpg?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=97zJvIeyjbSLcoyy&q=85&s=7f24e813ef5163444bdc94517e37499d)

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#minimum-swap-collateral-required)

Minimum Swap Collateral Required

| Collateral | Arbitrum & Base | Ethereum | Solana | BSC |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| USDT | 50  | 500 | 50  | 50  |
| ETH | 0.02 | 0.2 | —   | —   |
| YUSD | —   | 500 | —   | 50  |
| SOL | —   | —   | 2   | —   |
| BNB | —   | —   | —   | 0.5 |
| WBTC | —   | 0.005 | —   | —   |
| USD1 | —   | 500 | —   | 500 |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/multi-collateral#maximum-swap-collateral-threshold)

Maximum Swap Collateral Threshold

| Collateral | Arbitrum & Base | Ethereum | Solana | BSC |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| USDT | 500,000 | 1,000,000 | 500,000 | 500,000 |
| ETH | 100 | 500 | —   | —   |
| YUSD | —   | 50,000 | —   | 50,000 |
| SOL | —   | —   | 500 | —   |
| BNB | —   | —   | —   | 500 |
| WBTC | —   | 5   | —   | —   |
| USD1 | —   | 10,000 | —   | 10,000 |

[Supported Chains](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/supported-chains)
[Builders](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders)

# Integration Checklist - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Builder quickstart

Integration Checklist

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Phase 1: Planning & Setup](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-1-planning-%26-setup)
    
*   [Phase 2: Core Implementation](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-2-core-implementation)
    
*   [Phase 3: Advanced Features & Optimization](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-3-advanced-features-%26-optimization)
    
*   [Phase 4: Testing & Launch Preparation](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-4-testing-%26-launch-preparation)
    

Use this checklist to plan, implement, and launch a builder integration on Orderly. The goal is to cover the required user flows first, then add the operational pieces needed for a stable launch.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-1-planning-&-setup)

Phase 1: Planning & Setup
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before writing code, align the product scope and technical setup with the Orderly team.

*   **Choose an integration path:** Pick the stack that matches your product and team.
    *   [React Hooks](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/overview)
        
    *   [React UI components](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview)
        
    *   [Python connector](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-connector-python)
        
    *   Direct API access through the [EVM API docs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction)
        
*   **Set up builder identity:** Start from [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
    . If you are using Orderly One, go to [https://dex.orderly.network/](https://dex.orderly.network/)
     to create your builder ID and continue onboarding.
*   **Review system design:** Read the [Building on Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
    , [Smart Contract Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/overview)
    , and [Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/addresses)
     pages.
*   **Define launch scope:** Confirm your core user flows, testnet plan, support process, and go-to-market milestones before development starts.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-2-core-implementation)

Phase 2: Core Implementation
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implement the flows that every trading integration needs.

*   **[Account registration](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/accounts)
    :** Let users create and activate their Orderly account.
*   **[Wallet authentication and Orderly Keys](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication)
    :** Set up authentication for signed API access and trading actions.
*   **[Deposit and withdrawal](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/withdrawal-deposit)
    :** Support funding and withdrawal flows from supported chains.
*   **[Order management](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/order-management)
    :** Implement order placement, cancellation, and order state handling.
*   **Market and symbol data:** Integrate market metadata, prices, orderbook data, and trade updates from the [WebSocket API](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction)
     and relevant REST endpoints such as [available symbols](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-available-symbols)
    .

**Reduce-Only Orders:** Use the `reduce-only` flag for orders intended to close or reduce a position. This helps avoid cases where a closing order could otherwise increase Initial Margin Requirements (IMR) and fail because the account does not have enough margin.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-3-advanced-features-&-optimization)

Phase 3: Advanced Features & Optimization
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add the pieces that make the integration operationally complete.

*   **[Settle PnL](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl)
    :** Handle realized PnL settlement as part of the account lifecycle.
*   **[Custom fees](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)
    :** Configure default builder fees and any user-level fee logic you need.
*   **User data surfaces:** Display balances, positions, trade history, and account data using endpoints such as [Get account information](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-account-information)
    .
*   **Regulatory controls:** Implement the geo-blocking and compliance checks required for your product and jurisdiction.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist#phase-4-testing-&-launch-preparation)

Phase 4: Testing & Launch Preparation
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Verify the integration end to end before going live.

*   **Validate on testnet:** Run through registration, authentication, deposits, withdrawals, trading, and settlement on testnet before launch.
*   **Prepare for maintenance:** Use the [system maintenance status endpoint](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-system-maintenance-status)
    , ask the Orderly team for builder Telegram channel access, and make sure your UI handles maintenance windows clearly, including any order actions that remain available.
*   **Review support and launch readiness:** Make sure your team has clear ownership for user support, incident communication, and launch coordination with Orderly.

During system maintenance, market data and user positions will be unavailable. Open positions remain open and can still be liquidated if market conditions change after trading resumes.

[Smart Contract Addresses](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/addresses)
[Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)

⌘I

Classification

Release Classification

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Patch Release (Low Severity)](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#patch-release-low-severity)
    
*   [Minor Release (Moderate Severity)](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#minor-release-moderate-severity)
    
*   [Major Release (High Severity)](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#major-release-high-severity)
    
*   [Critical Release (Urgent Severity)](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#critical-release-urgent-severity)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#patch-release-low-severity)

Patch Release (Low Severity)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Definition:** Minor updates, typically involving bug fixes and small enhancements that do not impact the existing functionality significantly. **Characteristics:** No new major features. No breaking changes. Does not require user intervention.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#minor-release-moderate-severity)

Minor Release (Moderate Severity)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Definition:** Introduces new features or significant improvements to existing features, but does not fundamentally change the product. **Characteristics:** May include new features, enhancements, and performance improvements. Backward compatible; no breaking changes to existing APIs or core functionality.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#major-release-high-severity)

Major Release (High Severity)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Definition: Major changes that might include new features, substantial revisions, or updates that significantly modify the product’s functionality. **Characteristics:** Introduction of significant new features or major changes to existing ones. Possible backward incompatibilities or changes requiring user adaptation.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/classification#critical-release-urgent-severity)

Critical Release (Urgent Severity)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Definition: Emergency releases to fix critical bugs or security vulnerabilities. These releases are often unplanned but necessary for the smooth functioning and security of the product. **Characteristics:** Addresses critical bugs or security issues. Requires immediate deployment to prevent potential data loss, security breaches, or significant system downtime.

[TS SDK](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/sdk)

⌘I

# Supplemental Terms for Community Strategy Vault - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/strategy-vault/vault/public/supplemental-terms#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)
