Overview

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)

* * *

`@orderly.network/core` The core library is the foundational SDK used by hooks SDK and has the following functionalities:

*   Helpers for registration, Orderly Key generation and settle pnl
*   Key stores that are mainly used from hooks
*   Some helper fnuctions
*   Account object
*   KeyPair classes to sign messages

Technical overview is [here](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_core)
 and [here](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_core.utils)

Please refer to our [Liquidations page](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations)
 for detailed information about how liquidations work. **Common Issue Description:** Positions are liquidated unexpectedly, often reported during high volatility. **Troubleshooting Steps:**

*   Review margin requirements and ensure they are met before trading.
*   Check for any rapid price movements that might trigger liquidation.
*   Verify the liquidation price and compare it with market data to confirm accuracy.

[Stuck/delayed deposits](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/deposit-issues)

Affiliate Onboarding Guide

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)

On this page

*   [Website Domain Requirements](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#website-domain-requirements)
    
*   [Example Implementations](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#example-implementations)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#website-domain-requirements)

Website Domain Requirements
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We require two subdomains - one for mainnet and one for testnet. Here are some examples:

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#example-implementations)

Example Implementations

*   What Exchange
    *   Mainnet: `https://trade.what.exchange/`
    *   Testnet: `https://staging.what.exchange/`
*   WooFi Pro
    *   Mainnet: `https://pro.woofi.com/`
    *   Testnet: `https://testnet-pro.woofi.com/`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#tradingview-license)

TradingView License
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our platform uses TradingView for price charts, which requires a commercial license. You can obtain one at: [TradingView Advanced Charts](https://www.tradingview.com/advanced-charts/)
 When applying for the license, we can help with the following application details:

*   **Website URL**: Use your purchased domain
*   **Github Profile**: You will have to create a personal Github account, so that they can give you access to their library. Even though you will not need this access, because we already took care of it, it is required for the application process.
*   **Company Profile**: “Crypto Exchange”
*   **Own Data Feed**: Yes
*   **Reason for Request**: Mention that this is for a perpetual futures decentralized exchange

Please reach out to us if you need any assistance with the application process.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#builder-details-required)

Builder Details Required
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Builder Name**: xxxx
2.  **Builder ID**: xxxx
3.  **Admin Wallet Address**: xxxx
    *   This address will be used for the login admin tool
    *   For EVM addresses: Register at [Builder Registration Portal](https://orderlynetwork.github.io/broker-registration/)
        
    *   For SOL addresses: Please contact our team
4.  **Builder Fee Settings** (adjustable later)
    *   Default Taker Fee: 0.06%
    *   Default Maker Fee: 0.03%
    *   Note: Orderly charges a 0.03% taker fee and no maker fee. You earn the difference (e.g., 0.06% - 0.03% = 0.03% taker fee). [Learn more about our fee structure](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)
        .

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#design-requirements)

Design Requirements
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Logo Files**
    *   Primary Logo: Wide version (preferably SVG, alternatively PNG/WebP)
    *   Secondary Logo: Square version (preferably SVG, alternatively PNG/WebP)
    *   Favicon: ICO or PNG format for browser tab icon
2.  **CSS Customization**
    1.  Use our [Theme Builder](https://storybook.orderly.network/?path=/story/package-trading-tradingpage--page&globals=theme:custom)
         to experiment with colors, fonts, and styles
    2.  Customize colors with live preview

![Theme Builder Interface](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/build-on-omnichain/theme-builder.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=3670739b4efcd4cf5241cd4c46a5bc17)

3.  Copy the generated CSS code and provide it to our team

![Theme Builder CSS export](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/build-on-omnichain/theme-builder-css.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=15a13b3c03d68a2ade5f35a8277f2a8d)

We can alternatively do the CSS changes ourselves.

3.  **PNL Share Posters**
    *   We provide [default background templates](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/broker-template/tree/main/public/pnl)
         for PNL sharing functionality
    *   Please review these and let us know if you’d like to customize them for your platform
    *   You can provide your own poster designs that match your brand identity

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding#deployment)

Deployment
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We will handle the deployment process for you through Vercel. Our team will:

1.  Set up a test deployment for your review
2.  Make any necessary adjustments based on your feedback
3.  Deploy to production once everything is approved

This ensures a smooth transition from development to production while maintaining the highest quality standards.

[Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)
[Integration FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs)

⌘I

On this page

*   [Common error messages](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/error-response#common-error-messages)
    

Error responses follow the same format as successful responses, with `success` set to `false` and an `errorMsg` field explaining the issue. You can use the `id` field to map the error response with the corresponding request.

    {
      "id": "clientID7",
      "event": "subscribe",
      "success": false,
      "ts": 1614141150601,
      "errorMsg": "invalid symbol PERP_BTC_USDC"
    }
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/error-response#common-error-messages)

Common error messages
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Error message | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `invalid symbol <symbol>` | The requested symbol does not exist |
| `invalid topic <topic>` | The requested topic is not supported |
| `auth failed` | Authentication failed due to invalid credentials |
| `missing required parameter <param>` | A required parameter is missing |

[Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/authentication)
[Request orderbook](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/public/request-orderbook)

Delegate signer

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/delegate_signer \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --data '
    {
      "message": {
        "delegateContract": "0xaddress",
        "brokerId": "woofi_pro",
        "chainId": 421613,
        "timestamp": 1704879369551,
        "registrationNonce": 161111791392,
        "txHash": "0xtxhash"
      },
      "signature": "0x8668447f28534dc1b76566e41f5fcdeb6b131355b9cf428f3fae5c134e9a0fc55642a2771860484651e61043e8ac67a1a6e54031a4c66de44acd77c9d9b281e81b",
      "userAddress": "0xDd3287043493E0a08d2B348397554096728B459c"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989,
      "data": {
        "user_id": 100338,
        "account_id": "0xd890c9b8b86fba7312b919e32bbabd619228b32d59f6d0f68fdf4451a684a654",
        "valid_signer": "0xc6c4c5c41efa74ebc63c675dcf7034e8382f3a42"
      }
    }

POST

Try it

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#body-message)

message

object

required

Show child attributes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#body-signature)

signature

string

required

Signature generated by signing the message object.

Example:

`"0x8668447f28534dc1b76566e41f5fcdeb6b131355b9cf428f3fae5c134e9a0fc55642a2771860484651e61043e8ac67a1a6e54031a4c66de44acd77c9d9b281e81b"`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#body-user-address)

userAddress

string

required

Address of the wallet that signed the message object.

Example:

`"0xDd3287043493E0a08d2B348397554096728B459c"`

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/delegate-signer#response-data)

data

object

Show child attributes

The Orderly SDK has a default theme, but you can also customize the theme yourself.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#default-theme)

Default theme
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#colors)

Colors

The Components SDK uses the following colors:

*   Besides `base`, each color theme contains 3 colors:
    *   `default`: The default shade of the color
    *   `light`: A lighter shade than default
    *   `darken`: A darker shade than default
    *   `contrastText`: Text color, intended to contrast with default
*   `base-contrast` defines color of texts. The library uses transparency to differentiate the importance of texts.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#typography)

Typography

The Components SDK uses Manrope as the default font.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#rounded)

Rounded

| name | size |
| --- | --- |
| default | 6px |
| sm  | 4px |
| lg  | 8px |
| full | 9999px |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#customized-theme)

Customized theme
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#css-variables)

CSS Variables

You can change the color of elements through creating a `theme.css` under the project. Simply add the following into the file, and import it for it to take effect:

theme.css

    :root {
      /**
      It is often used for the color of main elements, such as background color
      */
      --oui-color-base-100: 93 83 123;
      --oui-color-base-200: 81 72 107;
      --oui-color-base-300: 68 61 89;
      --oui-color-base-400: 57 52 74;
      --oui-color-base-500: 51 46 66;
      --oui-color-base-600: 43 38 56;
      --oui-color-base-700: 36 32 47;
      --oui-color-base-800: 29 26 38;
      --oui-color-base-900: 22 20 28;
    
      /**
      primary color, used for buttons, border etc
      */
      --oui-color-primary: 182 79 255;
      --oui-color-primary-light: 208 140 255;
      --oui-color-primary-darken: 152 62 214;
    
      /**
      link color
      */
      --oui-color-link: 182 79 255;
    
    
      --oui-color-secondary: 255 255 255;
      --oui-color-tertiary: 218 218 218;
      --oui-color-quaternary: 218 218 218;
    
      /**
      error color or destructive action elements
      */
      --oui-color-danger: 232 88 175;
      --oui-color-danger-light: 255 103 191;
      --oui-color-danger-darken: 199 68 146;
    
      /**
      success color or constructive action elements
      */
      */
      --oui-color-success: 3 152 134;
      --oui-color-success-light: 0 181 159;
      --oui-color-success-darken: 0 119 105;
    
      /**
      warning color
      */
      --oui-color-warning: 255 207 115;
      --oui-color-warning-light: 229 199 0;
      --oui-color-warning-darken: 229 199 0;
    
      --oui-color-background: 27 32 40;
      --oui-color-background-contrast: 255 255 255;
    
      --oui-color-fill: 36 32 47;
      --oui-color-fill-light: 40 46 58;
    
      --oui-color-popover: 43 38 56;
      --oui-color-popover-foreground: 255 255 255;
    
    
      --oui-color-base-foreground: 255 255 255;
    
      --oui-color-trading-loss: 255 103 194;
      --oui-color-trading-loss-contrast: 40 46 58;
      --oui-color-trading-profit: 0 181 159;
      --oui-color-trading-profit-contrast: 40 46 58;
    
      /* rounded */
      --oui-rounded: 6px;
      --oui-rounded-sm: 4px;
      --oui-rounded-lg: 8px;
      --oui-rounded-full: 9999px;
    
      --oui-button-shadow: none;
    
      --oui-font-size-base: 16px;
    
      --oui-color-divider: 42 46 52;
    
      --oui-font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif;
    }
    

CSS variables must be defined without color space function. See the [Tailwind CSS documentation](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/text-color#changing-the-opacity)
 for more information.

Don’t include the color space function or opacity modifiers won’t work

    /* Using modern `rgb` */
    --oui-color-primary: rgb(182 79 255);
    --oui-color-primary-light: rgb(208 140 255);
    --oui-color-primary-darken: rgb(152 62 214);
    

Define your CSS variables as channels with no color space function

    --oui-color-primary: 182 79 255;
    --oui-color-primary-light: 208 140 255;
    --oui-color-primary-darken: 152 62 214;
    

**You can use the [Theme tool](https://sdk.orderly.network/theme)
 that we provide to quickly create your own theme!**

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#style-override)

Style override

The Components SDK provides the following two ways to override the style of components:

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#class)

class

To override the style of a certain class. For example, if we want to modify the style of a `button` component, we can achieve this by selecting the element using the `class` selector. For instance, to change the color of the `button` component:

    /* 
    general buttons
    */
    .oui-button {
      background-color: #000;
      color: #fff;
    }
    /* 
    primary buttons
    */
    .oui-button-primary {
      background-color: #000;
      color: #fff;
    }
    

If you want to change the `disabled` status of the `button` component:

    .oui-button-disabled {
      background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3);
      color: #999;
    }
    

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#id)

id

If you want to modify the style of a specific component, you can use the id selector to define the component’s style. For example, if you want to modify the style of the “Create Order” button:

    #orderly-order-entry-confirm-button {
      background-color: #000;
      color: #fff;
    }
    

**Supported `id`s are as below:**

| id  | module | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| orderly-deposit-confirm-button | deposit | Deposit confirm button |
| orderly-withdraw-confirm-button | withdraw | Withdraw confirm button |
| orderly-assets-margin-deposit-button | assets | Deposit button |
| orderly-assets-margin-withdraw-button | assets | Withdraw button |
| orderly-order-entry-confirm-button | order | Order entry - confirm button |
| orderly-position-cell-limit-close-button | positions | Position - limit close button |
| orderly-position-cell-mark-close-button | positions | Position - market close button |
| orderly-pending-cell-edit-button | pending orders | Edit pending order button |
| orderly-pending-cell-cancel-button | pending orders | Cancel pending order button |
| orderly-bottom-nav-bar-connect-button | bottom-nav-bar | bottom bar connect wallet button |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#appicons)

appIcons
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/sdks/react/replace-logo.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=c304847cfd57530ad8465e7fb4cd047d) You can add your logo to the indicated position in the diagram above using the following method:

    import { TradingPage, OrderlyAppProvider } from "@orderly.network/react";
    
    export default function Trading() {
      return (
        <OrderlyAppProvider
          networkId="testnet"
          brokerId="<Your broker id>"
          appIcons={{
            main: {
              img: "<main logo src>"
            },
            secondary: {
              img: "<secondary log src>"
            }
          }}
        >
          {/* Page Component */}
        </OrderlyAppProvider>
      );
    }
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#main)

main

The logo for the desktop version, it will be displayed in the top left corner of the page.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#img)

img

*   Type: `string`
*   Required: false

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#component)

component

*   Type: `ReactNode`
*   Required: false

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#classname)

className

*   Type: `string`
*   Required: false

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#secondary)

secondary

The logo for the mobile web version, it will be displayed in the top right corner of the page and in the top left corner of the bottomSheet component.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#img-2)

img

*   Type: `string`
*   Required: false

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#component-2)

component

*   Type: `ReactNode`
*   Required: false

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming#classname-2)

className

*   Type: `string`
*   Required: false

# Orderly Distributor Program - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#content-area)

Builder programs

Orderly Distributor Program

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)

On this page

*   [TL;DR](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#tldr)
    
*   [1\. Participant Profile](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#1-participant-profile)
    
*   [2\. Onboarding & Binding Rules](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#2-onboarding-%26-binding-rules)
    
*   [The 3-Step Setup](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-3-step-setup)
    
*   [Binding Rules (Guardrails)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#binding-rules-guardrails)
    
*   [3\. Orderly Tiering Programme](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#3-orderly-tiering-programme)
    
*   [Tiers & Privileges](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#tiers-%26-privileges)
    
*   [The Volume Formula](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-volume-formula)
    
*   [4\. Revenue Share Mechanics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#4-revenue-share-mechanics)
    
*   [The Core Equation](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-core-equation)
    
*   [5\. Settlement & Distribution](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#5-settlement-%26-distribution)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#tldr)

TL;DR
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Orderly Distributor Program is the core growth engine for the Orderly One ecosystem. It incentivizes **Distributors** to onboard new Builders (DEXs) through a tiered revenue-sharing model.

*   **Participant Role:** Anyone (EOA) or Builder Owners can participate as a Distributor.
*   **Profit Mechanism:** Revenue is generated via the “Fee Spread” between the Distributor’s tier and their Invitee’s tier.
*   **Scale Advantage:** Distributors level up using the aggregate volume of their entire invitee network plus their own volume.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#1-participant-profile)

1\. Participant Profile
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Feature | Distributor (EOA / Builder Owner) |
| --- | --- |
| **Role** | General users, Affiliates, or DEX Owners |
| **Revenue Stream** | Referral share (Spread between tiers) |
| **Revenue Settlement** | Trading account (EOA) or Builder admin account |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#2-onboarding-&-binding-rules)

2\. Onboarding & Binding Rules
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-3-step-setup)

The 3-Step Setup

Access the portal at [dex.orderly.network/distributor/](https://dex.orderly.network/distributor/)
 to begin:

1.  **Connect:** Link an EOA wallet via Ethereum, Arbitrum One, or Base.
2.  **Activate:** Complete key creation to initialize the Distributor profile.
3.  **Share:** Use unique distributor codes or URLs to bind invitees.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#binding-rules-guardrails)

Binding Rules (Guardrails)

*   **Unidirectional:** An invitee can only be bound to a single distributor.
*   **Non-Reciprocal:** Cross-referral (e.g., A invites B, B invites A) is strictly prohibited.
*   **Timing:** Binding must be established **at or before** the invitee’s DEX graduation.
*   **Immutability:** Once established, the binding relationship cannot be modified.
*   **Account Type Lock:** An EOA account registered as a Distributor cannot be converted into a Builder Admin account later.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#3-orderly-tiering-programme)

3\. Orderly Tiering Programme
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A distributor’s tier is determined by their **30-day Aggregate Volume** and **$ORDER Staking**.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#tiers-&-privileges)

Tiers & Privileges

| Tier | 30d Aggregate Volume | $ORDER Staked | Base Taker Fee | Tier Assignment Privilege |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Public** | ≥ $0 | ≥ 0 | 3.00 bps | \-  |
| **Silver** | ≥ $30M | ≥ 100K | 2.75 bps | \-  |
| **Gold** | ≥ $90M | ≥ 250K | 2.50 bps | Can assign Silver / Public |
| **Platinum** | ≥ $1B | ≥ 2M | 2.00 bps | Can assign Gold & below |
| **Diamond** | ≥ $10B | ≥ 7M | 1.00 bps | Can assign Platinum & below |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-volume-formula)

The Volume Formula

30d Aggregate Vol.\=Distributor Personal Vol.+∑(All Graduated Invitees’ Vol.)\\text{30d Aggregate Vol.} = \\text{Distributor Personal Vol.} + \\sum (\\text{All Graduated Invitees' Vol.})30d Aggregate Vol.\=Distributor Personal Vol.+∑(All Graduated Invitees’ Vol.) Reference: [Orderly Builder Programme](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees#builder-staking-programme)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#4-revenue-share-mechanics)

4\. Revenue Share Mechanics
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#the-core-equation)

The Core Equation

Distributors earn the “spread” between the invitee’s base fee and their own. **Distributor Margin:** Margin\=max⁡(0.1 bps,Invitee Base Fee−Distributor Base Fee)Margin = \\max(0.1 \\text{ bps}, \\text{Invitee Base Fee} - \\text{Distributor Base Fee})Margin\=max(0.1 bps,Invitee Base Fee−Distributor Base Fee) _(A guaranteed 0.1 bps minimum margin applies to Taker orders even if tiers are identical)._

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/vanguard-distributor-program#5-settlement-&-distribution)

5\. Settlement & Distribution
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Frequency:** Daily settlement at **00:00 UTC**.
*   **Automatic Distribution:**
    *   **Builder Owners:** Revenue is credited to the **Builder Admin Account**
    *   **General EOA:** Revenue is credited to the **Distributor**

[Supplemental Terms for DEXes](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-one/supplemental-terms-for-dexes)
[FAQ](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/faqs)

⌘I

Navigation

Core integration flows

User Flow Walkthrough

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Accounts / Access Keys](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#accounts-%2F-access-keys)
    
*   [Account Structure](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-structure)
    
*   [Account ID](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-id)
    
*   [Authorization](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#authorization)
    
*   [Access Key (Orderly Key)](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#access-key-orderly-key)
    
*   [Account Registration](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-registration)
    
*   [Add Access Key](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#add-access-key)
    
*   [Deposits / Withdrawals](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#deposits-%2F-withdrawals)
    
*   [Deposit](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#deposit)
    
*   [Withdrawal](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#withdrawal)
    
*   [Settle PnL](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#settle-pnl)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#accounts-/-access-keys)

Accounts / Access Keys
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-structure)

Account Structure

A wallet owner can register an account on Orderly for each supported builder. Each wallet owner can thus have multiple accounts (not interlinked with each other) registered with Orderly.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-id)

Account ID

Each account is identified by an account ID, calculated as follows:

1.  Convert the wallet address to bytes
2.  Convert the keccak256 hash of the `brokerId` string to bytes
3.  Concatenate the results of (1) and (2)
4.  Take the keccak256 hash of (3) and convert back to a hex string

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#authorization)

Authorization

Orderly uses the EIP-712 standard to authenticate wallets and accounts for critical actions, such as:

*   Account creation
*   Access key (Orderly Key) addition
*   Withdrawal requests

For these actions, wallet owners must sign specific messages from their wallet and pass the signature through the REST APIs. The following sections describe the steps needed to complete each action. Depositing into Orderly is a direct on-chain transaction that also requires signing from the wallet owner. For other actions, such as calling trading-related RESTful APIs, only the Orderly Key is required.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#access-key-orderly-key)

Access Key (Orderly Key)

Orderly uses the ed25519 elliptic curve standard for access keys. Users can generate access keys using any public ed25519 library and then add them to any account through a REST API. Access keys can have different scopes:

*   **read** — access all private read-only APIs
*   **trading** — in addition to read access, access all order APIs (create order, batch create order, edit order, cancel order, cancel order by client\_order\_id, cancel orders in bulk, batch cancel orders, batch cancel orders by client\_order\_id)

Access keys also have expirations for security reasons. The maximum allowed expiration is 365 days.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#account-registration)

Account Registration

To register an account on Orderly:

1.  Choose a valid chain/builder to register the account on
2.  Check if the wallet is already registered via [this](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/check-if-wallet-is-registered)
     API or if the account already exists via [this](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/check-if-account-exists)
     API
3.  Get a nonce from the [Get registration nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-registration-nonce)
     API
4.  Sign a message from the wallet using the EIP-712 standard in the following format and obtain the signature:

    {
      "brokerId": "woofi_dex",
      "chainId": 80001,
      "timestamp": 1685973017064,
      "registrationNonce": "194528949540"
    }
    

where:

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| brokerId | string | Y   | Builder ID |
| chainId | int | Y   | Chain ID of registering chain (within those supported by the Network) |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | Timestamp in UNIX milliseconds |
| registrationNonce | string | Y   | Valid nonce from [Get registration nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-registration-nonce) |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#add-access-key)

Add Access Key

To add a new access key on Orderly:

1.  Generate an ed25519 key pair
2.  Sign a message from the wallet using the EIP-712 standard in the following format and obtain the signature:

    {
      "brokerId": "woofi_dex",
      "orderlyKey": "ed25519:HqN9uKJioHjAJZbadgQRGzq2e7huKg6foCyNY43hWbCk",
      "scope": "trading",
      "timestamp": 1685973094398,
      "expiration": 1686081094398
    }
    

where:

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| brokerId | string | Y   | Builder ID |
| chainId | int | Y   | Chain ID of registering chain (within those supported by the Network) |
| orderlyKey | string | Y   | ed25519 public key |
| scope | string | Y   | Valid scopes are `read`, `trading`; multiple scopes can be sent, comma separated |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | Timestamp in UNIX milliseconds |
| expiration | timestamp | Y   | Expiration time of the key in UNIX milliseconds. Maximum allowed expiration is 365 days from add |

3.  Send all the necessary information via the [Add Orderly Key](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/add-orderly-key)
     API

Users can also check the validity of a particular Orderly access key via the Get Orderly Key API.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#deposits-/-withdrawals)

Deposits / Withdrawals
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#deposit)

Deposit

Token deposits can be made on any supported chain. To create a deposit request:

1.  Approve spending of the token by the smart contract
2.  Call the deposit function of the smart contract with the following structured data:

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| accountId | bytes | Y   | The account ID of the user in bytes |
| brokerHash | bytes | Y   | The keccak256 hash of the builder ID (e.g. “woofi\_dex”) |
| tokenHash | bytes | Y   | The keccak256 hash of the token string (e.g. “USDC”) |
| tokenAmount | int | Y   | Amount of tokens to be deposited |

Note that deposits will take some time to reflect depending on the origin chain, to allow for finalization of the cross-chain transaction. Once the deposit is credited, the balance will update and the transaction can be seen on the Get asset history API.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#withdrawal)

Withdrawal

To withdraw tokens from Orderly:

1.  Choose a valid chain to withdraw your funds to
2.  Check if the chain has sufficient liquidity
3.  Get a withdrawal nonce
4.  Sign an EIP-712 message in the following format:

    {
      "brokerId": "woofi_dex",
      "chainId": 80001,
      "receiver": "0x036Cb579025d3535a0ADcD929D05481a3189714b",
      "token": "USDC",
      "amount": "1000000",
      "withdrawNonce": 1,
      "timestamp": 1685973017064
    }
    

where:

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| brokerId | string | Y   | Builder ID |
| chainId | int | Y   | Chain ID of withdrawing chain (within those supported by the Network) |
| receiver | string | Y   | Address of the receiver, which should be equal to the address of the account |
| token | string | Y   | The string representation of the token being withdrawn (e.g. “USDC”) |
| amount | string | Y   | Amount of tokens to be withdrawn |
| withdrawNonce | int | Y   | Valid withdrawal nonce from [Get withdrawal nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-withdrawal-nonce) |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | Current timestamp in UNIX milliseconds |

5.  Call the request to withdraw API to complete the withdrawal request

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough#settle-pnl)

Settle PnL

Any realized and unrealized PnL accrued from open or closed positions needs to be “settled” into the USDC balance of the account. PnL settlement can happen anytime for any account if other users trigger a settlement. Any user can also trigger PnL settlement of their own account by following these steps:

1.  Get a Settle PnL nonce
2.  Sign an EIP-712 message in the following format:

    {
      "brokerId": "woofi_dex",
      "chainId": 80001,
      "settleNonce": 1,
      "timestamp": 1685973017064
    }
    

where:

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| brokerId | string | Y   | Builder ID |
| chainId | int | Y   | Chain ID of registering chain (within those supported by the Network) |
| settleNonce | int | Y   | Valid settlement nonce from [Get Settle PnL nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-settle-pnl-nonce) |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | Current timestamp in UNIX milliseconds |

3.  Call the Settle PnL API to complete the request

[Internal Transfer](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/internal-transfer)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction)

⌘I

# Setup - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

\[evm\] react hooks SDK

Setup

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Environments](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#environments)
    
*   [Wallet Integration with Orderly Provider](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#wallet-integration-with-orderly-provider)
    

You can use `@orderly.network/hooks` in two simple steps!

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#)

Installation

Inside your React project directory, run the following to install the SDK. You will also very likely need our Types SDK:

    npm install @orderly.network/types @orderly.network/hooks
    

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#)

Add OrderlyConfigProvider

Add the `OrderlyConfigProvider` component to your project, wrapping the root components.

*   Default
    
*   Custom
    

We recommend that you use the default config in the newly created project and provide your own `brokerId`. Start from [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
 to set up your builder identity. If you are using Orderly One, continue from [https://dex.orderly.network/](https://dex.orderly.network/)
.

    import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from "react";
    import { OrderlyConfigProvider } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    
    const brokerId = "<your id>";
    
    export const App: FC<PropsWithChildren> = ({ children }) => {
      return (
        <OrderlyConfigProvider brokerId={brokerId} networkId="mainnet">
          {children}
        </OrderlyConfigProvider>
      );
    };
    

If you are using an existing project and have already implemented the Orderly Key creation process, or have to modify the creation process for your own use cases, you can use your own `configStore` or `keyStore` via their respective property within `OrderlyConfigProvider`.

    import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from "react";
    import { OrderlyConfigProvider } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    import type { ConfigStore, OrderlyKeyStore } from "@orderly.network/core";
    
    const myConfigStore: ConfigStore = new <Your ConfigStore class>;
    const myKeyStore: OrderlyKeyStore = new <Your OrderlyKeyStore class>;
    
    export const App: FC<PropsWithChildren> = ({ children }) => {
    const brokerId = "<your id>";
    return (
      <OrderlyConfigProvider configStore={myConfigStore} keyStore={myKeyStore} networkId="mainnet">
        <children />
      </OrderlyProvider>
      );
    };
    

The respective interfaces the need to be implemented are:

*   [ConfigStore](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/interfaces/orderly_network_core.ConfigStore)
    
*   [OrderlyKeyStore](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/interfaces/orderly_network_core.OrderlyKeyStore)
    

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#)

Setup complete!

You can now use all the provided hooks within `@orderly.network/hooks`!

    import { useAccount } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    
    export const UserInfo = () => {
      const { account, state } = useAccount();
    
      return (
        <div>
          <div>Address: {state.address}</div>
        </div>
      );
    };
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#environments)

Environments
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `networkId` property within `OrderlyConfigProvider` can be set to `mainnet` or `testnet`. The default is `mainnet`. It is not possible to change the network during runtime. If you want to do so anyway, you need to reload the page.

    import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from "react";
    import { OrderlyConfigProvider } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    
    const brokerId = "<your id>";
    
    export const App: FC<PropsWithChildren> = ({ children }) => {
      return (
        <OrderlyConfigProvider brokerId={brokerId} networkId="testnet">
          {children}
        </OrderlyConfigProvider>
      );
    };
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup#wallet-integration-with-orderly-provider)

Wallet Integration with Orderly Provider
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Orderly Provider enables seamless integration with different blockchain wallets. It supports both EVM-compatible chains and Solana through dedicated wallet adapters. Here’s how to set up the provider with both ecosystems:

    import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from "react";
    import { OrderlyConfigProvider } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    import { DefaultEVMWalletAdapter } from "@orderly.network/default-evm-adapter";
    import { DefaultSolanaWalletAdapter } from "@orderly.network/default-solana-adapter";
    import { EthersProvider } from "@orderly.network/web3-provider-ethers";
    
    const brokerId = "<your id>";
    
    export const App: FC<PropsWithChildren> = ({ children }) => {
      return (
        <OrderlyConfigProvider
          brokerId={brokerId}
          networkId="testnet"
          walletAdapters={[\
            new DefaultEVMWalletAdapter(new EthersProvider()),\
            new DefaultSolanaWalletAdapter()\
          ]}
        >
          {children}
        </OrderlyConfigProvider>
      );
    };
    

[Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/overview)
[Low-level API](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api)

⌘I

# Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Affiliate's Quick Guide) - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Affiliate program

Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Affiliate's Quick Guide)

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [1\. Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#1-overview)
    
*   [2\. Prerequisites](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#2-prerequisites)
    
*   [3\. New vs. legacy affiliate programs](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#3-new-vs-legacy-affiliate-programs)
    
*   [4\. Coexistence of legacy affiliate program](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#4-coexistence-of-legacy-affiliate-program)
    
*   [5\. Referral commission structure](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-referral-commission-structure)
    
*   [5.1 Affiliate commission](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-1-affiliate-commission)
    
*   [Example: 3-Level Referral Network](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#example-3-level-referral-network)
    
*   [5.2 Referee’s commission rate adjustments](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate-adjustments)
    
*   [5.2.1 Default referee’s commission rate](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-1-default-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate)
    
*   [5.2.2 Custom referee’s commission rate](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-2-custom-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#1-overview)

1\. Overview
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly adds a brand new affiliate programme that allows DEXs and their affiliates to configure the percentage of referral commission share to foster the growth of their trading platform. The core model is a funneled and multilevel referral system:

*   Builders work with their direct affiliates to grow their trading platform, with attractive referral commissions for the direct affiliates to build their own multilevel referral networks.
*   Affiliates higher up the hierarchy have the flexibility to set higher commission sharing ratios. This allows affiliates to distribute a portion of their commissions to referees, based on their commission sharing ratio, thus creating a multilevel chain of referrals.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#2-prerequisites)

2\. Prerequisites
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any users can participate as affiliates, provided that the DEX activates the new affiliate program. In some cases, a trading volume requirement may be necessary. If a volume prerequisite is set, users must meet it to qualify as an affiliate and begin building their referral network. Since the required volume may vary between DEXs, users should confirm the specific prerequisites directly with the DEX they wish to join.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#3-new-vs-legacy-affiliate-programs)

3\. New vs. legacy affiliate programs
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The key differences between the legacy and new affiliate programs are summarized below:

| Details | Legacy | New |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Referral network** | **Single-level**, only direct referrals | **Multilevel** (up to 15 levels), direct and indirect referrals |
| **Referral commission** | Affiliates earn commissions from **direct referees only** | Affiliates earn commissions from both **direct and indirect referees** down the referral chain, with additional 10% bonus from direct referees |
| **L1L\_1L1​ affiliate’s commission rate** | Up to 100%, decided by builder | Up to 90%, decided by builder |
| **Referral code creation** | Created primarily by the builder or qualified affiliates upon meeting the applicable trading volume requirements. | Qualified affiliates can create codes after meeting the required trading volume (where applicable). Affiliates may only create one unique referral code. |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#4-coexistence-of-legacy-affiliate-program)

4\. Coexistence of legacy affiliate program
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This new affiliate program is a multilevel referral system which supersedes the legacy affiliate program. DEXs who have enabled the legacy affiliate program could choose to keep the legacy system or upgrade to the new one. If you are already an existing affiliate, please check with your respective DEX directly for details regarding the activation of the new program. Once activated by the DEX, you will be able to generate a new referral code under this system. Please note that all existing legacy referral codes will remain active, and you will continue to earn commission from them.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-referral-commission-structure)

5\. Referral commission structure
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEXs have the flexibility to determine the referral commission rate allocated to L1L\_1L1​ affiliates. These L1L\_1L1​ affiliates are the initial referrers who establish a multilevel network and do not report to any referrer above them.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-1-affiliate-commission)

5.1 Affiliate commission

Affiliates receive their referral commissions from the builder fees shared by DEXs. This commission is paid out for every trade executed on the DEX. (Fee)Builder\=(Trading fee)N−(Base fee)Builder\\text{(Fee)}\_\\text{Builder} = \\text{(Trading fee)}\_N - \\text{(Base fee)}\_\\text{Builder}(Fee)Builder​\=(Trading fee)N​−(Base fee)Builder​ where (Base fee)Builder\\text{(Base fee)}\_\\text{Builder}(Base fee)Builder​ depends on the builder fee tier of the DEX. L1L\_1L1​ affiliates may distribute a portion of their commission to their direct referees (L2L\_2L2​). This process can be extended further, as L2L\_2L2​ affiliates can further sub-divide their allocation to their direct referees (L3L\_3L3​), establishing a multilevel structure. This multilevel referral network can extend up to a total of 15 levels. For every trade executed by a user (LN)(L\_N)(LN​) on the DEX, the affiliates within the multilevel referral network can earn the following commission:

| Role | Affiliate commission rate | Affiliate commission |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1L\_1L1​ affiliate** | (X1−X2)(X\_1 - X\_2)(X1​−X2​) where X2∈\[0,X1\]X\_2 \\in \[0, X\_1\]X2​∈\[0,X1​\] | (X1−X2)×FeeBuilder(X\_1 - X\_2) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X1​−X2​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **L2L\_2L2​ affiliate** | (X2−X3)(X\_2 - X\_3)(X2​−X3​) where X3∈\[0,X2\]X\_3 \\in \[0, X\_2\]X3​∈\[0,X2​\] | (X2−X3)×FeeBuilder(X\_2 - X\_3) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X2​−X3​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **L3L\_3L3​ affiliate** | (X3−X4)(X\_3 - X\_4)(X3​−X4​) where X4∈\[0,X3\]X\_4 \\in \[0, X\_3\]X4​∈\[0,X3​\] | (X3−X4)×FeeBuilder(X\_3 - X\_4) \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}(X3​−X4​)×FeeBuilder​ |
| **…** | …   | …   |
| **LN−1L\_{N-1}LN−1​ user (direct referrer)** | XN−1+10%X\_{N-1} + 10\\%XN−1​+10% | \[XN−1+10%\]×FeeBuilder\[X\_{N-1} + 10\\%\] \\times \\text{Fee}\_\\text{Builder}\[XN−1​+10%\]×FeeBuilder​ — Direct referrer earns full rate plus 10% bonus |

> Note: Direct referrers earn a 10% bonus referral commission rate from trades executed by their direct referees.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#example-3-level-referral-network)

Example: 3-Level Referral Network

Consider a trade where Builder revenue for a given trade = $100, with the following commission rates:

*   X1\=40%X\_1 = 40\\%X1​\=40% (L1L\_1L1​ affiliate rate set by builder)
*   X2\=25%X\_2 = 25\\%X2​\=25% (L2L\_2L2​ rate set by L1L\_1L1​)
*   X3\=15%X\_3 = 15\\%X3​\=15% (L3L\_3L3​ rate set by L2L\_2L2​)

**Commission distribution for $100 Builder revenue:**

| Role | Calculation | Commission |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1L\_1L1​ affiliate | (40%−25%)×100(40\\% - 25\\%) \\times 100(40%−25%)×100 | **$15** |
| L2L\_2L2​ affiliate | (25%−15%)×100(25\\% - 15\\%) \\times 100(25%−15%)×100 | **$10** |
| L3L\_3L3​ affiliate (direct referrer) | (15%+10%)×100(15\\% + 10\\%) \\times 100(15%+10%)×100 | **$25** |
| **Total distributed** |     | **$50** |

In this example, when L4L\_4L4​ (the trader) executes a trade, L3L\_3L3​ earns their full 15%15\\%15% rate plus the 10%10\\%10% bonus, totaling 25.The25\. The 25.TheL\_4$ trader does not earn commission from their own trade.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate-adjustments)

5.2 Referee’s commission rate adjustments

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-1-default-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate)

5.2.1 Default referee’s commission rate

Each referral code has a default commission rate for referees who use it to create an account on the DEX. The affiliate owning the code can update this rate, subject to the following rules:

*   If the code has no referees, the affiliate can freely adjust the default commission rate up or down.
*   If the code has direct referees, the affiliate may only increase the default commission rate, applying changes to all direct referees on that rate. This restriction protects existing referrals and prevents unfavourable downstream changes.

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-affiliates-quick-guide#5-2-2-custom-referee%E2%80%99s-commission-rate)

5.2.2 Custom referee’s commission rate

Affiliates may customize a higher commission rate to overwrite the default rate for any direct referees, but cannot decrease it.

[Builders](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/builders)
[Orderly DEX's Affiliate Program (Builder's Quick Guide)](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/be-a-broker/orderly-dex-affiliate-program-brokers-quick-guide)

# Delisting Standards - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Search...

Search...

Navigation

Security, legal & FAQ

Delisting Standards

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Direct Delisting Threshold](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#direct-delisting-threshold)
    
*   [Evaluation Metrics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#evaluation-metrics)
    
*   [Additional Reference Metrics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#additional-reference-metrics)
    
*   [Evaluation Notes](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#evaluation-notes)
    

When deciding on delisting, Orderly comprehensively considers the following four aspects. Additionally, projects that fail to meet the absolute minimum threshold outlined below will be subject to direct delisting, irrespective of their performance in other metrics.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#direct-delisting-threshold)

Direct Delisting Threshold

Trading Volume Floor: Any project whose monthly trading volume consistently falls below the absolute threshold of $20 million will be directly delisted. This threshold serves as a fundamental indicator of a project’s minimum viable market presence and liquidity.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#evaluation-metrics)

Evaluation Metrics

The following four aspects are used for comprehensive evaluation and scoring: **1\. Trading Volume** Orderly sets a benchmark value for the monthly trading volume of a project. This benchmark is one of the important indicators for evaluating a project’s market activity and user engagement. If a project’s monthly trading volume meets or exceeds this benchmark, it receives a full score. If the monthly trading volume falls below this standard, the score is adjusted proportionally to reflect the project’s market appeal and user transaction frequency. In a highly competitive market, monthly trading volume provides an intuitive and real-time reflection, helping us gain deeper insights into the project’s sustainability. **2\. Number of Trading Users** Based on the set benchmark for the monthly number of trading users, Orderly believes this metric directly reflects the project’s active community and user engagement. If the number of users meets or exceeds this benchmark, it receives a full score. If the number falls below this standard, the score is adjusted proportionally. This metric is crucial as it not only reflects users’ trust in the project but also hints at the project’s competitiveness and prospects in the market. Additionally, active user participation may lead to higher brand recognition and network effects, which are vital for the platform’s long-term development. **3\. FTT (Number of New Users)** Orderly sets a monthly FTT benchmark value, representing the circulation and demand intensity of the project’s token in the market. If the monthly FTT meets or exceeds this benchmark, it receives a full score. If it falls below this standard, the score is calculated proportionally based on the actual value. The level of FTT reflects users’ recognition of the project and their motivation to participate, making it an undeniably important metric. By monitoring changes in FTT, Orderly can better assess the project’s potential value and its significance within the ecosystem. **4\. CMC/Coingecko Ranking** Orderly scores projects based on their ranking on CMC or Coingecko. This ranking can be seen as a general market recognition of the project’s value. Specifically, projects within certain ranking ranges receive different scores. Projects with superior rankings receive higher scores. Additionally, Orderly may apply manual score adjustments in special cases. For example, early-stage projects promoted under specific business strategies or ecosystems may receive additional points. This approach allows us to identify and incorporate promising early-stage opportunities into our ecosystem.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#additional-reference-metrics)

Additional Reference Metrics

Open Interest (OI): This is an important reference metric reflecting the total number of outstanding contracts in the market at a specific point in time. High OI typically indicates strong interest and market participation in the contract, enabling Orderly to gauge market sentiment and investor activity. Target Market Demand: Analyzing the specific demands of the project’s target market helps Orderly assess its future growth potential and competitive landscape. Project Development Dynamics: Regularly tracking the project’s technical improvements, community activities, partnerships, and other developments provides valuable information for the overall evaluation.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/delisting-standards#evaluation-notes)

Evaluation Notes

The specific numerical values and scoring calculation methods for each evaluation criterion have been hidden to protect Orderly’s strategies and competitive advantages. Please note that the above criteria may be adjusted based on market changes and business needs to maintain the timeliness and accuracy of the evaluation.

[FAQ](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/faqs)

Error Codes

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)

| Error Code | Status Code | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| \-1000 | 500 | An unknown error occurred while processing the request. |
| \-1000 | 500 | The data does not exist. |
| \-1001 | 401 | The API key or secret is in wrong format. |
| \-1002 | 401 | API key or secret is invalid. The key may have insufficient permissions, or is expired/revoked. |
| \-1003 | 429 | Rate limit exceeded. |
| \-1004 | 400 | An unknown parameter was sent. |
| \-1005 | 400 | Some parameters are in wrong format. |
| \-1005 | 400 | `ratio_qty_request` should be in range 0-1. |
| \-1005 | 400 | `extra_liquidation_ratio` should be in range 0-1. |
| \-1005 | 400 | If `extra_liquidation_ratio` > 0, `ratio_qty_request` must be 1. |
| \-1006 | 400 | The data is not found on the server. For example, attempting to cancel a `CANCELLED` order will raise this error. |
| \-1007 | 409 | The data already exists or the request is duplicated. |
| \-1008 | 400 | The settlement quantity is higher than the allowed amount. |
| \-1009 | 400 | Cannot request withdrawal settlement — deposit other arrears first. |
| \-1011 | 400 | Cannot place/cancel orders due to an internal network error. Please try again in a few seconds. |
| \-1012 | 400 | The order request was rejected by an internal module. The account may be in liquidation or experiencing other internal errors. Please try again in a few seconds. |
| \-1012 | 400 | Another liquidation is in process. |
| \-1101 | 400 | Risk exposure is too high. This may be caused by a large order size or low leverage. Check current exposure via the client info endpoint. |
| \-1101 | 400 | Insufficient margin after the operation. |
| \-1102 | 400 | The order value (price × size) is too small. |
| \-1103 | 400 | Order price is below the minimum allowed price (`quote_min`). |
| \-1103 | 400 | Order price exceeds the maximum allowed price (`quote_max`). |
| \-1103 | 400 | Order price does not match the tick size. |
| \-1104 | 400 | Order quantity is below the minimum allowed amount (`base_min`). |
| \-1104 | 400 | Order quantity exceeds the maximum allowed amount (`base_max`). |
| \-1104 | 400 | Order quantity does not match the step size. |
| \-1104 | 400 | Visible quantity does not match the step size. |
| \-1105 | 400 | Price is X% too high or X% too low from the mid price. |
| \-1201 | 400 | Total notional < 10000; least request ratio should be 1. |
| \-1201 | 400 | Least request ratio should be the specified value. |
| \-1202 | 400 | No liquidation needed — user margin is sufficient. |
| \-1202 | 400 | Cannot find the given `liquidationId`. |

# Integration FAQs - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-faqs#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Partner onboarding & support

Integration FAQs

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

What is Orderly Network?

Orderly is an omnichain trading infrastructure that provides orderbook-based trading services and shared liquidity for builders. It lets developers build their own trading application while keeping user settlement on-chain and user custody in the wallet.

How do I start integrating?

Start with the [Integration Checklist](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/integration-checklist)
, then choose the integration path that fits your stack: [React Hooks](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/overview)
, [React UI components](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview)
, the [Python connector](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-connector-python)
, or direct integration through the [API docs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction)
.

Do I need a Builder ID?

Not everyone does. If you are an API trader using an existing builder, you can select from the available builders through the [builder list endpoint](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-builder-list)
. If you are launching your own front end and onboarding your own users, start from [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
. If you are using Orderly One, continue from [https://dex.orderly.network/](https://dex.orderly.network/)
.

How are fees managed?

Builders can configure their own fees on top of Orderly’s base fee model. Builders can define a default fee rate and, if needed, apply custom fee logic for specific users. See [Custom Fees](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)
 for the builder flow and related endpoints.

What is the Builder Admin Wallet?

The builder admin wallet is the wallet associated with your builder setup. It is used for builder-level actions such as fee management and other admin-authenticated endpoints described in the API docs.

Can I access user data?

Most user data is only accessible by the account holder using the user’s Trading Key. Builders can retrieve builder-scoped data for their own users through dedicated builder endpoints, but not unrestricted data for all users on the system.

How do I handle system maintenance?

Monitor the [system maintenance status endpoint](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-system-maintenance-status)
 and stay in the builder Telegram channel for announcements. Ask the Orderly team for channel access during onboarding. During maintenance, market data and user trading surfaces may be unavailable, so your UI should clearly communicate the maintenance state and only expose order actions that remain available.

Where do I get help during integration?

Start with [Builder Onboarding](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/builder-onboarding)
 for builder setup. If you are using Orderly One, continue from [https://dex.orderly.network/](https://dex.orderly.network/)
. For implementation questions, join the [Orderly Discord](https://discord.gg/OrderlyNetwork)
. For a practical overview of support resources, see [Partner Support](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/partner-support)
.

[Affiliate Onboarding Guide](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/affiliate-onboarding)
[Accounts](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/accounts)

# Custom Fee Structure - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Navigation

Core integration flows

Custom Fee Structure

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Overview of Fee Structure](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#overview-of-fee-structure)
    
*   [User Flow for Builders](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#user-flow-for-builders)
    
*   [Builder Rebate Distribution](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#builder-rebate-distribution)
    
*   [FAQs](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#faqs)
    

The Custom Trading Fee System allows builders to tailor fee structures for their users beyond Orderly’s standard fees, giving builders the flexibility to set fees that align with their strategies and customer profiles.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#overview-of-fee-structure)

Overview of Fee Structure
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

|     | Base Fee | Custom Fee |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Who receives** | Orderly (100%) | Builder (100%) |
| **Taker** | 3 bps | Set by builder |
| **Maker** | 0 bps | Set by builder |

The base fee is subject to change according to the [builder staking programme](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/trading-basics/trading-fees#builder-staking-programme)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#user-flow-for-builders)

User Flow for Builders
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#)

Determine fee tiers

Decide on the logic for fee tiers based on specific criteria (e.g. volume, staked tokens), or set all users under the same fee rate.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#)

Calculate fee rates

Assign users to fee rates based on the chosen criteria.Orderly provides a [fee tier assignment tool](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-broker-fee-admin)
 for volume-based tiers.

The fee rate is the total charged to the user (Orderly base fee + custom fee). It cannot be less than the Orderly base fee.

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#)

Set the default fee rate

Set the `default` fee rate through [POST `/v1/broker/fee_rate/default`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-default-builder-fee)
.

4

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#)

Set per-user fees

Send updated fee assignments to Orderly:

*   **Users keeping their current rate (including default):** no update needed
*   **Users reset to default:** send through [POST `/v1/broker/fee_rate/set_default`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate)
    
*   **Users assigned a new rate:** send through [POST `/v1/broker/fee_rate/set`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate)
     — batch by `maker_fee`/`taker_fee` combination

All users are assigned the default fee rate unless overridden by the builder.

5

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#)

Retrieve fee rates

*   User fee rate: [GET `/v1/broker/user_info`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-user-fee-rates)
    
*   Default fee rate: [GET `/v1/broker/fee_rate/default`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-default-builder-fee)
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#builder-rebate-distribution)

Builder Rebate Distribution

Builders receive daily rebates in their fee wallet, calculated after deducting any referral rebates.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees#faqs)

FAQs
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Will there be limitations on how often fee rates can be changed?

There are no inherent limitations besides API rate limits, but we recommend prudent management to maintain consistency and trust with end users.

How to set fees for users?

Builders can develop a custom tiered structure based on different parameters like volume, staked tokens, etc.

Can I get help determining and setting fees?

Orderly provides a [fee tier assignment tool](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-evm-broker-fee-admin)
 to help builders assign users to volume-based tiers.

How does the rebate work for builders?

Builders keep all fees above the Orderly base fee (3 bps taker, 0 bps maker).

How are fee rebates distributed?

Builder fee rebates are distributed daily to the builder’s Orderly account registered with the wallet provided during the builder ID setup.

What are the fee wallet requirements?

Currently EOA only, with multisig support upcoming.

[Isolated Margin](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin)
[Delegate Signer](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/delegate-signer)

⌘I

# API Authentication - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#content-area)

API fundamentals

API Authentication

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
