On this page

*   [Generate Request Signature](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#generate-request-signature)
    
*   [Minimal Example](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#minimal-example)
    
*   [Full Example](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#full-example)
    
*   [Security](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#security)
    
*   [Request Timestamp](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#request-timestamp)
    
*   [Signature Verification](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#signature-verification)
    
*   [Orderly Key Validity](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#orderly-key-validity)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#generate-request-signature)

Generate Request Signature
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly uses the [`ed25519` elliptic curve standard](https://ed25519.cr.yp.to/)
 for request authentication via signature verification.

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Orderly Account ID

Register your account and obtain your account ID. The registration steps are provided [here](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/accounts)
. Add your account ID to the request header as `orderly-account-id`.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Orderly Key

Add your Orderly public key to the request header as `orderly-key`. To generate and add a new Orderly Key, see [the documentation](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication#orderly-key)
. You can also obtain Orderly keys from frontend builders like [WOOFi Pro](https://pro.woofi.com/)
.

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Timestamp

Take the current timestamp in milliseconds and add it as `orderly-timestamp` to the request header.

4

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Normalize request content

Normalize the message to a string by concatenating the following:

1.  Current timestamp in milliseconds, e.g. `1649920583000`
2.  HTTP method in uppercase, e.g. `POST`
3.  Request path **including** query parameters (without base URL), e.g. `/v1/orders?symbol=PERP_BTC_USDC`
4.  (Optional) If the request has a body, JSON stringify it and append

Example result:

    1649920583000POST/v1/order{"symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC", "order_type": "LIMIT", "order_price": 1521.03, "order_quantity": 2.11, "side": "BUY"}
    

5

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Generate signature

Sign the normalized content using the `ed25519` algorithm, encode the signature in base64 url-safe format, and add the result to the request header as `orderly-signature`.

6

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Content type

Set the `Content-Type` header:

*   `GET` and `DELETE`: `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`
*   `POST` and `PUT`: `application/json`

7

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#)

Send the request

The final request should have the following headers:

| Header | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | Request content type |
| `orderly-account-id` | Your Orderly account ID |
| `orderly-key` | Your Orderly public key |
| `orderly-signature` | ed25519 signature (base64url) |
| `orderly-timestamp` | Request timestamp (ms) |

The Orderly Key should be used without the `ed25519:` prefix when used in code samples below.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#minimal-example)

Minimal Example

This is a concise, single-file TypeScript example that demonstrates how to sign and send a request to the Orderly API using the `@noble/ed25519` library.

    import { signAsync, getPublicKeyAsync } from '@noble/ed25519';
    import bs58 from 'bs58';
    
    async function main() {
      const orderlyAccountId = 'YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID';
      const orderlySecret = 'YOUR_ORDERLY_SECRET'; // base58 encoded
      const baseUrl = 'https://testnet-api.orderly.org';
      const url = new URL(`${baseUrl}/v1/order`);
      const method = 'POST';
      const body = JSON.stringify({
        symbol: 'PERP_ETH_USDC',
        order_type: 'MARKET',
        order_quantity: 0.01,
        side: 'BUY'
      });
      const timestamp = Date.now();
    
      const privateKey = bs58.decode(orderlySecret);
      const message = `${timestamp}${method}${url.pathname}${url.search}${body}`;
      const signature = await signAsync(new TextEncoder().encode(message), privateKey);
    
      const response = await fetch(url, {
        method,
        body,
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'orderly-account-id': orderlyAccountId,
          'orderly-key': `ed25519:${bs58.encode(await getPublicKeyAsync(privateKey))}`,
          'orderly-timestamp': String(timestamp),
          'orderly-signature': Buffer.from(signature).toString('base64url'),
        },
      });
    
      const data = await response.json();
      console.log(data);
    }
    
    main();
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#full-example)

Full Example

*   Java
    
*   Python
    
*   TypeScript
    
*   Shell
    

AuthenticationExample.java

Signer.java

    import io.github.cdimascio.dotenv.Dotenv;
    import net.i2p.crypto.eddsa.EdDSAPrivateKey;
    import net.i2p.crypto.eddsa.spec.EdDSANamedCurveTable;
    import net.i2p.crypto.eddsa.spec.EdDSAParameterSpec;
    import net.i2p.crypto.eddsa.spec.EdDSAPrivateKeySpec;
    import okhttp3.OkHttpClient;
    import okhttp3.Request;
    import okhttp3.Response;
    
    import org.bitcoinj.base.Base58;
    import org.json.JSONObject;
    
    public class AuthenticationExample {
    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
    String baseUrl = "https://testnet-api.orderly.org";
    String orderlyAccountId = "<orderly-account-id>";
    
          Dotenv dotenv = Dotenv.load();
          OkHttpClient client = new OkHttpClient();
    
          String key = dotenv.get("ORDERLY_SECRET");
          EdDSAParameterSpec spec = EdDSANamedCurveTable.getByName(EdDSANamedCurveTable.ED_25519);
          EdDSAPrivateKeySpec encoded = new EdDSAPrivateKeySpec(Base58.decode(key), spec);
          EdDSAPrivateKey orderlyKey = new EdDSAPrivateKey(encoded);
    
          Signer signer = new Signer(baseUrl, orderlyAccountId, orderlyKey);
    
          JSONObject json = new JSONObject();
          json.put("symbol", "PERP_ETH_USDC");
          json.put("order_type", "MARKET");
          json.put("order_quantity", 0.01);
          json.put("side", "BUY");
          Request req = signer.createSignedRequest("/v1/order", "POST", json);
          String res;
          try (Response response = client.newCall(req).execute()) {
             res = response.body().string();
          }
          JSONObject obj = new JSONObject(res);
    
    }
    }
    

authentication\_example.py

signer.py

    import json
    import os
    from base58 import b58decode
    from requests import Request, Session
    
    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PrivateKey
    
    from signer import Signer
    
    base_url = "https://testnet-api.orderly.org"
    orderly_account_id = "<orderly-account-id>"
    
    key = b58decode(os.environ.get("ORDERLY_SECRET"))
    orderly_key = Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(key)
    
    session = Session()
    signer = Signer(orderly_account_id, orderly_key)
    
    req = signer.sign_request(
        Request(
            "POST",
            "%s/v1/order" % base_url,
            json={
                "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
                "order_type": "MARKET",
                "order_quantity": 0.01,
                "side": "BUY",
            },
        )
    )
    res = session.send(req)
    response = json.loads(res.text)
    

authenticationExample.ts

signer.ts

    import bs58 from 'bs58';
    import { config } from 'dotenv';
    import { webcrypto } from 'node:crypto';
    
    import { signAndSendRequest } from "./signer";
    
    // this is only necessary in Node.js to make `@noble/ed25519` dependency work
    if (!globalThis.crypto) globalThis.crypto = webcrypto as any;
    
    config();
    
    async function main() {
      const baseUrl = 'https://testnet-api.orderly.org';
      const orderlyAccountId = '<orderly-account-id>';
    
      const orderlyKey = bs58.decode(process.env.ORDERLY_SECRET!);
    
      const res = await signAndSendRequest(orderlyAccountId, orderlyKey, `${baseUrl}/v1/order`, {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify({
          symbol: 'PERP_ETH_USDC',
          order_type: 'MARKET',
          order_quantity: 0.01,
          side: 'BUY'
        })
      });
      const response = await res.json();
      console.log(response);
    }
    
    main();
    

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/order \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: ed25519:8tm7dnKYkSc3FzgPuJaw1wztr79eeZpN35nHW5pL5XhX' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: dG4bkKiqG0dUYLzViRZkvbI6Sy239JxAdNMIBxFZ4w030Jofr0ORV06GHtvXZkaZaWUXE+XAU3fnzKN/5fDeBQ==' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: 1649920583000' \
      --data '{
      "symbol": "<symbol>",
      "order_type": "<order_type>",
      "side": "<side>",
      "reduce_only": false
    }'
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#security)

Security
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly validates every request through three checks. A request must pass all three to be accepted.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#request-timestamp)

Request Timestamp

The request is rejected if the `orderly-timestamp` header differs from the API server time by more than 300 seconds.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#signature-verification)

Signature Verification

The `orderly-signature` header must be a valid ed25519 signature generated from the normalized request content and signed with your Orderly secret key.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication#orderly-key-validity)

Orderly Key Validity

The `orderly-key` header must reference a key that has been added to the network, is associated with the account, and has not expired.

[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction)
[Error Codes](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/error-codes)

# Liquidity Provider - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Overview & FAQ

Liquidity Provider

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Deposits](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#deposits)
    
*   [Withdrawal](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#withdrawal)
    
*   [Claim Your Withdrawal](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#claim-your-withdrawal)
    
*   [How-to Guide](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#how-to-guide)
    

LPs can make deposits or submit a withdrawal request anytime via the [Orderly OmniVault App](https://app.orderly.network/vaults)
. Transactions will be processed in the subsequent vault period. Please note it may take a few minutes for your transaction to reflect across the app’s data, as every transaction undergoes a cross-chain messaging via the LayerZero protocol to Orderly chain.[1](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#ref21) If your on-chain transaction (deposit or withdrawal request) has a success status, it is assured that Orderly OmniVault has received your transaction request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#deposits)

Deposits
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LPs have the flexibility to deposit USDC from any supported network. On the deposit section on the app, after inputting the amount of USDC, you will see an estimated number of shares you will receive based on the current share price. However, the actual number of shares issued may vary from the estimate, as the share price at the time of your deposit request may differ from the final vault period settlement share price. Every deposit transaction is subject to:

*   Minimum Deposit Amount: 10 USDC
*   Deposit Lock-up Duration: 2 days
    *   The lock-up duration begins at the start of the vault period during which your deposit is processed, not at the exact time you made the deposit.
    *   Once the lock-up period ends, the shares will be available for withdrawal.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#withdrawal)

Withdrawal
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LPs can submit withdrawal requests from any supported network. The amount of USDC claimable on each network is determined by the blockchain network on which the withdrawal requests are submitted to. In the withdrawal section of the app, after inputting the number of shares to withdraw, you will see an estimated USDC amount based on the current share price. However, the actual claimable amount may differ from this estimate, as the final settlement share price may vary from the current estimate. The withdrawal gate is a safety mechanism designed to protect the interests of all LPs and maintain vault stability. It limits the percentage (10%) of total vault assets that can be redeemed in a single vault period. If total withdrawal requests exceed this threshold, the excess requests will be queued and processed in subsequent vault periods. Every withdrawal request transaction is subject to:

*   Minimum Withdrawal Amount: 10 USDC

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#claim-your-withdrawal)

Claim Your Withdrawal
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the withdrawal requests have been assigned to a vault period, Orderly OmniVault will begin the redemption process. LPs can typically claim their redeemed USDC approximately 15 to 30 minutes after the end of that vault period. Delays may occasionally occur due to factors such as network conditions or unforeseen delays, particularly during peak periods. When your withdrawal request status is marked as “Claimable” in the app, you may proceed to claim your USDC on the respective blockchain network.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/liquidity-provider#how-to-guide)

How-to Guide
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Refer to our [how-to-guide](https://orderly.network/blog/omnivaulthow)
 blog for the clip on how to perform deposit/withdrawal request/claim with Orderly OmniVault!

* * *

1 Orderly does not control the LayerZero protocol and is not responsible for delays, message failures, or other issues related to cross-chain messaging.

[Mechanics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/mechanics)
[FAQ](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/faq)

# Isolated Margin - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

Navigation

Core integration flows

Isolated Margin

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#overview)
    
*   [Step 1: Set Leverage for Isolated Mode](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-1-set-leverage-for-isolated-mode)
    
*   [Batch Leverage Updates](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#batch-leverage-updates)
    
*   [Step 2: Place an Order](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-2-place-an-order)
    
*   [Step 3: Adjust Position Margin](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-3-adjust-position-margin)
    
*   [Step 4: Monitor Position](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-4-monitor-position)
    
*   [WebSocket Updates](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#websocket-updates)
    
*   [Account Balance](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#account-balance)
    
*   [Liquidation Behavior](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#liquidation-behavior)
    
*   [Related Pages](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#related-pages)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#overview)

Overview
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This guide walks through the full lifecycle of using Isolated Margin via the API. Both Cross and Isolated margin modes are supported simultaneously — you can hold a Cross position and an Isolated position on the same symbol at the same time. Simply specify `margin_mode` when placing an order. For a conceptual overview, see [Isolated Margin basics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/isolated-margin)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-1-set-leverage-for-isolated-mode)

Step 1: Set Leverage for Isolated Mode
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each symbol maintains independent leverage settings per margin mode. Set the leverage for the Isolated mode specifically. **API:** [POST `/v1/client/leverages`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-leverage-setting)
 **Request body (single symbol):**

    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "leverage": 10,
      "margin_mode": "ISOLATED"
    }
    

You can also query the current leverage: **API:** [GET `/v1/client/leverage`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-leverage-setting)
 with query params `symbol=PERP_ETH_USDC&margin_mode=ISOLATED`

The leverage you set in Isolated mode determines how much margin is allocated from your available balance when opening a position. Higher leverage = less margin allocated = tighter liquidation price.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#batch-leverage-updates)

Batch Leverage Updates

When using [POST `/v1/client/leverages`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-leverage-setting)
 without a `symbol` parameter, the behavior differs by mode:

| Mode | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| **Cross** | All cross-margin symbols update atomically — all succeed or none do |
| **Isolated** | Only applies to symbols with no active isolated positions or pending orders; eligible symbols always update |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-2-place-an-order)

Step 2: Place an Order
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Place an order specifying the `margin_mode` parameter. **API:** [POST `/v1/order`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order)
 **Request body:**

    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "order_type": "LIMIT",
      "side": "BUY",
      "order_price": 2500,
      "order_quantity": 1,
      "margin_mode": "ISOLATED"
    }
    

If `margin_mode` is omitted, the symbol’s default margin mode is used. You can change the default display mode via [POST `/v1/client/margin_mode`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-margin-mode)
 — this is primarily for frontend display purposes and does not restrict which mode you can trade in.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-3-adjust-position-margin)

Step 3: Adjust Position Margin
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After opening an Isolated position, you can add or reduce the margin allocated to it. **API:** [POST `/v1/position_margin`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin)
 **Add margin** (lowers liquidation price, reduces risk):

    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "amount": "100",
      "type": "ADD"
    }
    

**Reduce margin** (raises liquidation price, frees up balance):

    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "amount": "50",
      "type": "REDUCE"
    }
    

| Type | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| `ADD` | Transfers margin from available balance to the isolated position |
| `REDUCE` | Transfers margin from the isolated position back to available balance |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#step-4-monitor-position)

Step 4: Monitor Position
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Query the position with the `margin_mode` parameter to get isolated-specific data. **API:** [GET `/v1/position/{symbol}`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-one-position-info)
 with query param `margin_mode=ISOLATED` Key fields in the response:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `margin` | Amount of margin allocated to this isolated position |
| `margin_mode` | `ISOLATED` |
| `leverage` | Current leverage for this position |
| `est_liq_price` | Estimated liquidation price |
| `position_qty` | Position size |
| `unsettled_pnl` | Current unsettled PnL |
| `imr` | Initial margin ratio |
| `mmr` | Maintenance margin ratio |

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#websocket-updates)

WebSocket Updates

Subscribe to the private `position` topic to receive real-time updates for position changes including margin, PnL, and liquidation price. See [WebSocket API](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/private/account)
 for details.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#account-balance)

Account Balance

The [GET `/v1/client/aggregate/holding`](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-aggregate-holding)
 endpoint includes two isolated-margin-specific fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `isolated_margin` | Total margin currently allocated to all isolated positions |
| `isolated_order_frozen` | Margin frozen in pending isolated orders |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#liquidation-behavior)

Liquidation Behavior
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When an Isolated position is liquidated:

*   Only the margin assigned to that position is lost
*   Other positions (Cross or Isolated) and your account balance are **unaffected**
*   The position is handled independently by the liquidation engine

For full details, see [Liquidations](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations)
.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/isolated-margin#related-pages)

Related Pages
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Isolated Margin basics](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/isolated-margin)
     — conceptual overview and comparison with Cross Margin
*   [Margin, Leverage & PnL](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/margin-leverage-and-pnl)
     — margin ratios, leverage tiers, and PnL calculations
*   [Order Management](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/order-management)
     — order lifecycle and API reference
*   [Liquidations](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations)
     — liquidation process and tiers

[Order Management](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/order-management)
[Custom Fee Structure](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/custom-fees)

⌘I

Referral code management (legacy)

Create referral code

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Create referral code

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/referral/create \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "account_id": "<string>",
      "referral_code": "<string>",
      "max_rebate_rate": 123,
      "referrer_rebate_rate": 123,
      "referee_rebate_rate": 123
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

referral

/

create

Try it

Create referral code

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/referral/create \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "account_id": "<string>",
      "referral_code": "<string>",
      "max_rebate_rate": 123,
      "referrer_rebate_rate": 123,
      "referee_rebate_rate": 123
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#body-account-id)

account\_id

string

required

the account id of the referrer

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#body-referral-code)

referral\_code

string

required

the referral code that admin wants to create

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#body-max-rebate-rate)

max\_rebate\_rate

number

required

the referral code’s rebate rate (max 1)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#body-referrer-rebate-rate)

referrer\_rebate\_rate

number

required

rebate rate of referrer

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#body-referee-rebate-rate)

referee\_rebate\_rate

number

required

rebate rate of referee

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-referral-code#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

Navigation

User issues

Transaction/order issues

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Use this page when a user reports a failed deposit, withdrawal, registration, or order request and the failure reason is still unclear.

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/transaction-failures#)

Confirm what failed

Collect the wallet address, builder ID, chain, market symbol, timestamp, and any transaction hash, order ID, or API error message.

Distinguish between an on-chain transaction failure and an Orderly API or order placement failure before continuing.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/transaction-failures#)

Check the relevant flow and prerequisites

For wallet and account setup issues, verify that the user followed the [Accounts](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/accounts)
 and [Wallet Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication)
 guides.For trading requests, confirm that the user submitted the correct payload and review the [API error codes](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/error-codes)
.

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/transaction-failures#)

Check chain-specific transaction status

If the failure is on-chain, confirm the user is interacting with a supported deployment from the [address list](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/addresses)
, then inspect the transaction in the relevant explorer for revert reasons, gas issues, or confirmation delays.

4

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/transaction-failures#)

Escalate with complete context

If the failure is still unresolved, send the collected details, the failing request payload, and any explorer or API response evidence to Orderly support so the issue can be reproduced quickly.

[Introduction overview](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Stuck/delayed withdrawals](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/withdrawal-issues)

⌘I

# Stuck/delayed deposits - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/deposit-issues#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

User issues

Stuck/delayed deposits

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Deposits do not reflect immediately in user’s account. If users are having issues with deposits please do the following:

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/deposit-issues#)

First Step

Check our [Dune dashboard](https://dune.com/orderly_network/orderly-support-dashboard)
 and check if deposit went through to any builder. Sometimes users deposit on one builder and expect the deposit to be reflected on another builder’s site.If the deposit is seen on the Dune dashboard, the funds are in the user’s account and most likely a front-end issue if the balance is not displaying.

Make sure to refresh the dashboard before checking.

Deposits times vary based on chain. Polygon deposit requires 500 block confirmations which takes about 20 minutes.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/deposit-issues#)

Second Step

If that wasn’t successful, please search the user’s wallet address on [LayerZero explorer](https://layerzeroscan.com/)
.A message appears with Orderly as the destination chain for any deposits we have received.

*   If a message is in-flight, the deposit is processing.
*   If a message is delivered, the deposit was processed.
*   If no message appears, Orderly has not received or processed the deposit. Please proceed to next step.

**Example of a processed deposit on LZ scan**

![](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/images/troubleshooting/lz-scan.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=16376f3f9002f6ce60acc638f2857504)

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/deposit-issues#)

Third Step

If that wasn’t successful, please send a message to Orderly with

*   User’s address
*   Deposit amount
*   Approximate time of deposit.

[Stuck/delayed withdrawals](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/withdrawal-issues)
[Liquidation issues](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/liquidation-issues)

\[evm\] react components SDK

Wallet connect

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Using the wallet connection component provided by Orderly](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#using-the-wallet-connection-component-provided-by-orderly)
    
*   [Install Wallet Connector Dependencies](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#install-wallet-connector-dependencies)
    
*   [Add the WalletConnectorProvider Component](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#add-the-walletconnectorprovider-component)
    
*   [Wallet Connector Privy](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#wallet-connector-privy)
    
*   [Configuration options](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#configuration-options)
    
*   [Behavior based on customChains:](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#behavior-based-on-customchains)
    
*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#example)
    
*   [Custom wallet connection provider](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#custom-wallet-connection-provider)
    
*   [Interface & Context](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#interface-%26-context)
    
*   [Implementation](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#implementation)
    

`@orderly.network/react-app` is an application layer of the Orderly API. It is mainly responsible for rendering the UI, handling user interaction events, and completing trading actions. Therefore, it does not include built-in wallet connection functionality. In places where wallet connection needs to be handled, `@orderly.network/react-app` utilizes React’s context mechanism to obtain information about the currently connected wallet or call wallet connection functions provided by the context. To facilitate this, `@orderly.network/react-app` defines a context named `WalletConnectorContext`. Any component that implements the `WalletConnectorContext.Provider` defined in `WalletConnectorContextState` can serve as the wallet connection component for the app. Please refer to [this link](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#custom-wallet-connection-provider)
 for more details.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#using-the-wallet-connection-component-provided-by-orderly)

Using the wallet connection component provided by Orderly
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly SDK provides a built-in wallet connection component supporting both EVM and Solana. Here’s how to integrate it:

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#install-wallet-connector-dependencies)

Install Wallet Connector Dependencies

    npm install @orderly.network/wallet-connector
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#add-the-walletconnectorprovider-component)

Add the WalletConnectorProvider Component

Wrap your application with the `WalletConnectorProvider` for wallet connection functionality:

    import { WalletConnectorProvider } from "@orderly.network/wallet-connector";
    import { WalletAdapterNetwork } from "@solana/wallet-adapter-base";
    
    const App: FC<{ children: ReactNode }> = (props) => {
      return (
        <WalletConnectorProvider solanaInitial={{network : WalletAdapterNetwork.Mainnet}}>
          <OrderlyAppProvider
            brokerId="orderly"
            brokerName="Orderly"
            networkId="testnet"
            appIcons={"/logo.svg"} // Path to your app icons
          >
    
            {props.children}
          </OrderlyAppProvider>
        </WalletConnectorProvider>
      );
    };
    

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#wallet-connector-privy)

Wallet Connector Privy
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Privy wallet connector consists of three parts:

*   **Privy**: Provides social login, featuring an injected wallet, EVM wallets, and Solana wallets.
*   **Wagmi**: Offers connectivity for EVM wallets, such as MetaMask and WalletConnect.
*   **Solana**: Provides connectivity for Solana wallets, such as Phantom and Ledger.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#configuration-options)

Configuration options

*   `network` (**required**) - e.g., `mainnet` or `testnet`. This determines the network used by wallets like Solana and Abstract.
*   `customChains` (**optional**) - Builders can define which chains to display.
*   `termsOfUse` (**optional**) - URL for Terms of Use; if not provided, terms section won’t be shown.

    interface WalletConnectorPrivyProps{
      network: Network;
      customChains?: Chains;
      termsOfUse?: string;
      privyConfig?: InitPrivy;
      wagmiConfig?: InitWagmi;
      solanaConfig?: InitSolana;
    }
    

*   If `privyConfig` is not configured, the Privy connector will be disabled.
*   If `wagmiConfig` is not configured, the Wagmi connector will be disabled.
*   If `solanaConfig` is not configured, the Solana connector will be disabled.
*   If `abstractConfig` is not configured, the Abstract Global Wallet connector will be disabled.
*   At least one of `privyConfig`, `wagmiConfig`, or `solanaConfig` must be provided.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#behavior-based-on-customchains)

Behavior based on `customChains`:

*   If only Solana chains are included, Privy will show only the Solana injected wallet and disable the Wagmi connector.
*   If only EVM chains are included, Privy will show only the EVM injected wallet and disable the Solana connector.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#example)

Example

    import WalletConnectorPrivyProvider from "@orderly.network/wallet-connector-privy";
    
    <WalletConnectorPrivyProvider
      termsOfUse="https://learn.woo.org/legal/terms-of-use"
      network={Network.testnet}
      privyConfig={{
        appid: "your privy appid",
        config: {
          appearance: {
            theme: "dark",
            accentColor: "#181C23",
            logo: "/orderly-logo.svg",
          },
        },
      }}
      wagmiConfig={{
        connectors: [\
          wagmiConnectors.injected(),\
          wagmiConnectors.walletConnect({\
            projectId: "your project id",\
            showQrModal: true,\
            storageOptions: {},\
            metadata: {\
              name: "Orderly Network",\
              description: "Orderly Network",\
              url: "https://orderly.network",\
              icons: ["https://oss.orderly.network/static/sdk/chains.png"],\
            },\
          }),\
        ],
      }}
      solanaConfig={{
        mainnetRpc: "",
        devnetRpc: "https://api.devnet.solana.com",
        wallets: wallets,
        onError: (error: WalletError, adapter?: Adapter) => {
          console.log("-- error", error, adapter);
        },
      }}
      abstractConfig={{}}
    >
      <OrderlyAppProvider>
        {props.children}
      </OrderlyAppProvider>
    </WalletConnectorPrivyProvider>
    

* * *

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#custom-wallet-connection-provider)

Custom wallet connection provider
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also customize wallet connections by implementing all the properties defined in `WalletConnectorContextState`. Define the component in the following format:

    import { PropsWithChildren } from "react";
    import { WalletConnectorContext } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    
    export const WalletConnector = ({ children }: PropsWithChildren) => {
      return (
        <WalletConnectorContext.Provider
          value={{
            /*
             * implement all properties defined in WalletConnectorContextState
             */
          }}
        >
          {children}
        </WalletConnectorContext.Provider>
      );
    };
    

After defining the component, you also need to add this component to the parent of [`OrderlyAppProvider`](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-a-global-configuration-provider)
.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#interface-&-context)

Interface & Context

    export type ConnectedChain = {
      id: string;
    };
    
    export interface WalletConnectorContextState {
      connect: () => Promise<any[]>;
      disconnect: (options: any) => Promise<any[]>;
      connecting: boolean;
      setChain: (options: any) => Promise<any>;
      chains: any[];
      switchChain: (options: { chainId: string }) => Promise<any>;
      wallet: any;
      connectedChain: ConnectedChain | null;
      settingChain: boolean;
    }
    
    export const WalletConnectorContext = createContext<WalletConnectorContextState>(
      {} as WalletConnectorContextState
    );
    
    export const useWalletConnector = () => {
      return useContext(WalletConnectorContext);
    };
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/wallet#implementation)

Implementation

The following example demonstrates a wallet connection component implemented using `web3modal`. You can use this example as a reference to implement your own wallet connection component.

    import { WalletConnectorContext, WalletState } from "@orderly.network/hooks";
    import { ChainNamespace } from "@orderly.network/types";
    import { createWeb3Modal, defaultWagmiConfig, useWeb3Modal } from "@web3modal/wagmi/react";
    import { FC, PropsWithChildren, useEffect, useState } from "react";
    import { useAccount as useWagmiAccount } from "wagmi";
    import { arbitrum, mainnet } from "wagmi/chains";
    
    // 1. Get projectId at https://cloud.reown.com/
    const projectId = "YOUR_PROJECT_ID";
    
    // 2. Create wagmiConfig
    const metadata = {
      name: "Web3Modal",
      description: "Web3Modal Example",
      url: "https://web3modal.com",
      icons: ["https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37784886"]
    };
    
    const chains = [mainnet, arbitrum];
    export const wagmiConfig = defaultWagmiConfig({ chains, projectId, metadata });
    
    // 3. Create modal
    createWeb3Modal({ wagmiConfig, projectId });
    
    export const OrderlyProvider: FC<PropsWithChildren> = ({ children }) => {
      const [wallet, setWallet] = useState<WalletState>({
        chains: chains.map((chain) => ({
          namespace: ChainNamespace.evm,
          id: chain.id
        })),
        accounts: [],
        icon: "",
        label: "",
        provider: null
      });
    
      const { open } = useWeb3Modal();
      const { address, isConnecting, chain, connector, status } = useWagmiAccount();
    
      useEffect(() => {
        const run = async () => {
          if (!connector) return;
          setWallet({
            ...wallet,
            accounts: await connector.getAccounts().then((addresses) =>
              addresses.map((addr) => ({
                address: addr
              }))
            ),
            provider: await connector.getProvider(),
            label: (await connector.getClient?.().then((client) => client.name)) ?? ""
          });
        };
        run();
      }, [address, wallet, setWallet, connector]);
    
      return (
        <WalletConnectorContext.Provider
          value={{
            connect: () => {
              return open().then(() => []);
            },
            disconnect: async () => {
              connector?.disconnect();
              return [];
            },
            setChain: async ({ chainId }) => {
              return connector?.switchChain?.({ chainId: Number(chainId) });
            },
            chains,
            connectedChain: chain ? { id: chain.id, namespace: ChainNamespace.evm } : null,
            namespace: ChainNamespace.evm,
            connecting: isConnecting,
            settingChain: status === "reconnecting",
            wallet
          }}
        >
          {children}
        </WalletConnectorContext.Provider>
      );
    };
    

[Theming](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming)
[Next.js](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/next)

⌘I

# Settle PnL - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Core integration flows

Settle PnL

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#example)
    

Any realized and unrealized PnL accrued from open or closed positions needs to be “settled” into the USDC balance of the account. PnL settlement can be triggered at any time — either by other users or by the account owner. To trigger PnL settlement for your own account:

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#)

Get a Settle PnL nonce

Get a nonce from [Get Settle PnL Nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-settle-pnl-nonce)
.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#)

Obtain signature from EIP-712

Sign an EIP-712 message in the following format:

    {
      "brokerId": "woofi_dex",
      "chainId": 80001,
      "settleNonce": 1,
      "timestamp": 1685973017064
    }
    

where:

| **Name** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| brokerId | string | Y   | Builder ID |
| chainId | int | Y   | Chain ID of the connected chain where the message was signed |
| settleNonce | int | Y   | Valid settlement nonce from [Get Settle PnL Nonce](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-settle-pnl-nonce) |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | Current timestamp in UNIX milliseconds |

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#)

Request PnL settlement

Submit the request through [Request PnL Settlement API](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/request-pnl-settlement)
.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/settle-pnl#example)

Example

The example code is very similar to the [Orderly Key registration](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication#full-example)
, except it uses the EIP-712 [on-chain domain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/wallet-authentication#on-chain-domain)
 and the signed message is different.

[Deposit/Withdrawal](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/withdrawal-deposit)
[Order Management](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flows/order-management)

# Orders/position not closing at expected prices - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/wrong-execution-price#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

User issues

Orders/position not closing at expected prices

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

**Issue Description:** Users find that their trading positions do not close at the set limit prices, affecting expected gains or losses. **Troubleshooting Steps:**

*   Confirm that the order was placed correctly and at the intended price.
*   Check for any slippage due to market volatility or liquidity issues.
*   Review the trading history to verify the order execution details.

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/wrong-execution-price#)

First Step

Confirm that the order was placed correctly and at the intended price.

*   If it is a limit order, check if a proper limit price was provided by the user and execution price is:

1.  greater than or equal to limit price for shorts.
2.  lower than or equal to limit price for longs

*   If it is a market order, check if slippage was provided during order placement, otherwise the price execution is not guaranteed. Execution price will have higher slippage on market orders especially during periods of high volatility.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/wrong-execution-price#)

Second Step

If the execution does not match the above, please get in touch.

[Liquidation issues](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/user-issues/liquidation-issues)

⌘I

# Overview - Orderly

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

\[evm\] perp SDK

Overview

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Install](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#install)
    
*   [Usage](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#usage)
    
*   [Function list](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#function-list)
    
*   [account](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#account)
    
*   [positions](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#positions)
    

`@orderly.network/perp` A library of calculation formulas related to Orderly futures trading. This library is designed to be purely functional, with no context dependencies. Simply call the appropriate functions directly for seamless integration.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#install)

Install
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

    npm install @orderly.network/perp
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#usage)

Usage
-------------------------------------------------------------------

    import { maxQty, liqPrice } from "@orderly.network/perp";
    import { OrderSide } from "@orderly.network/types";
    
    // calculation of the maximum allowable position size for an account
    const myMaxQty = () => {
      const qty = maxQty("PERP_ETH_USDC", OrderSide.BUY);
      console.log(qty); //
    };
    
    // calculation of the liquidation price of a position
    const myLiqPrice = () => {
      const price = liqPrice(
        25986.2, //markPrice
        1981.66, // total collateral
        0.2, // positions size
        0.05 //MMR
      );
      console.log(myLiqPrice);
    };
    

If you are a React developer, we recommend utilizing our React Hooks library:[`@orderly.network/hooks`](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/overview)
. It encapsulates all the calculation formulas, and the hook internally manages the required data for computations.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#function-list)

Function list
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#account)

account

*   `IMR` - Calculate the IMR of the account.
*   `availableBalance` - Calculate the available balance of the account.
*   `currentLeverage` - Calculate the current leverage of the account.
*   `freeCollateral` - Calculate the available collateral of the account.
*   `maxQty` - Calculate the maximum quantity the account can open.
*   `totalCollateral` - Calculate the total collateral of the account.
*   `totalInitialMarginWithOrders` - Calculate the total initial margin of the account with orders.
*   `totalMarginRatio` - Calculate the total margin ratio of the account.
*   `totalUnrealizedROI` - Calculate the total unrealized return on investment of the account.
*   `totalValue` - Calculate the total value of the account.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/perp/overview#positions)

positions

*   `MMR` - Calculate the MMR of the position.
*   `liqPrice` - Calculate the liquidation price of the position.
*   `maintenanceMargin` - Calculate the maintenance margin of the position.
*   `totalNotional` - Calculate the total notional value of the position.
*   `unrealizedPnL` - Calculate the unrealized profit and loss of an individual position.
*   `unrealizedPnLROI` - Calculate the unrealized return on investment of an individual position.
*   `totalUnrealizedPnL` - Calculate the total unrealized profit and loss of the positions.
*   `unsettlementPnL` - Calculate the unsettlement profit and loss of an individual position.
*   `totalUnsettlementPnL` - Calculate the total unsettlement profit and loss of the positions.

[Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/core/overview)
[README](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/README)

⌘I

# Authentication - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/authentication#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Websocket API

Authentication

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

{
        "id":"123r",
        "event":"auth",
        "params":{
            "orderly_key":"ed25519:CUS69ZJOXwSV38xo...",
            "sign":"4180da84117fc9753b...",
            "timestamp":1621910107900
        }
    }

{
        "id":"123r",
        "event":"auth",
        "success":true,
        "ts":1621910107315
    }

Refer to [API authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication)
 for more details about how to sign the request with `orderly-key` and `orderly-secret`. The **request method, request path,** and **request body** are all blank. The message to sign is therefore just the **timestamp**. You can authenticate when creating the WebSocket connection by appending the authentication parameters to the subscription URL as query parameters. This eliminates the need to send a separate auth request. For example, `wss://ws-private-evm.orderly.org/v2/ws/private/stream/0xd7379678a303b57d7d43eb23c64dd7528ac8cb4efe983a3aedcfe9b4aa4cb984?orderly_key=xxxxxxxx&timestamp=xxxxx&sign=xxxxx` will authenticate the user when creating the connection, no need to send auth request again.

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| orderly\_key | string | Y   | Orderly Key |
| sign | string | Y   | signature |
| timestamp | timestamp | Y   | timestamp |

    {
        "id":"123r",
        "event":"auth",
        "params":{
            "orderly_key":"ed25519:CUS69ZJOXwSV38xo...",
            "sign":"4180da84117fc9753b...",
            "timestamp":1621910107900
        }
    }
    

    {
        "id":"123r",
        "event":"auth",
        "success":true,
        "ts":1621910107315
    }
    

[PING/PONG](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/ping-pong)
[Error Response](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/error-response)

Navigation

\[evm\] react components SDK

Getting started

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Add Basic Dependencies](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-basic-dependencies)
    
*   [Install the Base Component Libraries](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#install-the-base-component-libraries)
    
*   [Add Styles to Your Project](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-styles-to-your-project)
    
*   [Add a Global Configuration Provider](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-a-global-configuration-provider)
    
*   [Add Wallet Connection](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-wallet-connection)
    
*   [Install Wallet Connector Dependencies](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#install-wallet-connector-dependencies)
    
*   [Add the WalletConnectorProvider Component](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-the-walletconnectorprovider-component)
    
*   [Add Pages](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-pages)
    
*   [Page Layout](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#page-layout)
    
*   [Add the Scaffold component to your application:](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-the-scaffold-component-to-your-application)
    
*   [Page Components](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#page-components)
    

Follow the steps below to integrate the Orderly SDK into your project:

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-basic-dependencies)

Add Basic Dependencies
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#install-the-base-component-libraries)

Install the Base Component Libraries

    npm install @orderly.network/ui @orderly.network/react-app
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-styles-to-your-project)

Add Styles to Your Project

Reference the style file by adding the following code to your project:

    import "@orderly.network/ui/dist/styles.css";
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-a-global-configuration-provider)

Add a Global Configuration Provider

Wrap your application with the `OrderlyAppProvider` component to set global configurations:

    import React, { FC, ReactNode } from "react";
    import { OrderlyAppProvider } from "@orderly.network/react-app";
    
    const App: FC<{ children: ReactNode }> = (props) => {
      return (
        <OrderlyAppProvider
          brokerId="orderly"
          brokerName="Orderly"
          networkId="testnet"
          appIcons={"/logo.svg"} // Path to your app icons
        >
    
          {props.children}
        </OrderlyAppProvider>
      );
    };
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-wallet-connection)

Add Wallet Connection

Orderly SDK provides a built-in wallet connection component supporting both EVM and Solana. Here’s how to integrate it:

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#install-wallet-connector-dependencies)

Install Wallet Connector Dependencies

    npm install @orderly.network/wallet-connector
    

#### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-the-walletconnectorprovider-component)

Add the WalletConnectorProvider Component

Wrap your application with the `WalletConnectorProvider` for wallet connection functionality:

    import { WalletConnectorProvider } from "@orderly.network/wallet-connector";
    import { WalletAdapterNetwork } from "@solana/wallet-adapter-base";
    
    const App: FC<{ children: ReactNode }> = (props) => {
      return (
        <WalletConnectorProvider solanaInitial={{network: WalletAdapterNetwork.Mainnet }}>
          <OrderlyAppProvider
            brokerId="orderly"
            brokerName="Orderly"
            networkId="testnet"
            appIcons={"/logo.svg"} // Path to your app icons
          >
    
            {props.children}
          </OrderlyAppProvider>
        </WalletConnectorProvider>
      );
    };
    

Alternatively, you can integrate third-party wallet libraries by implementing the `WalletConnectorContextState` interface from `@orderly.network/hooks`.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-pages)

Add Pages
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#page-layout)

Page Layout

If you want to use the SDK’s built-in top navigation, side navigation, and other layout features, install the layout library:

    npm install @orderly.network/ui-scaffold
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#add-the-scaffold-component-to-your-application)

Add the Scaffold component to your application:

    import Scaffold from "@orderly.network/ui-scaffold";
    
    <Scaffold
      mainNavProps={
        {
          // Please refer to the example code and configure as needed.
        }
      }
      footerProps={{}}
      routerAdapter={{
        onRouteChange, //Handle user click events on the navigation menu.
        currentPath: "/"
      }}
    >
      {/* page component */}
      {children}
    </Scaffold>;
    

For certain page components, the SDK also provides ready-made layout components. For example, you can directly import the layout for `@orderly.network/portfolio`.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/getting_started#page-components)

Page Components

The SDK includes the following page components: Trading - `@orderly.network/trading` Portfolio - `@orderly.network/portfolio` Referral - `@orderly.network/referral` Markets - `@orderly.network/markets` Trading Rewards - `@orderly.network/trading-rewards` Affiliate - `@orderly.network/affiliate` You can integrate these with your preferred React routing framework. Here are examples for popular frameworks:

*   [Next.js](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-js-sdk-nextjs-template)
    
*   [CRA](https://github.com/OrderlyNetwork/orderly-js-sdk-cra-template)
    

For detailed usage, refer to the [Orderly Storybook](https://storybook.orderly.network/)
.

[Overview](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/overview)
[Theming](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/react/theming)

# Introduction - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

API fundamentals

Introduction

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Base Endpoints](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#base-endpoints)
    
*   [REST Request Conventions](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#rest-request-conventions)
    
*   [Authorization](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#authorization)
    
*   [Symbol Format](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#symbol-format)
    
*   [Rate Limit](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#rate-limit)
    
*   [Error Message](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#error-message)
    

Orderly provides two interfaces for client communication:

*   [**RESTful API**](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#restful-api)
    : For actions such as creating/canceling orders, fetching balances, and managing accounts.
*   [**WebSocket API**](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#websocket-api)
    : For real-time orderbook data feeds and order update feeds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#base-endpoints)

Base Endpoints
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Environment | URL |
| --- | --- |
| **Mainnet** | `https://api.orderly.org/` |
| **Testnet** | `https://testnet-api.orderly.org` |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#rest-request-conventions)

REST Request Conventions
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `GET` and `DELETE`: parameters must be sent as a **query string**.
*   `POST` and `PUT`: parameters must be sent in the **request body** with content type `application/json`.
*   Parameters may be sent in any order.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#authorization)

Authorization
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All private endpoints require signing via cryptographically secure keys (Orderly Key). Set the corresponding headers in your request — see [API Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication)
 for details.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#symbol-format)

Symbol Format
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Orderly uses the format `PERP_<SYMBOL>_USDC` for symbol names, e.g. `PERP_ETH_USDC`.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#rate-limit)

Rate Limit
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rate limits are counted per Orderly Key. If your application exceeds the rate limit for an endpoint, the server will return HTTP `429`. Wait until the next time window before retrying.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/introduction#error-message)

Error Message
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Errors consist of three parts: an error code, a detail message, and a success flag.

    {
      "success": false,
      "code": -1005,
      "message": "order_price must be a positive number"
    }
    

All APIs return this format on failure. The `message` field contains the specific error detail. See [Error Codes](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/error-codes)
 for the full list.

[User Flow Walkthrough](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/user-flow-walkthrough)
[API Authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication)

⌘I

Fee settings

Update user fee rate

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Update user fee rate

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/broker/fee_rate/set \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "maker_fee_rate": 123,
      "taker_fee_rate": 123,
      "account_ids": [\
        "<string>"\
      ],
      "rwa_taker_fee_rate": 123,
      "rwa_maker_fee_rate": 123
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

broker

/

fee\_rate

/

set

Try it

Update user fee rate

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/broker/fee_rate/set \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "maker_fee_rate": 123,
      "taker_fee_rate": 123,
      "account_ids": [\
        "<string>"\
      ],
      "rwa_taker_fee_rate": 123,
      "rwa_maker_fee_rate": 123
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#body-maker-fee-rate)

maker\_fee\_rate

number

required

Maker fee charged to users by default (0.0001 = 0.01%)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#body-taker-fee-rate)

taker\_fee\_rate

number

required

Taker fee charged to users by default (0.0001 = 0.01%)

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#body-account-ids)

account\_ids

string\[\]

required

List of account\_ids for which the fees need to be changed

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#body-rwa-taker-fee-rate)

rwa\_taker\_fee\_rate

number

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#body-rwa-maker-fee-rate)

rwa\_maker\_fee\_rate

number

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-user-fee-rate#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/privacy-policy#content-area)

Security, legal & FAQ

Privacy Policy

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
