Delegate signer settle PnL

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/delegate_settle_pnl \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "delegateContract": "<string>",
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "settleNonce": 123,
        "timestamp": 123
      }
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "settle_pnl_id": 1
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

delegate\_settle\_pnl

Try it

Delegate signer settle PnL

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/delegate_settle_pnl \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "delegateContract": "<string>",
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "settleNonce": 123,
        "timestamp": 123
      }
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "settle_pnl_id": 1
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#body-signature)

signature

string

required

Signature generated by signing the message object.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#body-user-address)

userAddress

string

required

Address of the wallet that signed the message object.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#body-verifying-contract)

verifyingContract

string

required

Contract address used to verify the wallet signature.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-settle-pnl#body-message)

message

object

required

Message object signed by the wallet owner.

Show child attributes

#### Response

Delegate signer

Delegate signer withdraw request

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Delegate signer withdraw request

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/delegate_withdraw_request \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "delegateContract": "<string>",
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "receiver": "<string>",
        "token": "<string>",
        "amount": "<string>",
        "withdrawNonce": "<string>",
        "timestamp": "<string>"
      }
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "withdraw_id": 123
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

delegate\_withdraw\_request

Try it

Delegate signer withdraw request

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/delegate_withdraw_request \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "delegateContract": "<string>",
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "receiver": "<string>",
        "token": "<string>",
        "amount": "<string>",
        "withdrawNonce": "<string>",
        "timestamp": "<string>"
      }
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "withdraw_id": 123
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#body-signature)

signature

string

required

The signature generated by signing the message object via EIP-712

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#body-user-address)

userAddress

string

required

The address of the wallet signing the message object via EIP-712

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#body-verifying-contract)

verifyingContract

string

required

Contract address used to verify the wallet signature.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#body-message)

message

object

required

Message object signed by the wallet owner.

Show child attributes

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#response-data)

data

object

required

Show child attributes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/delegate-signer-withdraw-request#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

# Low-level API - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

API requests

Low-level API

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [RESTful API](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#restful-api)
    
*   [Private APIs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#private-apis)
    
*   [WebSocket](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#websocket)
    

Orderly provides both public and private APIs. Public APIs can be fetched directly. Private APIs require [authentication](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/api-authentication)
 and these can be performed using hooks from `@orderly.network/hooks`.

**Reminder:** Most often you might not have to use the following hooks, as `@orderly.network/hooks` likely implements higher level hooks on top of the request module. For example, you can use `usePositionStream` for position management, and `useOrderEntry` for order placement. You may consider to use these hooks if you want to build out more advanced usage of Orderly, or if the provided hooks are not suitable for your use case.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#restful-api)

RESTful API
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`@orderly.network/hooks` provides the following hooks for builders to access Orderly’s RESTful APIs:

*   [useQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-query)
     - `GET` Public API
*   [usePrivateQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-private-query)
     - `GET` Private API
*   [usePrivateInfiniteQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-private-infinite-query)
     - A version of `usePrivateQuery` that supports infinite scrolling
*   [useLazyQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-lazy-query)
    
*   [useMutation](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-mutation)
     - `POST` APIs
*   [useWS](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-ws)
     - WebSocket client

The above hooks are based on [SWR](https://swr.vercel.app/)
 and have the following properties:

*   Request responses are cached. Cached data are used in priority according to the config, and is used as a global status management.
*   Removes any duplicate requests. When request parameters are the same, it will automatically cancel the duplicate request
*   When the window is focused or when the connection is recovered, automatically retries requests.
*   Automatically retries any failed network requests.
*   For more information, please check the [official swr documentation](https://swr.vercel.app/)
    .

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#private-apis)

Private APIs

Use `usePrivateQuery`, `usePrivateInfiniteQuery` or `useMutation` to access any private API. These are slightly different from `useQuery`:

*   They have a built-in account status check. Developers do not have to check the current login status of the user. If the user has not logged in, the request will not be sent until the user status has changed to a logged in status.
*   They will automatically sign all requests using the `OrderlyKey` according to Orderly’s standards.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api#websocket)

WebSocket
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

`@orderly.network/hooks` provides a `useWS` hook for accessing Orderly’s Websocket API. `useWS` returns the `WS` instance of `@orderly.network/net`. Builders can use `WS` to subscribe, cancel, or to send messages through the Websocket. For all available topics of the Websocket, please check the [Websocket API documentation](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/introduction)
.

[Setup](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/setup)
[useQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-query)

⌘I

Get Strategy Vault Info

cURL

    curl --request GET \
      --url https://api-sv.orderly.org/v1/public/strategy_vault/vault/info

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989,
      "data": {
        "rows": [\
          {\
            "vault_id": "",\
            "vault_address": "",\
            "vault_type": "protocol",\
            "vault_name": "Orderly OmniVault",\
            "description": "Earn passive yields effortlessly, no trading expertise required. Deposit...",\
            "sp_address": "xxxxxxx",\
            "sp_name": "yyyy",\
            "asset": "USDC",\

            "status": "live",\
            "vault_start_time": 1702989203989,\
            "performance_fee_rate": 0.3,\

              {\
                "chain_id": "1",\
                "chain_name": "Ethereum"\
              },\
              {\
                "chain_id": "42161",\
                "chain_name": "Arbitrum"\
              },\
              {\
                "chain_id": "8453",\
                "chain_name": "Base"\
              },\
              {\
                "chain_id": "10",\
                "chain_name": "Optimism"\
              },\
              {\
                "chain_id": "137",\
                "chain_name": "Polygon"\
              }\
            ],\
            "tvl": 123456789,\
            "valid_hpr": 30,\
            "30d_apy": 0.24,\
            "recovery_30d_apy": 0,\
            "vault_lifetime_net_pnl": 123456,\
            "lp_counts": 1204,\
            "total_main_shares": 123456789,\
            "lifetime_apy": 0.21,\
            "lock_duration": 48,\

            "min_withdrawal_amount": 10,\
            "gate_threshold_pct": 0.1,\

          }\

    }

#### Query Parameters

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/strategy-vault/vault/public/vault-info#parameter-vault-id)

vault\_id

string

return all if empty

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/strategy-vault/vault/public/vault-info#parameter-status)

status

string

return all if no filter

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/strategy-vault/vault/public/vault-info#parameter-broker-ids)

broker\_ids

string

can filter multiple broker\_id per query.

200 - application/json

success

boolean

`true`

timestamp

number

Example:

`1702989203989`

data

object

Show child attributes

# useTPSL - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Orders

useTPSL

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Create TP/SL Orders](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#create-tp%2Fsl-orders)
    
*   [Update TP/SL Order](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#update-tp%2Fsl-order)
    
*   [Method One](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#method-one)
    
*   [Method Two](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#method-two)
    
*   [Cancel TP/SL Order](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-tp%2Fsl-order)
    
*   [Cancel all Algo orders, including POSITIONAL\_TP\_SL and TP\_SL orders;](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-all-algo-orders-including-positional-tp-sl-and-tp-sl-orders)
    
*   [Cancel TP or SL order](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-tp-or-sl-order)
    
*   [Filtering TP/SL Orders](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#filtering-tp%2Fsl-orders)
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#create-tp/sl-orders)

Create TP/SL Orders
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const [\
      /**\
       * Formatted TP/SL order data ready for UI display\
       * The order includes the following data:\
       * [tp/sl]_pnl: Estimated takeProfit profit\
       * [tp/sl]_offset: takeProfit \
       * [tp/sl]_offset_percentage\
       * quantity\
       * ...\
       */\
      ComputedAlgoOrder,\
      {\
        /**\
         * Update the take profit and stop loss order, this will merge the new data with the old one\
         * key values can be: quantity|tp_trigger_price|sl_trigger_price|tp_pnl|sl_pnl|tp_offset|sl_offset|tp_offset_percentage|sl_offset_percentage\
         * \
         * If you want to set the trigger price, you can use one of the following methods:\
         * 1. Directly set triggerPrice:\
         * setValue('tp_trigger_price',60000)\
         * 2. Set PnL, automatically calculate trigger_price:\
         * setValue('tp_pnl',100)\
         * 3. Set price offset, automatically calculate trigger_price:\
         * setValue('tp_offset', 100)\
         * 4. Set price offset percentage, automatically calculate trigger_price\
         * setValue('tp_offset_percentage',10)\
         * \
         * Using any of the above methods to set the trigger price will calculate the corresponding pnl/offset/offset_percentage, which will be returned in the order object\
         * \
         * When setting stop loss order, replace `tp` with `sl`\
         */\
        setValue: (key: string, value: number | string) => void;\
        setValues: (values: Partial<ComputedAlgoOrder>) => void;\
        \
        /**\
         * Submit the TP/SL order\
         */\
        submit: () => Promise<void>;\
        /**\
         * Order data validation result\
         */\
        errors: ValidateError | null;\
        /**\
         * You can manually call this method to validate the Order data. If validation passes, the order data will be returned, otherwise an error will be thrown\
         */\
        validate: () => Promise<\
          AlgoOrderEntity<\
            AlgoOrderRootType.POSITIONAL_TP_SL | AlgoOrderRootType.TP_SL\
          >\
        >;\
      }\
    ] = useTPSLOrder(
      /**
      * Position of a specific trading pair currently held by the user, must include "symbol" | "average_open_price" | "position_qty" fields;
      */
      position: Partial<API.PositionTPSLExt> &
        Pick<API.PositionTPSLExt, "symbol" | "average_open_price" | "position_qty">,
      options?: {
        /**
         * Default order data, required if modifying TP/SL order
        */
        defaultOrder?: API.AlgoOrder;
      }
    )
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#update-tp/sl-order)

Update TP/SL Order
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#method-one)

Method One

    const [ComputedAlgoOrder,{submit}] = useTPSLOrder(position,{
      defaultOrder,// Must pass in order data
    })
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#method-two)

Method Two

Using `useOrderStream` hook

    const [_, {updateTPSLOrder}] = useOrderStream();
    updateTPSLOrder(orderId, childOrders);
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-tp/sl-order)

Cancel TP/SL Order
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

\*\* Using useOrderStream \*\*

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-all-algo-orders-including-positional-tp-sl-and-tp-sl-orders)

Cancel all Algo orders, including POSITIONAL\_TP\_SL and TP\_SL orders;

    const [_, {cancelAllTPSLOrders}] = useOrderStream()
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#cancel-tp-or-sl-order)

Cancel TP or SL order

    const [_, {cancelTPSLChildOrder}] = useOrderStream();
    
    cancelTPSLChildOrder(childOrderId, rootAlgoOrderId);
    

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-tp-sl#filtering-tp/sl-orders)

Filtering TP/SL Orders
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const [orders] = useOrderStream({
      includes:[AlgoOrderRootType.TP_SL, AlgoOrderRootType.POSITIONAL_TP_SL], // Show only TP/SL orders
      //excludes:[AlgoOrderRootType.TP_SL, AlgoOrderRootType.POSITIONAL_TP_SL],// Do not show TP/SL orders
    })
    

[useOrderStream](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/orders/use-order-stream)
[usePositionStream](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/positions/use-position-stream)

⌘I

Update leverage setting

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)

Update leverage setting

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/client/leverages \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "leverage": 123,
      "symbol": "PERP_BTC_USDC",
      "margin_mode": "CROSS"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989,
      "data": {
        "symbol": "PERP_BTC_USDC",
        "margin_mode": "ISOLATED",
        "leverage": 19
      }
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

client

/

leverages

Try it

Update leverage setting

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/client/leverages \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-leverage-setting#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-leverage-setting#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-leverage-setting#parameter-orderly-key)

# Liquidations - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Perpetual futures basics

Liquidations

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [When does liquidation happen?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#when-does-liquidation-happen)
    
*   [How Orderly’s decentralized liquidations work](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#how-orderly%E2%80%99s-decentralized-liquidations-work)
    
*   [What triggers a liquidation?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#what-triggers-a-liquidation)
    
*   [Liquidation Decision Flow](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-decision-flow)
    
*   [Liquidation Tiers](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-tiers)
    
*   [How much gets liquidated?](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#how-much-gets-liquidated)
    
*   [Liquidation fees](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-fees)
    
*   [Full vs. partial liquidator takeover](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#full-vs-partial-liquidator-takeover)
    

Liquidation is the process of closing out a trader’s positions when their account no longer has enough margin to support them. Think of it as a safety valve: it prevents one trader’s losses from growing so large that they affect other users on the platform. This page explains when liquidation happens, how it works on Orderly, and what fees are involved.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#when-does-liquidation-happen)

When does liquidation happen?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An account is subject to liquidation if its Account Margin Ratio falls below its Maintenance Margin Ratio. In simple terms, if the value backing your positions shrinks too much relative to the size of those positions, the system steps in. Orderly uses the [Mark Price](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/mark-price-index-price-and-last-price#mark-price)
 to evaluate positions, not the last traded price. Because the Mark Price is derived from multiple reputable spot exchanges (see [Index Price](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/mark-price-index-price-and-last-price#index-price)
) and the funding rate, it is more stable than the [Last Price](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/mark-price-index-price-and-last-price#last-price)
. This makes it much harder for bad actors to manipulate the market price just to trigger liquidations. When an account enters liquidation, two things happen immediately:

1.  All open orders on the account are cancelled.
2.  The account’s USDC balance is frozen.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#how-orderly%E2%80%99s-decentralized-liquidations-work)

How Orderly’s decentralized liquidations work
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

On most centralized exchanges (CEXes), liquidations work by force-selling positions directly into the order book. This often causes a chain reaction: the forced selling pushes the price down further, which triggers more liquidations, which pushes the price down even more. This cascading effect hurts traders and creates opportunities for market manipulation. Orderly does things differently. Instead of dumping positions onto the order book, Orderly transfers them to **liquidators** at a discount. Anyone with an Orderly account can act as a liquidator, as long as they have enough margin to take over the positions. This approach is fully decentralized and avoids the cascading liquidation problem. Further details on how to claim liquidating positions can be found in the API docs.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#what-triggers-a-liquidation)

What triggers a liquidation?
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-decision-flow)

Liquidation Decision Flow
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The liquidation process follows a strict hierarchy of steps to ensure system stability.

1

[](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#)

Detection

Orderly’s risk engine detects that the **Account Margin Ratio (AMR)** has fallen below the **Maintenance Margin Ratio (MMR)** required for the account.

2

[](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#)

Freeze & Cancel

*   All open orders for the account are cancelled immediately.
*   All account actions (withdrawals, transfers) are frozen.

3

[](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#)

Liquidation Calculation

The system determines how many positions need to be transferred to bring the AMR back to the **Initial Margin Ratio (IMR)**. It groups positions into **Low Tier (BTC/ETH)** or **High Tier (others)** for liquidators to claim.

4

[](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#)

Transfer & Settlement

Liquidators claim the positions at a discount. If the liquidated account still has a positive balance, the fees are split between the liquidator and the **Insurance Fund**.

5

[](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#)

Insurance Fund Coverage (If Bankrupt)

If the account’s total value is less than zero (bankrupt), or insufficient to pay the liquidator:

*   The liquidator is still paid the **Liquidator Fee** from the Insurance Fund.
*   The Insurance Fund absorbs the remaining debt and takes over any un-liquidated positions.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-tiers)

Liquidation Tiers
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Not all trading pairs carry the same risk. Orderly groups them into two tiers, which affects how liquidators can claim positions:

*   **Low Tier Risk:** Liquidators must claim a proportional share of all low-tier positions. They cannot cherry-pick just one symbol.
*   **High Tier Risk:** Liquidators can claim a single symbol at a time.

| Tier | Perp Markets |
| --- | --- |
| Low | BTC ; ETH |
| High | Others |

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#how-much-gets-liquidated)

How much gets liquidated?
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The system calculates how much of a position needs to be liquidated for both low-tier and each high-tier symbol. The goal is to liquidate just enough so that the Account Margin Ratio comes back up to the Initial Margin Ratio. In other words, the system tries to do the minimum necessary rather than wiping out the whole account. If the account has multiple positions in the low-tier group, each position is partially liquidated in proportion to its size (volume-weighted).

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#liquidation-fees)

Liquidation fees
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When an account is liquidated, a fee is charged. This fee is split between the Orderly **Insurance Fund** and the **liquidator** who takes over the positions. The fee rates depend on which market the position is in:

| Perp Market | Liquidation Fee | Liquidator Fee |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BTC | 0.60% | 0.30% |
| ETH | 0.60% | 0.30% |
| SOL | 0.60% | 0.30% |
| Others | 1.20% | 0.60% |

Here is how the total fees are calculated: **Low Tier Liquidation** (multiple symbols liquidated together):

    User Liquidation Fee = Sum[Liquidation Fee(Symbol i) * Abs(Notional i)]
    Liquidator Fee = Sum[Liquidator Fee(Asset i) * Abs(Notional i)]
    

**High Tier Liquidation** (one symbol at a time):

    User Liquidation Fee = Liquidation Fee(Symbol i) * Abs(Notional i)
    Liquidator Fee = Sum[Liquidator Fee(Asset i) * Abs(Notional i)]
    

How the fee is split depends on how much margin the liquidated account has left:

*   **Plenty of margin remaining:** If the Account Margin Ratio is higher than the User Liquidation Fee, the fee is split evenly — the Insurance Fund and the liquidator each receive `0.5 * User Liquidation Fee`.
*   **Low margin remaining:** If the Account Margin Ratio is between the User Liquidation Fee and the Liquidator Fee, the liquidator still gets `0.5 * User Liquidation Fee`, and whatever is left goes to the Insurance Fund.
*   **Almost no margin remaining:** If the remaining margin cannot even cover the Liquidator Fee, the account’s entire balance and positions are transferred to the Insurance Fund. Liquidators can still claim those positions later (see [Insurance Fund & ADL](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/insurance-fund-and-adl)
    ).

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/liquidations#full-vs-partial-liquidator-takeover)

Full vs. partial liquidator takeover
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Liquidators do not always have to take over the entire position. Whether they can take a partial amount depends on the size of the position:

*   **Below the threshold:** For low-tier liquidations under 10,000 USDC notional, the liquidator must take over all positions. For high-tier liquidations under 5,000 USDC notional, the liquidator must claim the entire position for that symbol.
*   **Above the threshold:** The liquidator can choose to take over just a percentage of the positions, as long as the notional they take exceeds the threshold.

Before any transfer happens, Orderly checks that the liquidator actually has enough margin to absorb the positions, using the same margin checks that apply to new order placements.

[Funding Rate](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/funding-rate)
[Insurance Fund & ADL](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/trade-on-orderly/perpetual-futures/insurance-fund-and-adl)

⌘I

Configure auto-cancel during maintenance

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/client/maintenance_config \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "maintenance_cancel_order_flag": true
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "success_alter": "Success! Your change has been saved!"
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

OK

Navigation

Order management

Create order

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)

[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Create order

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/order \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "order_type": "<string>",
      "side": "<string>",
      "client_order_id": "<string>",
      "order_price": 123,
      "order_quantity": 123,
      "order_amount": 123,
      "visible_quantity": 123,
      "reduce_only": true,
      "slippage": 123,
      "order_tag": "<string>",
      "level": 123,
      "post_only_adjust": true,
      "margin_mode": "CROSS"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "order_id": 13,
        "client_order_id": "testclientid",
        "order_type": "LIMIT",
        "order_price": 100.12,
        "order_quantity": 0.987654,
        "error_message": "none"
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

order

Try it

Create order

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/order \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "order_type": "<string>",
      "side": "<string>",
      "client_order_id": "<string>",
      "order_price": 123,
      "order_quantity": 123,
      "order_amount": 123,
      "visible_quantity": 123,
      "reduce_only": true,
      "slippage": 123,
      "order_tag": "<string>",
      "level": 123,
      "post_only_adjust": true,
      "margin_mode": "CROSS"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "order_id": 13,
        "client_order_id": "testclientid",
        "order_type": "LIMIT",
        "order_price": 100.12,
        "order_quantity": 0.987654,
        "error_message": "none"
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#parameter-x-recv-window)

x-recv-window

number

Use this parameter to control the timeout threshold for placing order, unit in miliseconds

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-symbol)

symbol

string

required

Example:

`"PERP_ETH_USDC"`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-order-type)

order\_type

string

required

`LIMIT`/`MARKET`/`IOC`/`FOK`/`POST_ONLY`/`ASK`/`BID`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-side)

side

string

required

`SELL`/`BUY`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-client-order-id)

client\_order\_id

string

36 length, accepts hyphen but cannot be the first character, default: null

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-order-price)

order\_price

number

If order\_type is MARKET/ASK/BID, then is not required, otherwise this parameter is required.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-order-quantity)

order\_quantity

number

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-order-amount)

order\_amount

number

For MARKET/ASK/BID order, the order size in terms of quote currency

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-visible-quantity)

visible\_quantity

number

The order quantity shown on orderbook. (default: equal to order\_quantity) Visible quantity is not supported for post-only orders.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-reduce-only)

reduce\_only

boolean

Default false

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-slippage)

slippage

number

`MARKET` orders beyond this slippage will not be executed

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-order-tag)

order\_tag

string

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-level)

level

number

Integer value from `0` to `4`. This parameter controls wether to present the price of bid0 to bid4 or ask0 to ask4. Only allowed when `order_type` is `BID` or `ASK`.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-post-only-adjust)

post\_only\_adjust

boolean

If set to true, then price will be adjusted to 1 tick close to current best price. Only supported for `POST_ONLY` type orders

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#body-margin-mode)

margin\_mode

enum<string>

Margin mode for the order. Default: the symbol's current default margin mode.

Available options:

`CROSS`,

`ISOLATED`

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#response-data)

data

object

required

Show child attributes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-order#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

[Settle sub account PnL](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/settle-sub-account-pnl)
[Batch create order](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/batch-create-order)

⌘I

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)

Add Orderly key

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/orderly_key \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --data '
    {
      "message": {
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "orderlyKey": "<string>",
        "scope": "<string>",
        "timestamp": 123,
        "expiration": 123,
        "chainType": "<string>",
        "tag": "<string>",
        "subAccountId": "<string>"
      },
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "orderly_key": "ed25519:FRXntsPJBCy6dzKv9WPw4eYSw3rKU9Npz3T6UmvvJc9Z",
        "id": 123
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

orderly\_key

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/add-orderly-key#body-message)

message

object

required

Message object containing the message that is signed by the wallet owner

Show child attributes

string

required

The signature generated by signing the message object via EIP-712

userAddress

string

required

The address of the wallet signing the message object via EIP-712

OK

success

boolean

required

data

object

required

timestamp

integer

Example:

`1702989203989`

    }
    '

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

v1

/

broker

fee\_rate

# Add or reduce position margin - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Account config

Add or reduce position margin

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Add or reduce position margin

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/position_margin \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "amount": "3",
      "type": "ADD"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

position\_margin

Try it

Add or reduce position margin

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/position_margin \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "symbol": "PERP_ETH_USDC",
      "amount": "3",
      "type": "ADD"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#body-symbol)

symbol

string

required

Symbol of the isolated position

Example:

`"PERP_ETH_USDC"`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#body-amount)

amount

string

required

Amount of margin to add or reduce

Example:

`"3"`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#body-type)

type

enum<string>

required

Operation type

Available options:

`ADD`,

`REDUCE`

Example:

`"ADD"`

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/add-or-reduce-position-margin#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

[Update margin mode](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/update-margin-mode)
[Get max leverage setting](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/public/get-max-leverage-setting)

Fee settings

Reset user fee rate

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)

[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Reset user fee rate

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/broker/fee_rate/set_default \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "account_ids": [\
        "<string>"\
      ]
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

broker

/

fee\_rate

/

set\_default

Try it

Reset user fee rate

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/broker/fee_rate/set_default \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "account_ids": [\
        "<string>"\
      ]
    }
    '

200

      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/reset-user-fee-rate#body-account-ids)

account\_ids

string\[\]

required

List of account\_ids for which the fees need to be changed

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

boolean

required

Example:

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

# Get one position info - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-one-position-info#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Positions

Get one position info

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Get one position info

cURL

    curl --request GET \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/position/{symbol} \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>'

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {

# useLocalStorage - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Utility

useLocalStorage

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage#example)
    
*   [Using with different data types](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage#using-with-different-data-types)
    

Utility hook to store values in browser’s local storage.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#uselocalstorage)
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage#example)

Example

    const [needConfirm, setNeedConfirm] = useLocalStorage("CONFIRM_ORDER", true);
    
    return (
      <>
        {/* ... */}
        <label>
          Confirm Order
          <input
            type="checkbox"
            checked={needConfirm}
            onChange={() => {
              setNeedConfirm(!needConfirm);
            }}
          />
        </label>
      </>
    );
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage#using-with-different-data-types)

Using with different data types

    type ChartConfig = {
      timeframe: string;
      indicators: string[];
      theme: "light" | "dark";
    };
    
    const [chartConfig, setChartConfig] = useLocalStorage<ChartConfig>("CHART_CONFIG", {
      timeframe: "1h",
      indicators: ["MA", "RSI"],
      theme: "light"
    });
    
    const toggleTheme = () => {
      setChartConfig((prev) => ({
        ...prev,
        theme: prev.theme === "light" ? "dark" : "light"
      }));
    };
    
    return (
      <button onClick={toggleTheme}>
        Switch to {chartConfig.theme === "light" ? "dark" : "light"} theme
      </button>
    );
    

[useEventEmitter](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-event-emitter)
[useSessionStorage](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-session-storage)

⌘I

# useEventEmitter - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-event-emitter#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Utility

useEventEmitter

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-event-emitter#example)
    

Utility hook that lets you emit events in one Component and receive them elsewhere.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#useeventemitter)
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-event-emitter#example)

Example

Emitter.tsx

Receiver.tsx

    const eventEmitter = useEventEmitter();
    const [state, setState] = useState();
    
    useEffect(() => {
      eventEmitter.emit("state:update", state);
    }, [state]);
    

[useConfig](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-config)
[useLocalStorage](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-local-storage)

⌘I

Create withdraw request

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/withdraw_request \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>' \
      --data '
    {
      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "receiver": "<string>",
        "token": "<string>",
        "amount": "<string>",
        "withdrawNonce": "<string>",
        "timestamp": "<string>",
        "chainType": "<string>",
        "allowCrossChainWithdraw": true
      }
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "withdraw_id": 123
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

POST

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

withdraw\_request

Try it

Create withdraw request

cURL

    curl --request POST \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/withdraw_request \
      --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \

      --data '

      "signature": "<string>",
      "userAddress": "<string>",
      "verifyingContract": "<string>",
      "message": {
        "brokerId": "<string>",
        "chainId": 123,
        "receiver": "<string>",
        "token": "<string>",
        "amount": "<string>",
        "withdrawNonce": "<string>",
        "timestamp": "<string>",
        "chainType": "<string>",
        "allowCrossChainWithdraw": true

200

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#body-signature)

signature

string

required

The signature generated by signing the message object via EIP-712

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#body-user-address)

userAddress

string

required

The address of the wallet signing the message object via EIP-712

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#body-verifying-contract)

verifyingContract

string

required

Contract address used to verify the wallet signature.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#body-message)

message

object

required

Message object signed by the wallet owner.

Show child attributes

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#response-data)

data

object

required

Show child attributes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/create-withdraw-request#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-media-query#example)

Example

    const isTablet = useMediaQuery("(max-width: 768px)");
    
    return <div>{isTablet ? "is phone or tablet" : "is desktop"}</div>;

# useConfig - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-config#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Utility

useConfig

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-config#example)
    

This hook allows to return various configuration values.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#useconfig)
    

Possible keys:

    export type ConfigKey =
      | "apiBaseUrl"
      | "klineDataUrl"
      | "privateWsUrl"
      | "publicWsUrl"
      | "operatorUrl"
      | "domain"
      | "brokerId"
      | "networkId"
      | "env"
      | "PROD_URL"
      | "markets";
    

If no key is provided, then the entire `ConfigStore` will be returned.

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-config#example)

Example

    // get specific config
    const networkId = useConfig("networkId");
    
    useEffect(() => {
      if (networkId === "testnet") {
        // do something
      }
    }, [networkId]);
    

[Utility](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/util)
[useEventEmitter](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/util/use-event-emitter)

⌘I

Navigation

Referral code management (legacy)

Get referral code info

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)

[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Get referral code info

cURL

    curl --request GET \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/referral/admin_info \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>'

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "data": {
        "rows": [\
          {\
            "account_id": "0x114f583aea808c5812e16109b6c087660285a26f0a31dcc0f9b54f1a6a0fefbd",\
            "user_address": "0xa8ec6970fec8842dd229eef170f624c3d77dab45",\
            "number_of_codes": "1",\
            "total_invites": 0.03,\
            "total_traded": 0.01,\
            "referee_volume": 0.01,\
            "referee_fee": 0.01,\
            "orderly_fee": 1111,\
            "referral_codes": [\
              {\
                "code": "C4",\
                "max_rebate_rate": 0.4,\
                "referrer_rebate_rate": 0.3,\
                "referee_rebate_rate": 0.1,\
                "total_referrer_rebate": 123.456,\
                "total_referee_rebate": 123.456\
              }\
            ]\
          }\
        ],
        "meta": {
          "records_per_page": 25,
          "current_page": 1,
          "total": 50
        }
      },
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

GET

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

/

v1

/

referral

/

admin\_info

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Query Parameters

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-page)

page

string

start from 1

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-size)

size

string

Only one of `user_address` and `account_id` can be provided

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-user-address)

user\_address

string

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-account-id)

account\_id

string

Only one of `user_address` and `account_id` can be provided

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#parameter-sort-by)

sort\_by

string

`total_invites`/`total_traded`/`referee_volume` descending

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#response-data)

data

object

required

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-referral-code-info#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Account

useWalletConnector

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-wallet-connector#example)
    

Get wallet connection.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#usewalletconnector)
    

    export interface WalletConnectorContextState {
      connect: () => Promise<WalletState[]>;
      disconnect: (options: any) => Promise<any[]>;
      connecting: boolean;
      setChain: (options: { chainId: string | number }) => Promise<any>;
      chains: any[];
      wallet: any;
      connectedChain: ConnectedChain | null;
      settingChain: boolean;
    }
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-wallet-connector#example)

Example

    const wallet = useWalletConnector();
    
    return (
      <button
        onClick={() => {
          wallet.connect();
        }}
      >
        Connect wallet
      </button>
    );
    

[useAccountInstance](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-account-instance)

      "new_referral_code": "<string>"
    }

https://api.orderly.orghttps://testnet-api.orderly.org

v1

/

    {
      "current_referral_code": "<string>",
      "new_referral_code": "<string>"
    }
    '

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989
    }

#### Headers

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#parameter-orderly-timestamp)

orderly-timestamp

string

required

Timestamp of the signed request in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#parameter-orderly-account-id)

orderly-account-id

string

required

Account ID of the authenticated account.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#parameter-orderly-key)

orderly-key

string

required

Public orderly key used to sign the request.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#parameter-orderly-signature)

orderly-signature

string

required

Signature of the request payload generated with the orderly key.

#### Body

application/json

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#body-current-referral-code)

current\_referral\_code

string

required

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#body-new-referral-code)

new\_referral\_code

string

required

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/edit-referral-code#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

# useSettleSubscription - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-settle-subscription#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Account

useSettleSubscription

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Example](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-settle-subscription#example)
    

Subscribe to [PnL settlements](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/websocket-api/private/pnl-settlement)
 for current account via Websockets.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#usesettlesubscription)
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-settle-subscription#example)

Example

    useSettleSubscription({
      onMessage: (data: any) => {
        const { status } = data;
    
        switch (status) {
          case "COMPLETED":
            // settlement completed
            break;
          case "FAILED":
            // settlement failed
            break;
          default:
            break;
        }
      }
    });
    

[useMarginRatio](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-margin-ratio)
[useDaily](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/account/use-daily)

⌘I

# useWS - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-ws#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

API requests

useWS

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

On this page

*   [Subscribing to private topics](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-ws#subscribing-to-private-topics)
    

Allows you to subscribe to public topics.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#usews)
    

    subscribe(
      topic: string | { [key:string]:any },
      callback: WSMessageHandler | Omit<WSMessageHandler, "onUnsubscribe">,
      once?: boolean,
      id?: string
    ): unsubscribe | undefined
    

Subscribing to the `trade` topic:

    const ws = useWS();
    
    useEffect(() => {
      const unsubscribe = ws.subscribe(
        {
          id: `${symbol}@trade`,
          event: "subscribe",
          topic: `${symbol}@trade`,
          ts: Date.now()
        },
        {
          onMessage: (data: any) => {
            //
          }
        }
      );
      () => {
        // Unsubscribes when the component unloads
        unsubscribe();
      };
    }, []);
    

### 

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-ws#subscribing-to-private-topics)

Subscribing to private topics

Subscribing to the `executionreport` topic:

    const ws = useWS();
    
    useEffect(() => {
      const unsubscript = ws.privateSubscribe(
        {
          id: "executionreport",
          event: "subscribe",
          topic: "executionreport",
          ts: Date.now()
        },
        {
          onMessage: (data) => {
            // do something
          }
        }
      );
      () => {
        // Unsubscribes when the component unloads
        unsubscribe();
      };
    }, []);
    

[useMutation](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-mutation)
[useWsStatus](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-ws-status)

⌘I

Get asset history

cURL

      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>'

200

          "current_page": 1
        },

            "updated_time": 1688699193096,\
            "chain_id": "986532"\
