#### Query Parameters

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-token)

token

string

Filter results to a specific token symbol.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-side)

side

string

Filter by transfer direction: `DEPOSIT` or `WITHDRAW`.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-status)

status

string

Filter by transfer status. Supported values: `NEW`, `CONFIRM`, `PROCESSING`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `PENDING_REBALANCE`.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-startt)

start\_t

number

Filter results created at or after this timestamp in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-endt)

end\_t

number

Filter results created at or before this timestamp in milliseconds.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-page)

page

number

Page number to return. Starts from 1.

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#parameter-size)

size

number

Number of records to return per page.

#### Response

200 - application/json

OK

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#response-success)

success

boolean

required

Indicates whether the request was successful.

Example:

`true`

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#response-data)

data

object

required

Show child attributes

[​](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/restful-api/private/get-asset-history#response-timestamp)

timestamp

integer

Server timestamp in milliseconds.

Example:

`1702989203989`

# useQuery - Orderly

[Skip to main content](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-query#content-area)

[Orderly home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=6d10f536662ee9dc140acd8ce6642ab8)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/orderly/OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=OzqpKdtQuSFdqT6W&q=85&s=9717bbcd5a10f00b1c74d21c487c84a8)](https://orderly.network/docs)

latest

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

API requests

useQuery

[Home](https://orderly.network/docs/home)
[Introduction](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/getting-started/what-is-orderly)
[Build on Omnichain](https://orderly.network/docs/build-on-omnichain/building-on-omnichain)
[SDKs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/overview)
[Strategy vault](https://orderly.network/docs/introduction/orderly-omniVault/overview)
[Troubleshooting](https://orderly.network/docs/troubleshooting/introduction)
[Release notes](https://orderly.network/docs/release-notes/omnichain)

Use `useQuery` to access any public API, for example to get all available symbols on Orderly.

*   [Tech docs](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/tech-doc/modules/orderly_network_hooks#usequery)
    

    const { data, error, isLoading } = useQuery<API.Symbol[]>("/v1/public/info");
    
    return (
      <pre className="text-sm">
        {isLoading && <div>Loading...</div>}
        {data && <div className="text-slate-500">{JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}</div>}
      </pre>
    );
    

The `data` returned by `useQuery` is already serialized in JSON format. If you want to access the raw data, you can provide a `formatter` to override the default serialization of the hook. For example:

    const { data, error } = useQuery("/v1/public/info", {
      formatter: (data) => data
    });
    

[Low-level API](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/api)
[usePrivateQuery](https://orderly.network/docs/sdks/hooks/api/use-private-query)

⌘I

Get transfer nonce

cURL

    curl --request GET \
      --url https://api.orderly.org/v1/transfer_nonce \
      --header 'orderly-account-id: <orderly-account-id>' \
      --header 'orderly-key: <orderly-key>' \
      --header 'orderly-signature: <orderly-signature>' \
      --header 'orderly-timestamp: <orderly-timestamp>'

200

    {
      "success": true,
      "timestamp": 1702989203989,
      "data": {
        "transfer_nonce": 1
      }
    }
